blocks.html_en:
In many historical societies, religious prohibitions on usury meant the charging of interest of any kind.

Jump in a time machine to 1515 and ask Martin Luther, or to 1260 and ask Thomas Aquinas, they'd tell you it's sinful.

And in the present age, a fair number of Islamic folk consider interest against their religion's rules. So there's a Halal finance industry where, for example, you can get a "murabahah contract" where the bank buys a house, then sells the house to you at a higher price, while allowing you to pay them in monthly instalments.

sentences.en: In many historical societies, religious prohibitions on usury meant the charging of interest of any kind.Jump in a time machine to 1515 and ask Martin Luther, or to 1260 and ask Thomas Aquinas, they'd tell you it's sinful.And in the present age, a fair number of Islamic folk consider interest against their religion's rules.
sentences.zh: 在许多历史社会中，宗教禁止高利贷意味着收取任何形式的利息。乘坐时光机到 1515 年问马丁·路德，或者到 1260 年问托马斯·阿奎那，他们会告诉你这是有罪的。在当今时代，相当多的伊斯兰民众认为利息违反了他们的宗教规则。

sentences.en: So there's a Halal finance industry where, for example, you can get a "murabahah contract" where the bank buys a house, then sells the house to you at a higher price, while allowing you to pay them in monthly instalments.
sentences.zh: 因此，存在清真金融行业，例如，您可以获得“murabahah 合同”，银行购买房屋，然后以更高的价格将房屋卖给您，同时允许您按月分期付款。

Older than any of those:

"Thou shalt not lend upon interest to thy brother: interest of money, interest of victuals, interest of any thing that is lent upon interest" (Deut 23.20 JPS Tanakh).

blocks.html_en: I love when religions have rule lawyers like this. It readily discredits the religion. As if their all powerful god can be fooled by fancy paperwork or legal loopholes.

sentences.en: I love when religions have rule lawyers like this.
sentences.zh: 我喜欢宗教有这样的规则律师。

sentences.en: It readily discredits the religion.
sentences.zh: 它很容易抹黑宗教。

sentences.en: As if their all powerful god can be fooled by fancy paperwork or legal loopholes.
sentences.zh: 好像他们全能的上帝会被花哨的文书或法律漏洞所愚弄。

blocks.html_en:
The bit that isn't rules-lawyered away is that the risk is shared. For the deal to be compliant with the religious law, the lender must accept the same risk as the borrower, equally.

So I guess in this case if the house burns down and the insurance only pays 50% of the agreed value then the lender only receives 50% of their agreed repayment.

sentences.en: The bit that isn't rules-lawyered away is that the risk is shared.
sentences.zh: 规则中没有规定的一点是风险是共担的。

sentences.en: For the deal to be compliant with the religious law, the lender must accept the same risk as the borrower, equally.So I guess in this case if the house burns down and the insurance only pays 50% of the agreed value then the lender only receives 50% of their agreed repayment.
sentences.zh: 为了使交易符合宗教法，贷款人必须接受与借款人相同的风险，平等。所以我猜在这种情况下，如果房子被烧毁，保险只支付约定价值的 50%，那么贷款人只能收到约定还款的 50%。

blocks.html_en: Usually an interest-paying loan is backed by a guarantee, so the lender can pursue the borrower for repayment by claims on other assets.

sentences.zh: 通常，付息贷款有担保作为担保，因此贷款人可以通过对其他资产的债权来要求借款人偿还。

blocks.html_en: They’re not trying to fool God, they’re trying to fool you into going along with it. They don’t care what God thinks and may not even believe in Him at all, but unless they can convince you of the loophole they’re stuck with the rules themselves.

sentences.en: They’re not trying to fool God, they’re trying to fool you into going along with it.
sentences.zh: 他们不是想愚弄上帝，而是想愚弄你，让你同意。

sentences.en: They don’t care what God thinks and may not even believe in Him at all, but unless they can convince you of the loophole they’re stuck with the rules themselves.
sentences.zh: 他们不在乎上帝怎么想，甚至可能根本不相信他，但除非他们能让你相信这个漏洞，否则他们就会遵守规则本身。

blocks.html_en: It's concretely different. If the house becomes worthless, the "borrower" can walk away from the contract, owe nothing, and the bank keeps the house. The bank had better consider the value of the house, not just the ability of the "borrowed" to pay, when issuing this contract.

sentences.en: It's concretely different.
sentences.zh: 具体来说是不同的。

sentences.en: If the house becomes worthless, the "borrower" can walk away from the contract, owe nothing, and the bank keeps the house.
sentences.zh: 如果房子变得一文不值，“借款人”可以解除合同，不欠任何东西，而银行则保留房子。

sentences.en: The bank had better consider the value of the house, not just the ability of the "borrowed" to pay, when issuing this contract.
sentences.zh: 银行在签订这份合同时最好考虑房子的价值，而不仅仅是“借来的”人的支付能力。

sentences.zh: 如果房子变得一文不值，“借款人”可以解除合同，不欠任何东西，而银行则保留房子。

sentences.en: The bank had better consider the value of the house, not just the ability of the "borrowed" to pay, when issuing this contract.
sentences.zh: 银行在签订这份合同时最好考虑房子的价值，而不仅仅是“借来的”人的支付能力。

blocks.html_en:
It’s an interesting thought. The growth is so extreme that if the S&P 500 fell 50% today it would reach levels last seen in 2022. Given that the timespan is so short, I’m honestly not sure it would be as bad for 401ks as people expect unless all of your investment was concentrated in the last 4 years.

I suppose it’s worse if your calculation is, “I’ll retire when my 401k hits $X absolute value,” but I think most people just retire at a certain age instead with risk spread across decades.

sentences.en: It’s an interesting thought.
sentences.zh: 这是一个有趣的想法。

sentences.en: The growth is so extreme that if the S&P 500 fell 50% today it would reach levels last seen in 2022.
sentences.zh: 这种增长是如此极端，如果标准普尔 500 指数今天下跌 50%，它将达到 2022 年最后一次出现的水平。

sentences.zh: 鉴于时间跨度如此之短，老实说，我不确定 401k 会像人们预期的那样糟糕，除非你所有的投资都集中在过去 4 年。我认为如果你的计算是“当我的 401k 达到 X 美元绝对值时我就会退休”，情况会更糟，但我认为大多数人只是在某个年龄退休，而不是把风险分散到几十年。

blocks.html_en: One of the big issues with this is sequence of returns risk. If you retire and rely on your portfolio but the market dives for a year or two right after you leave the workforce, your total portfolio value is screwed because you were selling at a low point.

sentences.en: One of the big issues with this is sequence of returns risk.
sentences.zh: 其中一个大问题是回报风险的顺序。

sentences.en: If you retire and rely on your portfolio but the market dives for a year or two right after you leave the workforce, your total portfolio value is screwed because you were selling at a low point.
sentences.zh: 如果你退休并依赖你的投资组合，但在你离开劳动力市场后市场暴跌一两年，你的投资组合总价值就会因为你在低点出售而受到打击。

blocks.html_en:
Tangentially: I think a lot of people forget/underestimate the degree to which the industries behind their job are ones that they need to diversify away-from.

In other words, a programmer should invest a bit more away from software than average, a realtor should invest a bit more away from properties than average, a coal-miner should invest a bit more away from energy and mining, etc.

If you have your job, you can weather a stock-downturn, and if investments are solid, you can weather a period of unemployment by liquidating some, but if both hit trouble simultaneously then that's much much worse.

sentences.en: Tangentially: I think a lot of people forget/underestimate the degree to which the industries behind their job are ones that they need to diversify away-from.In other words, a programmer should invest a bit more away from software than average, a realtor should invest a bit more away from properties than average, a coal-miner should invest a bit more away from energy and mining, etc.If you have your job, you can weather a stock-downturn, and if investments are solid, you can weather a period of unemployment by liquidating some, but if both hit trouble simultaneously then that's much much worse.
sentences.zh: 切线：我认为很多人忘记/低估了他们工作背后的行业需要多元化的程度。换句话说，程序员应该比平均水平多投资一点软件，房地产经纪人应该比平均水平多投资一点房地产，煤矿工人应该多投资能源和采矿业，等等。如果你有工作，你就可以经受住股票低迷的考验，如果投资稳定，你可以通过清算来度过一段失业时期。有一些，但如果两者同时遇到麻烦，那就更糟糕了。

blocks.html_en: That makes sense, and it probably should be said more. It's probably just because we invest in what we know. If you're a real estate broker, you have an interest in properties, you think about it all the time, so you'll buy your own properties, or invest perhaps in builder stocks. If you're in tech, well, I don't need to say it because that's most of us. If you work in the energy sector, I imagine you know a thing or two about transport and esoterica of speculative miners or drillers, etc.

> It's probably just because we invest in what we know.

I'm mostly thinking of folks that will passively invest in a big broad index fund, and then assume they've reached the end in terms of balancing industry/sector risk.

blocks.html_en:
My go-to example is always Enron, and the impact on employees whose retirement funds were in their own too-awesome-to-fail employer.

Oh, sure, it's way worse because of the fraud-angle, but even if it had just been a more honest kind of mania, the arrangement was reckless and bad.

blocks.html_en:
Last week I was at the bank in my hometown, a small rural community. The teller took a phone call, and I overheard her say "You have $1.53 in your checking account, and $150 in savings".

Presumably this is their total net worth. I think this is way more common than people on this type of forum realize. Most will work until they literally can't anymore, then scrape by on social security until they die. I think it's important to keep that perspective.

sentences.en: Last week I was at the bank in my hometown, a small rural community.
sentences.zh: 上周我在家乡的一个乡村小社区的银行。

sentences.en: The teller took a phone call, and I overheard her say "You have $1.53 in your checking account, and $150 in savings".Presumably this is their total net worth.
sentences.zh: 出纳员接了电话，我无意中听到她说“你的支票账户里有 1.53 美元，储蓄有 150 美元”。大概这就是他们的总净资产。

sentences.en: I think this is way more common than people on this type of forum realize.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种情况比此类论坛上的人们意识到的要普遍得多。

sentences.en: Most will work until they literally can't anymore, then scrape by on social security until they die.
sentences.zh: 大多数人都会工作，直到他们真的无法工作为止，然后靠社会保障勉强度日，直到去世。

sentences.en: I think it's important to keep that perspective.
sentences.zh: 我认为保持这种观点很重要。

It is either that, or they have tons of debt. (Sometimes both!)

The average person is struggling in modern America.

sentences.zh: （有时两者兼而有之！） 在现代美国，普通人都在挣扎。

blocks.html_en:
Because the average person also makes a litany of poor financial decisions. $100K student loan balances for an state school arts degree, forgoing health insurance but expecting to receive $200K in care for free, or buying that $80K F150 on a 12.5% loan and rolling in negative equity.

The US is second in the world for median equivalised household disposable income, second only to Luxembourg and 10%+ above Norway. For daily median per person income after taxes and transfers, we're only behind Norway, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Qatar, and the UAE. Outside of petrostates, microstates, and Switzerland, no country has richer "average" people.

The US certainly doesn't have the safety net of some of these other states, but these aren't holes you're being thrown into by society: they're pits you've deliberately jumped into in 99% of cases.

sentences.en: Because the average person also makes a litany of poor financial decisions. $100K student loan balances for an state school arts degree, forgoing health insurance but expecting to receive $200K in care for free, or buying that $80K F150 on a 12.5% loan and rolling in negative equity.The US is second in the world for median equivalised household disposable income, second only to Luxembourg and 10%+ above Norway.
sentences.zh: 因为普通人也会做出一连串糟糕的财务决策。公立学校艺术学位的学生贷款余额为 10 万美元，放弃健康保险，但希望获得 20 万美元的免费护理，或者以 12.5% 的贷款购买 8 万美元的 F150，并滚动负资产。 美国的家庭可支配收入中位数在世界上排名第二，仅次于卢森堡，比挪威高出 10% 以上。

sentences.en: For daily median per person income after taxes and transfers, we're only behind Norway, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Qatar, and the UAE.
sentences.zh: 对于税后和转移支付后的人均每日收入中位数，我们仅落后于挪威、瑞士、卢森堡、卡塔尔和阿联酋。

sentences.en: Outside of petrostates, microstates, and Switzerland, no country has richer "average" people.The US certainly doesn't have the safety net of some of these other states, but these aren't holes you're being thrown into by society: they're pits you've deliberately jumped into in 99% of cases.
sentences.zh: 除了石油国家、微型国家和瑞士之外，没有哪个国家拥有比其他国家更富裕的“普通”人口。美国当然没有其他一些国家的安全网，但这些并不是你被社会扔进的洞：它们是你在 99% 的情况下故意跳进去的坑。

blocks.html_en:
Your error here, or the missing piece if we're generous, is what people are spending money on. Health care is astronomically more expensive here. Schooling isn't free after high school. Day care isn't free. Hell, even property taxes are simply 'not a thing' in France or the UK, where the taxes and Council Tax, respectively, are a tiny fraction of what Americans pay in property taxes ---- which, of course, pay for the 'free public schools.'

Now, let's talk about insurance, that's also much higher. In states like California and Florida, home insurance is through the roof, in some states like NJ and NY, car insurance is through the roof. Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).

You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.

That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about. The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.

sentences.en: Your error here, or the missing piece if we're generous, is what people are spending money on.
sentences.zh: 你在这里的错误，或者如果我们慷慨的话，缺失的部分就是人们花钱的地方。

sentences.en: Health care is astronomically more expensive here.
sentences.zh: 这里的医疗保健费用要高得多。

sentences.en: Schooling isn't free after high school.
sentences.zh: 高中毕业后，学校教育并不是免费的。

sentences.en: Day care isn't free.
sentences.zh: 日托不是免费的。

sentences.en: Hell, even property taxes are simply 'not a thing' in France or the UK, where the taxes and Council Tax, respectively, are a tiny fraction of what Americans pay in property taxes ---- which, of course, pay for the 'free public schools.'Now, let's talk about insurance, that's also much higher.
sentences.zh: 天哪，在法国或英国，即使是财产税也“不算什么”，那里的税收和市政税分别只占美国人支付的财产税的一小部分——当然，财产税是为“免费公立学校”支付的。现在，让我们谈谈保险，这也高得多。

sentences.en: In states like California and Florida, home insurance is through the roof, in some states like NJ and NY, car insurance is through the roof.
sentences.zh: 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。

sentences.en: Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about.
sentences.zh: 两者都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。你可能会反驳说，这些欧洲国家（以及类似的国家，如德国、比荷卢经济联盟等）的能源成本要高得多，购买力可能更高，但工资却低得多。也就是说，人们乱花钱的比喻需要考虑世界各地许多人永远不需要考虑的事情的惊人成本。

sentences.en: The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.
sentences.zh: 法国低劣的工资被如此之多的生活必需品所掩盖，而这些生活必需品无需高成本或在某些情况下不需要任何成本。

sentences.zh: 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。

sentences.en: Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).
sentences.zh: 两者的涨幅都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。

blocks.html_en: You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.

sentences.en: You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.
sentences.zh: 您可能会反驳说，这些欧洲国家（以及德国、比荷卢经济联盟等类似国家）的能源成本要高得多，购买力可能更高，但工资却低得多。

blocks.html_en: That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about. The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.

sentences.en: That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，这种人们乱花钱的比喻需要考虑世界上许多人从来不需要考虑的事情的令人震惊的成本。

sentences.en: The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.
sentences.zh: 法国低劣的工资被如此之多的生活必需品所掩盖，而这些生活必需品无需高成本或在某些情况下不需要任何成本。

blocks.html_en:
>Your error here, or the missing piece if we're generous, is what people are spending money on. Health care is astronomically more expensive here. Schooling isn't free after high school. Day care isn't free. Hell, even property taxes are simply 'not a thing' in France or the UK, where the taxes and Council Tax, respectively, are a tiny fraction of what Americans pay in property taxes ---- which, of course, pay for the 'free public schools.'

A US worker at the average wage keeps roughly 70 cents of every labor-cost dollar; a worker in Belgium, Germany, France, Austria, or Italy keeps closer to 47 cents, even before you add in the effect of VAT. Nobody in Europe is getting those services you mentioned for "free" - you're just making everyone else pay for them with taxing their labor. You almost connected the dots when it came to property taxes paying for public services, but missed that Europe assesses income taxes.

>Now, let's talk about insurance, that's also much higher. In states like California and Florida, home insurance is through the roof, in some states like NJ and NY, car insurance is through the roof. Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).

This is a bundle of issues. As a quick list, compare the size/value of an average property in California or Florida to a property in Europe, assuming they even own the property (remember, Europe's home ownership rate is lower than Florida's). Same goes for car ownership costs: American cars are larger, more expensive, driven more, and are more exposed to damages from uninsured motorists, because states like NY, NJ, and CA think it's racist to enforce uninsured (or even unlicensed) motorist laws. Just like health insurance, allowing free-riders on insurance systems is financially disastrous.

>You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.

I'm not sure what you're trying to say here. Yes, Europeans can get a number of services paid for by their neighbor, but it doesn't make them "richer" by any reasonable measure. Quantifying standard of living is incredibly difficult, because even as this exchange shows, people will value different things differently. But broadly speaking, my original point still stands: Americans should not be struggling to live in America, absent poor personal decisions, particularly if you're willing to lower the standard of living to that of an average European (a smaller rented property, driving far fewer miles in a compact car, no air conditioning, etc, etc).

>That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about. The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.

What outrageous costs are those? Community college remains very affordable, and costs for 2 years at a state school can be managed, especially against the greater lifetime earning potential in America. Healthcare costs OOP is capped at $9,200 on an ACA plan, which can be nearly free for middle to lower income brackets, and that debt itself is basically unenforceable in most cases these days, assuming you truly don't have the assets to pay.

sentences.en: >Your error here, or the missing piece if we're generous, is what people are spending money on.
sentences.zh: >你在这里的错误，或者如果我们慷慨的话，缺失的部分就是人们花钱的地方。

sentences.en: Health care is astronomically more expensive here.
sentences.zh: 这里的医疗保健费用要高得多。

sentences.en: Schooling isn't free after high school.
sentences.zh: 高中毕业后，学校教育并不是免费的。

sentences.en: Day care isn't free.
sentences.zh: 日托不是免费的。

sentences.en: Hell, even property taxes are simply 'not a thing' in France or the UK, where the taxes and Council Tax, respectively, are a tiny fraction of what Americans pay in property taxes ---- which, of course, pay for the 'free public schools.'A US worker at the average wage keeps roughly 70 cents of every labor-cost dollar; a worker in Belgium, Germany, France, Austria, or Italy keeps closer to 47 cents, even before you add in the effect of VAT.
sentences.zh: 天哪，在法国或英国，甚至连财产税也根本“不算什么”，那里的税收和市政税分别只占美国人缴纳的财产税的一小部分——当然，财产税是为“免费公立学校”支付费用的。美国工人的平均工资大约相当于每美元劳动力成本的 70 美分；即使不考虑增值税，比利时、德国、法国、奥地利或意大利的工人的工资也接近 47 美分。

sentences.en: Nobody in Europe is getting those services you mentioned for "free" - you're just making everyone else pay for them with taxing their labor.
sentences.zh: 欧洲没有人“免费”获得你提到的这些服务——你只是让其他人通过对他们的劳动力征税来支付这些服务。

sentences.en: You almost connected the dots when it came to property taxes paying for public services, but missed that Europe assesses income taxes.>Now, let's talk about insurance, that's also much higher.
sentences.zh: 当谈到支付公共服务的财产税时，您几乎将这些点联系起来，但错过了欧洲评估所得税。>现在，让我们谈谈保险，这也高得多。

sentences.en: In states like California and Florida, home insurance is through the roof, in some states like NJ and NY, car insurance is through the roof.
sentences.zh: 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。

sentences.en: Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).This is a bundle of issues.
sentences.zh: 两者的涨幅都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。这是一系列问题。

sentences.en: As a quick list, compare the size/value of an average property in California or Florida to a property in Europe, assuming they even own the property (remember, Europe's home ownership rate is lower than Florida's).
sentences.zh: 作为一个快速列表，将加利福尼亚州或佛罗里达州的平均房产的大小/价值与欧洲的房产进行比较，假设他们甚至拥有该房产（请记住，欧洲的房屋拥有率低于佛罗里达州）。

sentences.en: Same goes for car ownership costs: American cars are larger, more expensive, driven more, and are more exposed to damages from uninsured motorists, because states like NY, NJ, and CA think it's racist to enforce uninsured (or even unlicensed) motorist laws.
sentences.zh: 汽车拥有成本也是如此：美国汽车更大、更贵、驾驶次数更多，并且更容易受到未投保驾驶者的损害，因为纽约州、新泽西州和加利福尼亚州等州认为执行未投保（甚至无照）驾驶者法律是种族主义。

sentences.en: Just like health insurance, allowing free-riders on insurance systems is financially disastrous.>You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.I'm not sure what you're trying to say here.
sentences.zh: 就像健康保险一样，允许保险系统搭便车在经济上是灾难性的。>你可能会反驳说，这些欧洲国家（以及德国、比荷卢经济联盟等类似国家）的能源成本要高得多，购买力可能更高，但工资却低得多。我不确定你在这里想说什么。

sentences.en: Yes, Europeans can get a number of services paid for by their neighbor, but it doesn't make them "richer" by any reasonable measure.
sentences.zh: 是的，欧洲人可以获得邻国付费的许多服务，但这并不会让他们以任何合理的标准衡量“更富有”。

sentences.en: Quantifying standard of living is incredibly difficult, because even as this exchange shows, people will value different things differently.
sentences.zh: 量化生活水平非常困难，因为即使正如这次交流所表明的那样，人们对不同事物的评价也不同。

sentences.en: But broadly speaking, my original point still stands: Americans should not be struggling to live in America, absent poor personal decisions, particularly if you're willing to lower the standard of living to that of an average European (a smaller rented property, driving far fewer miles in a compact car, no air conditioning, etc, etc).>That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about.
sentences.zh: 但从广义上讲，我最初的观点仍然成立：美国人不应该在没有糟糕的个人决定的情况下在美国艰难地生活，特别是如果你愿意将生活水平降低到欧洲人的平均水平（租用的房产较小，驾驶紧凑型汽车的行驶里程要少得多，没有空调等）。>也就是说，人们乱花钱的比喻需要考虑世界各地许多人永远不需要考虑的事情的令人震惊的成本。

sentences.en: The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.What outrageous costs are those?
sentences.zh: 法国低劣的工资被如此之多的生活必需品所掩盖，而无需高成本或在某些情况下无需任何成本。这些成本是什么？

sentences.en: Community college remains very affordable, and costs for 2 years at a state school can be managed, especially against the greater lifetime earning potential in America.
sentences.zh: 社区大学仍然非常负担得起，在公立学校学习两年的费用是可以控制的，特别是考虑到美国更大的终生收入潜力。

sentences.en: Healthcare costs OOP is capped at $9,200 on an ACA plan, which can be nearly free for middle to lower income brackets, and that debt itself is basically unenforceable in most cases these days, assuming you truly don't have the assets to pay.
sentences.zh: ACA 计划的医疗费用 OOP 上限为 9,200 美元，该计划对于中低收入阶层来说几乎是免费的，而且如今在大多数情况下，债务本身基本上是无法执行的，假设你确实没有资产来支付。

sentences.zh: 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。

sentences.en: Both going up way above inflation (like the items in my first paragraph).
sentences.zh: 两者的涨幅都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。

blocks.html_en: This is a bundle of issues. As a quick list, compare the size/value of an average property in California or Florida to a property in Europe, assuming they even own the property (remember, Europe's home ownership rate is lower than Florida's). Same goes for car ownership costs: American cars are larger, more expensive, driven more, and are more exposed to damages from uninsured motorists, because states like NY, NJ, and CA think it's racist to enforce uninsured (or even unlicensed) motorist laws. Just like health insurance, allowing free-riders on insurance systems is financially disastrous.

sentences.en: This is a bundle of issues.
sentences.zh: 这是一系列问题。

sentences.en: As a quick list, compare the size/value of an average property in California or Florida to a property in Europe, assuming they even own the property (remember, Europe's home ownership rate is lower than Florida's).
sentences.zh: 作为一个快速列表，将加利福尼亚州或佛罗里达州的平均房产的大小/价值与欧洲的房产进行比较，假设他们甚至拥有该房产（请记住，欧洲的房屋拥有率低于佛罗里达州）。

sentences.en: Same goes for car ownership costs: American cars are larger, more expensive, driven more, and are more exposed to damages from uninsured motorists, because states like NY, NJ, and CA think it's racist to enforce uninsured (or even unlicensed) motorist laws.
sentences.zh: 汽车拥有成本也是如此：美国汽车更大、更贵、驾驶次数更多，并且更容易受到未投保驾驶者的损害，因为纽约州、新泽西州和加利福尼亚州等州认为执行未投保（甚至无照）驾驶者法律是种族主义。

sentences.en: Just like health insurance, allowing free-riders on insurance systems is financially disastrous.
sentences.zh: 就像健康保险一样，允许保险系统搭便车会给经济带来灾难性的后果。

blocks.html_en: >You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.

sentences.en: >You might counter with energy costs are much higher in these European countries (and similar ones like Germany, Benelux, etc), and the purchasing power might be higher, but the wages are so so much lower.
sentences.zh: >您可能会反驳说，这些欧洲国家（以及德国、比荷卢经济联盟等类似国家）的能源成本要高得多，购买力可能更高，但工资却低得多。

blocks.html_en: I'm not sure what you're trying to say here. Yes, Europeans can get a number of services paid for by their neighbor, but it doesn't make them "richer" by any reasonable measure. Quantifying standard of living is incredibly difficult, because even as this exchange shows, people will value different things differently. But broadly speaking, my original point still stands: Americans should not be struggling to live in America, absent poor personal decisions, particularly if you're willing to lower the standard of living to that of an average European (a smaller rented property, driving far fewer miles in a compact car, no air conditioning, etc, etc).

sentences.en: I'm not sure what you're trying to say here.
sentences.zh: 我不确定你在这里想说什么。

sentences.en: Yes, Europeans can get a number of services paid for by their neighbor, but it doesn't make them "richer" by any reasonable measure.
sentences.zh: 是的，欧洲人可以获得邻国付费的许多服务，但这并不会让他们以任何合理的标准衡量“更富有”。

sentences.en: Quantifying standard of living is incredibly difficult, because even as this exchange shows, people will value different things differently.
sentences.zh: 量化生活水平非常困难，因为即使正如这次交流所表明的那样，人们对不同事物的评价也不同。

sentences.en: But broadly speaking, my original point still stands: Americans should not be struggling to live in America, absent poor personal decisions, particularly if you're willing to lower the standard of living to that of an average European (a smaller rented property, driving far fewer miles in a compact car, no air conditioning, etc, etc).
sentences.zh: 但从广义上讲，我最初的观点仍然成立：美国人不应该在没有糟糕的个人决定的情况下在美国艰难地生活，特别是如果你愿意将生活水平降低到欧洲人的平均水平（租用的房产较小，驾驶紧凑型汽车的行驶里程要少得多，没有空调等）。

blocks.html_en: >That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about. The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.

sentences.en: >That said, this trope of people misspending their money needs to consider this outrageous costs of things that many people around the world never need to think about.
sentences.zh: > 也就是说，这种人们乱花钱的比喻需要考虑世界上许多人永远不需要考虑的事情的令人震惊的成本。

sentences.en: The shitty wages in France are overshadowed by so many essentials being available without a high cost or any cost in some cases.
sentences.zh: 法国低劣的工资被如此之多的生活必需品所掩盖，而这些生活必需品无需高成本或在某些情况下不需要任何成本。

blocks.html_en: What outrageous costs are those? Community college remains very affordable, and costs for 2 years at a state school can be managed, especially against the greater lifetime earning potential in America. Healthcare costs OOP is capped at $9,200 on an ACA plan, which can be nearly free for middle to lower income brackets, and that debt itself is basically unenforceable in most cases these days, assuming you truly don't have the assets to pay.

sentences.en: What outrageous costs are those?
sentences.zh: 那些费用是多么离谱？

sentences.en: Community college remains very affordable, and costs for 2 years at a state school can be managed, especially against the greater lifetime earning potential in America.
sentences.zh: 社区大学仍然非常负担得起，在公立学校学习两年的费用是可以控制的，特别是考虑到美国更大的终生收入潜力。

sentences.en: Healthcare costs OOP is capped at $9,200 on an ACA plan, which can be nearly free for middle to lower income brackets, and that debt itself is basically unenforceable in most cases these days, assuming you truly don't have the assets to pay.
sentences.zh: ACA 计划的医疗费用 OOP 上限为 9,200 美元，该计划对于中低收入阶层来说几乎是免费的，而且如今在大多数情况下，债务本身基本上是无法执行的，假设你确实没有资产来支付。

blocks.html_en:
401k reduces your taxable income when depositing money, this is more tax efficient than paying normal income taxes and then also paying capital gains.

401k lets you rebalance a portfolio with zero tax implications.

The downsides are generally high fees and a 10% penalty for early withdrawal which makes them surprisingly bad for young people. They tend to start in lower tax brackets, have fewer reserves when unemployed, and face fewer risks from an unbalanced portfolio.

Pay down debt then Roth IRA when young 401k after 40 is often better than defaulting to a 401k, but saving anything tends to be more important than such optimizations.

sentences.en: 401k reduces your taxable income when depositing money, this is more tax efficient than paying normal income taxes and then also paying capital gains.401k lets you rebalance a portfolio with zero tax implications.The downsides are generally high fees and a 10% penalty for early withdrawal which makes them surprisingly bad for young people.
sentences.zh: 401k 会在存钱时减少您的应税收入，这比先缴纳正常所得税，然后再缴纳资本利得的税收效率更高。401k 可以让您在零税收影响的情况下重新平衡投资组合。缺点是费用通常很高，而且提前取款会受到 10% 的罚款，这对年轻人来说非常不利。

sentences.en: They tend to start in lower tax brackets, have fewer reserves when unemployed, and face fewer risks from an unbalanced portfolio.Pay down debt then Roth IRA when young 401k after 40 is often better than defaulting to a 401k, but saving anything tends to be more important than such optimizations.
sentences.zh: 他们往往开始时的税级较低，失业时储备金较少，投资组合不平衡带来的风险也较小。 40 岁后年轻时先偿还债务，然后使用 Roth IRA 支付 401k，通常比拖欠 401k 更好，但储蓄任何东西往往比这种优化更重要。

blocks.html_en:
I am alone in my peer group for doing something like this.

Cars, student debt, credit card debt all gone. (And I dread needing a new car). Covered downpayment on my house and cash for a nice shed that matches the house and a fence so my kid can play in the back yard with no issue.

Invested low 5 figures into myself taking a year off and now I am getting serious about the 401k at 41. And I am ok with that.

I never worked at a big tech company and I covered my mom's down payment and appliances and new carpet and part of her move for her to move close to me. Dad died when I was 11 so I am all she has and she was a public school teacher so she's on a small pension.

We all walk a different life and I know people that make my entire life savings in a year but I will eventually grow a retirement to get me through 10-15 years and then it will be what it will be. (Maybe a tank of helium and bag)

sentences.en: I am alone in my peer group for doing something like this.Cars, student debt, credit card debt all gone.
sentences.zh: 在我的同龄人中，我是唯一一个这样做的人。汽车、学生债务、信用卡债务都消失了。

sentences.en: (And I dread needing a new car).
sentences.zh: （我害怕需要一辆新车）。

sentences.zh: 支付了我房子的首付和现金，购买了一个与房子和栅栏相匹配的漂亮棚子，这样我的孩子就可以毫无问题地在后院玩耍。为自己休息一年投资了低 5 位数，现在我正在认真对待 41 岁的 401k。

sentences.en: And I am ok with that.I never worked at a big tech company and I covered my mom's down payment and appliances and new carpet and part of her move for her to move close to me.
sentences.zh: 我对此很满意。我从未在大型科技公司工作过，我承担了我妈妈的首付、电器和新地毯以及她搬家的一部分，以便她搬到我身边。

sentences.en: Dad died when I was 11 so I am all she has and she was a public school teacher so she's on a small pension.We all walk a different life and I know people that make my entire life savings in a year but I will eventually grow a retirement to get me through 10-15 years and then it will be what it will be.
sentences.zh: 我11岁的时候爸爸去世了，所以我是她的一切，她是一名公立学校的老师，所以她只有很少的养老金。我们都过着不同的生活，我认识一些人，一年之内就能赚到我一生的积蓄，但我最终会退休，让我度过10-15年，然后就会是这样。

sentences.en: (Maybe a tank of helium and bag)
sentences.zh: （也许是氦气罐和袋子）

sentences.en: Covered downpayment on my house and cash for a nice shed that matches the house and a fence so my kid can play in the back yard with no issue.Invested low 5 figures into myself taking a year off and now I am getting serious about the 401k at 41.
sentences.zh: 支付了我房子的首付和现金，购买了一个与房子和栅栏相匹配的漂亮棚子，这样我的孩子就可以毫无问题地在后院玩耍。为自己休息一年投资了低 5 位数，现在我正在认真对待 41 岁的 401k。

sentences.en: And I am ok with that.I never worked at a big tech company and I covered my mom's down payment and appliances and new carpet and part of her move for her to move close to me.
sentences.zh: 我对此很满意。我从未在大型科技公司工作过，我承担了我妈妈的首付、电器和新地毯以及她搬家的一部分，以便她搬到我身边。

sentences.en: Dad died when I was 11 so I am all she has and she was a public school teacher so she's on a small pension.We all walk a different life and I know people that make my entire life savings in a year but I will eventually grow a retirement to get me through 10-15 years and then it will be what it will be.
sentences.zh: 我11岁的时候爸爸去世了，所以我是她的一切，她是一名公立学校的老师，所以她只有很少的养老金。我们都过着不同的生活，我认识一些人，一年之内就能赚到我一生的积蓄，但我最终会退休，让我度过10-15年，然后就会是这样。

blocks.html_en:
During the dot-com crisis. Nasdaq fell around 78% from its peak and S&P by around 49% so it isn't unprecedented (ironically has both aspects of being both tech and are within the same time-era)

It created an actual recession albeit thankfully short one for the case of dotcom (sadly not for 2007) and a really recessionary environment which causes unemployment and just straight up fear and panic.

I do understand what you are talking about and overall in long term, perhaps things flatten out but atleast speaking financially so, its better to be on a smooth sailing road rather than insane ups and downs with retirement money if preferable.

> I think most people just retire at a certain age instead with risk spread across decades.

The issue in my opinion is with people near that certain age you mention and who retire in the time during boom just before bust. They would then get the 50% hit on their savings instantly with an recession/inflation/unemployment environment which in my opinion might be genuinely devastating.

(supposing that they had their investments in stocks, I wouldn't consider that any retiree would have all their money in stocks but there have been some other comments which show a sizable amount, @kipchak's comment shows 50% stock for retirement. so a 50% shock on top of that could lead to a wipe out of 25% of your retirement fund which is honestly still pretty crazy.)

sentences.en: During the dot-com crisis.
sentences.zh: 在互联网危机期间。

sentences.en: Nasdaq fell around 78% from its peak and S&P by around 49% so it isn't unprecedented (ironically has both aspects of being both tech and are within the same time-era)It created an actual recession albeit thankfully short one for the case of dotcom (sadly not for 2007) and a really recessionary environment which causes unemployment and just straight up fear and panic.I do understand what you are talking about and overall in long term, perhaps things flatten out but atleast speaking financially so, its better to be on a smooth sailing road rather than insane ups and downs with retirement money if preferable.> I think most people just retire at a certain age instead with risk spread across decades.The issue in my opinion is with people near that certain age you mention and who retire in the time during boom just before bust.
sentences.zh: 纳斯达克指数较峰值下跌了约 78%，标准普尔指数下跌了约 49%，所以这并不是史无前例的（具有讽刺意味的是，它既是科技股，又处于同一时代），它造成了一场真正的衰退，尽管谢天谢地，对于网络泡沫来说，衰退是短暂的（遗憾的是 2007 年不是），而且是一个真正的衰退环境，导致失业和直接的恐惧和恐慌。我确实理解你在说什么，总体而言，从长远来看，也许情况会趋于平缓，但至少从财务上来说，最好是走上一帆风顺的道路，而不是在退休金上疯狂起起落落。>我认为大多数人只是在某个年龄退休，而不是风险分散在几十年内。我认为问题在于那些接近你提到的某个年龄的人，以及在萧条前的繁荣时期退休的人。

sentences.en: They would then get the 50% hit on their savings instantly with an recession/inflation/unemployment environment which in my opinion might be genuinely devastating.(supposing that they had their investments in stocks, I wouldn't consider that any retiree would have all their money in stocks but there have been some other comments which show a sizable amount, @kipchak's comment shows 50% stock for retirement.
sentences.zh: 然后，在经济衰退/通货膨胀/失业环境下，他们的储蓄将立即受到 50% 的打击，在我看来，这可能确实是毁灭性的。（假设他们投资于股票，我不认为任何退休人员会将所有资金都投资于股票，但也有其他一些评论显示了相当大的数额，@kipchak 的评论显示 50% 的股票用于退休。

sentences.en: so a 50% shock on top of that could lead to a wipe out of 25% of your retirement fund which is honestly still pretty crazy.)
sentences.zh: 因此，除此之外，50% 的冲击可能会导致您的退休基金损失 25%，老实说，这仍然相当疯狂。）

blocks.html_en:
This kind of metric is always used to shock and awe in pop media when talking about the GFC, but it's not how actual investment works. In practice most investments are DCAed.

No person puts all of their retirement savings into QQQ or SPY at the peak and sell it off at the trough. Instead folks drip their savings into their weighted portfolio and withdraw money from their weighted portfolio as expenses accrue. Now obviously the GFC was a huge deal, but this sort of facile understanding of stock markets always leads to big misunderstandings. There's a reason Monte Carlo analyses of these events are used to model these scenarios.

(Though I imagine there were many people who tried to re-balance their portfolio into a less equity-heavy model abruptly during the GFC and based on equities performance at the time, it was probably the right move as long as taxes were taken into account.)

sentences.en: This kind of metric is always used to shock and awe in pop media when talking about the GFC, but it's not how actual investment works.
sentences.zh: 当流行媒体谈论全球金融危机时，这种衡量标准总是被用来令人震惊和敬畏，但这并不是实际投资的运作方式。

sentences.en: In practice most investments are DCAed.No person puts all of their retirement savings into QQQ or SPY at the peak and sell it off at the trough.
sentences.zh: 实际上，大多数投资都是 DCA 的。没有人在高峰时将所有退休储蓄投入 QQQ 或 SPY，并在低谷时出售。

sentences.en: Instead folks drip their savings into their weighted portfolio and withdraw money from their weighted portfolio as expenses accrue.
sentences.zh: 相反，人们将储蓄投入到加权投资组合中，并随着费用的增加从加权投资组合中提取资金。

sentences.en: Now obviously the GFC was a huge deal, but this sort of facile understanding of stock markets always leads to big misunderstandings.
sentences.zh: 显然，全球金融危机是一件大事，但这种对股市的肤浅理解总是会导致很大的误解。

sentences.en: There's a reason Monte Carlo analyses of these events are used to model these scenarios.(Though I imagine there were many people who tried to re-balance their portfolio into a less equity-heavy model abruptly during the GFC and based on equities performance at the time, it was probably the right move as long as taxes were taken into account.)
sentences.zh: 使用对这些事件的蒙特卡罗分析来模拟这些情景是有原因的。（尽管我想，在全球金融危机期间，有很多人试图将他们的投资组合突然重新平衡为股票比重较低的模型，并且基于当时的股票表现，但只要考虑到税收，这可能是正确的举动。）

blocks.html_en:
This kind of metric is always used to shock and awe in pop media when talking about the GFC, but it's not how actual investment works. In practice most investments are DCAed.

No person puts all of their retirement savings into QQQ or SPY at the peak and sell it off at the trough. Instead folks drip their savings into their weighted portfolio and withdraw money from their weighted portfolio as expenses accrue. Now obviously the GFC was a huge deal, but this sort of facile understanding of stock markets always leads to big misunderstandings. There's a reason Monte Carlo analyses of these events are used to model these scenarios.

(Though I imagine there were many people who tried to re-balance their portfolio into a less equity-heavy model abruptly during the GFC and based on equities performance at the time, it was probably the right move as long as taxes were taken into account.)

sentences.en: This kind of metric is always used to shock and awe in pop media when talking about the GFC, but it's not how actual investment works.
sentences.zh: 当流行媒体谈论全球金融危机时，这种衡量标准总是被用来令人震惊和敬畏，但这并不是实际投资的运作方式。

sentences.en: In practice most investments are DCAed.No person puts all of their retirement savings into QQQ or SPY at the peak and sell it off at the trough.
sentences.zh: 实际上，大多数投资都是 DCA 的。没有人在高峰时将所有退休储蓄投入 QQQ 或 SPY，并在低谷时出售。

sentences.en: Instead folks drip their savings into their weighted portfolio and withdraw money from their weighted portfolio as expenses accrue.
sentences.zh: 相反，人们将储蓄投入到加权投资组合中，并随着费用的增加从加权投资组合中提取资金。

sentences.zh: 显然，全球金融危机是一件大事，但这种对股市的肤浅理解总是会导致很大的误解。

sentences.zh: 使用对这些事件的蒙特卡罗分析来模拟这些情景是有原因的。（尽管我想，在全球金融危机期间，有很多人试图将他们的投资组合突然重新平衡为股票比重较低的模型，并且基于当时的股票表现，但只要考虑到税收，这可能是正确的举动。）

blocks.html_en:
> (supposing that they had their investments in stocks, I wouldn't consider that any retiree would have all their money in stocks but there have been some other comments which show a sizable amount, @kipchak's comment shows 50% stock for retirement. so a 50% shock on top of that could lead to a wipe out of 25% of your retirement fund which is honestly still pretty crazy.)

What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis? "Bubble Insurance" if you will. Going 100% bonds is simply intolerable for the time window for most retirees, but an equity wipeout of this magnitude is equally intolerable.

blocks.html_en:
They are probably not going to be implemented using long put positions, but a product with a similar return profile to what you're looking for is a buffered ETF. Basically, over a defined period, you agree to a maximum possible downside in exchange for a capped upside. They are available in ETF form from a number of providers.

For example, you could have an S&P 500 fund that, over the next year, will have a maximum of 0% capital losses (it can't go down), you will only get the first, say, 5% of gains that the equity index makes. So if stocks go up 20% the next year, your return is capped at 5%, but if they crash 50%, you don't absorb any capital losses. In practice, the return cap is going to be just a bit above the corresponding Treasury bill for the same duration.

These can be constructed in various different ways and institutionally I'm sure there are more bespoke ways that are more efficient from a fees/returns and tax perspective, but one way to do this on your own without going the ETF route is:

- Pick an amount you'd like to invest. - Buy a Treasury bill for some duration. Treasury bills are discounted at the time of purchase and return the target amount when the bill matures. For instance, if you buy a $100k 1-year Treasury bill, it might cost $96.5k today. - Now you have $3.5k in your pocket and a guarantee that you'll get $100k in a year when the bill matures. Use that $3.5k now to purchase call options or vertical spreads on the S&P 500 index to capture the upside that you can. Your return is limited by the structure of that options trade and what its maximum payoff is.

If you're willing to accept more than 0% downside, then you can achieve a higher potential upside cap as well.

sentences.en: They are probably not going to be implemented using long put positions, but a product with a similar return profile to what you're looking for is a buffered ETF.
sentences.zh: 它们可能不会使用看跌期权多头头寸来实现，但与您正在寻找的回报率相似的产品是缓冲 ETF。

sentences.en: Basically, over a defined period, you agree to a maximum possible downside in exchange for a capped upside.
sentences.zh: 基本上，在规定的时间内，您同意最大可能的下跌，以换取有限的上涨。

sentences.en: They are available in ETF form from a number of providers.For example, you could have an S&P 500 fund that, over the next year, will have a maximum of 0% capital losses (it can't go down), you will only get the first, say, 5% of gains that the equity index makes.
sentences.zh: 许多提供商都提供 ETF 形式的基金。例如，您可能拥有一只 S&P 500 基金，在接下来的一年中，该基金的资本损失最多为 0%（不能下跌），您只能获得股票指数收益的第一笔收益，比如 5%。

sentences.en: So if stocks go up 20% the next year, your return is capped at 5%, but if they crash 50%, you don't absorb any capital losses.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果明年股票上涨 20%，您的回报率上限为 5%，但如果股票下跌 50%，您就不会吸收任何资本损失。

sentences.en: In practice, the return cap is going to be just a bit above the corresponding Treasury bill for the same duration.These can be constructed in various different ways and institutionally I'm sure there are more bespoke ways that are more efficient from a fees/returns and tax perspective, but one way to do this on your own without going the ETF route is:- Pick an amount you'd like to invest. - Buy a Treasury bill for some duration.
sentences.zh: 在实践中，回报上限将略高于相同期限内相应的国库券。这些可以通过各种不同的方式构建，从制度上讲，我确信从费用/回报和税收的角度来看，有更多定制的方式更有效，但不走 ETF 路线而自行完成此操作的一种方法是： - 选择您想要投资的金额。 - 购买一段期限的国库券。

sentences.en: Treasury bills are discounted at the time of purchase and return the target amount when the bill matures.
sentences.zh: 国库券在购买时贴现，并在票据到期时返还目标金额。

sentences.en: For instance, if you buy a $100k 1-year Treasury bill, it might cost $96.5k today. - Now you have $3.5k in your pocket and a guarantee that you'll get $100k in a year when the bill matures.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果您购买一张 10 万美元的一年期国库券，今天可能花费 9.65 万美元。 - 现在您的口袋里有 3,500 美元，并保证您将在一年内账单到期时获得 10 万美元。

sentences.en: Use that $3.5k now to purchase call options or vertical spreads on the S&P 500 index to capture the upside that you can.
sentences.zh: 现在用这 3,500 美元购买看涨期权或 S&P 500 指数的垂直价差，以抓住可能的上涨空间。

sentences.en: Your return is limited by the structure of that options trade and what its maximum payoff is.If you're willing to accept more than 0% downside, then you can achieve a higher potential upside cap as well.
sentences.zh: 您的回报受到期权交易结构及其最大收益的限制。如果您愿意接受超过 0% 的下跌，那么您也可以获得更高的潜在上涨上限。

sentences.zh: 例如，如果您购买一张 10 万美元的一年期国库券，今天可能花费 9.65 万美元。 - 现在您的口袋里有 3,500 美元，并保证您将在一年内账单到期时获得 10 万美元。

sentences.en: Use that $3.5k now to purchase call options or vertical spreads on the S&P 500 index to capture the upside that you can.
sentences.zh: 现在用这 3,500 美元购买看涨期权或 S&P 500 指数的垂直价差，以抓住可能的上涨空间。

sentences.en: Your return is limited by the structure of that options trade and what its maximum payoff is.
sentences.zh: 您的回报受到期权交易结构及其最大收益的限制。

blocks.html_en: If you're willing to accept more than 0% downside, then you can achieve a higher potential upside cap as well.

sentences.en: If you're willing to accept more than 0% downside, then you can achieve a higher potential upside cap as well.
sentences.zh: 如果您愿意接受超过 0% 的下跌，那么您也可以获得更高的潜在上涨上限。

blocks.html_en: Even someone close to retirement doesn't need to go 100% bonds. It's not like someone needs all their retirement money on day 1. The part that remains in equities will continue generating dividends that will get reinvested, and recover over time.

sentences.en: Even someone close to retirement doesn't need to go 100% bonds.
sentences.zh: 即使是接近退休的人也不需要 100% 购买债券。

sentences.en: It's not like someone needs all their retirement money on day 1.
sentences.zh: 这不像有人在第一天就需要所有的退休金。

sentences.en: The part that remains in equities will continue generating dividends that will get reinvested, and recover over time.
sentences.zh: 留在股票中的部分将继续产生股息，这些股息将被再投资，并随着时间的推移而恢复。

blocks.html_en:
The cost of insurance is carry e.g. when you buy an option you are paying for the convexity with time decay. It is quite expensive.

It is worth saying however that part of tail hedging is that the payoff is worth a lot more when everything else has tanked, so e.g. even if you (say) get 10% on your puts when the wider portfolio is still down 40% (made up numbers), you can deploy that capital at probably quite a high expected return.

sentences.en: The cost of insurance is carry e.g. when you buy an option you are paying for the convexity with time decay.
sentences.zh: 保险费用由个人承担，例如当您购买期权时，您正在为随时间衰减的凸度付出代价。

sentences.en: It is quite expensive.It is worth saying however that part of tail hedging is that the payoff is worth a lot more when everything else has tanked, so e.g. even if you (say) get 10% on your puts when the wider portfolio is still down 40% (made up numbers), you can deploy that capital at probably quite a high expected return.
sentences.zh: 这是相当昂贵的。然而，值得一提的是，尾部对冲的一部分是，当其他一切都陷入困境时，回报会更有价值，所以例如即使你（比如说）在更广泛的投资组合仍下跌 40%（编造的数字）时获得了 10% 的看跌期权，你也可以以相当高的预期回报来部署该资本。

It would cost an extreme amount. The only reason to do something like that would be to defer capital gains into retirement while protecting your position.

You can use a collar for this at somewhat reasonable cost. Not sure how rolling that would compare to just using it to defer until you can cheaply sell and buy some fixed income ladder. Probably badly.

Also, there’s no capital gains to defer if you use a retirement account, which will be a better place for fixed income anyway.

blocks.html_en:
> What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis? "Bubble Insurance" if you will.

I expect that would not be a cost-effective way of attaining the risk profile you'd be looking for.

I expect there won't be a more cost-effective way of managing your portfolio risk than by simply adjusting your split of broadly-diversified equities vs bonds.

sentences.en: > What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis?
sentences.zh: > 您认为每月投资组合中每 100 万美元的股票风险敞口的保护性看跌期权的成本是多少？

sentences.en: "Bubble Insurance" if you will.I expect that would not be a cost-effective way of attaining the risk profile you'd be looking for.I expect there won't be a more cost-effective way of managing your portfolio risk than by simply adjusting your split of broadly-diversified equities vs bonds.
sentences.zh: 如果你愿意的话，可以选择“泡沫保险”。我认为这不是实现您所寻求的风险状况的一种经济高效的方式。我认为，管理投资组合风险的最佳方式莫过于简单地调整广泛多元化的股票与债券的比例。

My homeowners insurance isn't "cost-effective" either but I still do it. I think the reason that investors don't is because they are greedy or irrational or both.

That was the message that I got from a financial podcast I listened to a couple weeks ago anyway.

blocks.html_en:
I'd content that homeowners' insurance is quite cost-effective, because there isn't a cheaper alternative to hedge your risk.

I'm saying that for the cost of buying puts to hedge against equity downside in a retirement portfolio, for any given level of risk, you'd probably be better off just selling some of the equities and buying bonds instead.

e.g. try and find any equity-focused ETF with downside protection that generally outperforms a bog-standard stock/bond split total-market ETF for whatever measure of volatility/downside protection that you want.

sentences.en: I'd content that homeowners' insurance is quite cost-effective, because there isn't a cheaper alternative to hedge your risk.I'm saying that for the cost of buying puts to hedge against equity downside in a retirement portfolio, for any given level of risk, you'd probably be better off just selling some of the equities and buying bonds instead.e.g. try and find any equity-focused ETF with downside protection that generally outperforms a bog-standard stock/bond split total-market ETF for whatever measure of volatility/downside protection that you want.
sentences.zh: 我认为房主保险相当具有成本效益，因为没有更便宜的替代方案来对冲您的风险。我的意思是，对于购买看跌期权以对冲退休投资组合中股票下跌的成本，对于任何给定的风险水平，您可能最好只出售一些股票并购买债券。尝试找到任何具有下行保护的以股票为中心的 ETF，无论您想要何种程度的波动性/下行保护，其表现通常优于沼泽标准股票/债券分割全市场 ETF。

blocks.html_en:
I'd content that homeowners' insurance is quite cost-effective, because there isn't a cheaper alternative to hedge your risk.

I'm saying that for the cost of buying puts to hedge against equity downside in a retirement portfolio, for any given level of risk, you'd probably be better off just selling some of the equities and buying bonds instead.

e.g. try and find any equity-focused ETF with downside protection that generally outperforms a bog-standard stock/bond split total-market ETF for whatever measure of volatility/downside protection that you want.

blocks.html_en: I think the risk for increasing your bond exposure as compensation would be if instead of a low growth/low inflation scenario (Bonds do well) there's a low growth+high inflation scenario (1940s, 1970s, 2022) and the negative correlation between stocks and bonds doesn't hold.

sentences.en: I think the risk for increasing your bond exposure as compensation would be if instead of a low growth/low inflation scenario (Bonds do well) there's a low growth+high inflation scenario (1940s, 1970s, 2022) and the negative correlation between stocks and bonds doesn't hold.
sentences.zh: 我认为增加债券敞口作为补偿的风险是，如果不是低增长/低通胀情景（债券表现良好），而是低增长+高通胀情景（1940 年代、1970 年代、2022 年），并且股票和债券之间的负相关性不成立。

blocks.html_en:
Yeah, but in the abstract that's just saying "if you time the market, you can beat it", and we know that generally, the only way people are able to time the market is with random luck.

And more specifically, it's not low growth/high inflation that kills bond portfolio returns, it's interest rates increasing that devalue bonds, i.e. the transition from low inflation to high inflation. So yeah, you can construct a portfolio that hedges against that... but I'd be surprised if you can do it without decreasing your risk-adjusted expected returns below a plain stock/bond index fund - whatever hedging method you use is either going to increase your interest-rate risk (bonds), or your inflation-rate risk (cash), or is going to limit your upside (buffer etfs), or is just going sap your upfront returns (protective puts).

sentences.en: Yeah, but in the abstract that's just saying "if you time the market, you can beat it", and we know that generally, the only way people are able to time the market is with random luck.And more specifically, it's not low growth/high inflation that kills bond portfolio returns, it's interest rates increasing that devalue bonds, i.e. the transition from low inflation to high inflation.
sentences.zh: 是的，但抽象地说，这只是说“如果你把握市场时机，你就能击败它”，而且我们知道，一般来说，人们能够把握市场时机的唯一方法是随机运气。更具体地说，并不是低增长/高通胀会扼杀债券投资组合的回报，而是利率上升导致债券贬值，即从低通胀到高通胀的转变。

sentences.en: So yeah, you can construct a portfolio that hedges against that...
sentences.zh: 所以是的，你可以构建一个投资组合来对冲......

sentences.en: but I'd be surprised if you can do it without decreasing your risk-adjusted expected returns below a plain stock/bond index fund - whatever hedging method you use is either going to increase your interest-rate risk (bonds), or your inflation-rate risk (cash), or is going to limit your upside (buffer etfs), or is just going sap your upfront returns (protective puts).
sentences.zh: 但如果你能在不将风险调整后的预期回报率降低到普通股票/债券指数基金以下的情况下做到这一点，我会感到惊讶——无论你使用什么对冲方法，要么会增加你的利率风险（债券），要么你的通胀率风险（现金），要么会限制你的上涨空间（缓冲ETF），要么只会削弱你的前期回报（保护性看跌期权）。

blocks.html_en:
In that sense, isn't the 60/40 or Boglehead perspective also timing the market, in the sense that you are betting the regime of the past will continue into the near future?

To me the diversification hedge options (say GUNR) seem like they are helping you get closer to regime neutral. Or in other words you are giving up returns to cover more macro scenarios and betting less on what the future looks like.

sentences.en: In that sense, isn't the 60/40 or Boglehead perspective also timing the market, in the sense that you are betting the regime of the past will continue into the near future?To me the diversification hedge options (say GUNR) seem like they are helping you get closer to regime neutral.
sentences.zh: 从这个意义上说，60/40 或 Boglehead 观点不是也在为市场计时吗？从某种意义上说，你押注过去的政权将持续到不久的将来？对我来说，多元化对冲期权（比如 GUNR）似乎正在帮助你接近政权中立。

sentences.zh: 或者换句话说，你正在放弃回报以涵盖更多宏观场景，并减少对未来的押注。

blocks.html_en:
In a macro sense, I agree with that, but it's very difficult to compete with Vanguard for the fees/overhead to hedge in more regime neutral ways.

It's effectively impossible to hedge against every possibility, including temporary drawdowns, while still having positive returns after inflation.

sentences.zh: 从宏观角度来看，我同意这一点，但很难与先锋集团竞争费用/管理费用，以更中立的方式对冲。实际上不可能对冲所有可能性，包括暂时的回撤，同时在通胀后仍能获得正回报。

blocks.html_en:
> What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis? "Bubble Insurance" if you will. Going 100% bonds is simply intolerable for the time window for most retirees, but an equity wipeout of this magnitude is equally intolerable.

Investment companies have to remain profitable or at-worst neutral as such they would generally charge a decent bit of money for this type of setup. (If they end up having too big of losses then perhaps it could be similar to the the 2007 Banking/Investment companies crisis.)

Generally speaking I am not a financial advisor but you can take a look at international index funds/ETF's in general which have less exposure to AI in general.

and you can follow the age rule created by Mr Bogle where you have (age)% in bonds and (100-age)% in stocks, so at 70 you have 70% bonds, 30% stocks.

So again taking the example of dot com bubble, International Index funds fell from my understanding 30-40% and suppose that you had 30% stocks and 70% bonds.

So that would only have a 30% times 30 % which is 9% which perhaps might be more managable as compared to the previous 25%. There might be some other strategies as well which can help in diversification

Hope this helps!

sentences.en: > What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis?
sentences.zh: > 您认为每月投资组合中每 100 万美元的股票风险敞口的保护性看跌期权的成本是多少？

sentences.en: "Bubble Insurance" if you will.
sentences.zh: 如果你愿意的话，可以选择“泡沫保险”。

sentences.en: Going 100% bonds is simply intolerable for the time window for most retirees, but an equity wipeout of this magnitude is equally intolerable.Investment companies have to remain profitable or at-worst neutral as such they would generally charge a decent bit of money for this type of setup.
sentences.zh: 对于大多数退休人员来说，100% 的债券在这个时间窗口内简直是无法忍受的，但如此大规模的股票损失同样令人无法忍受。投资公司必须保持盈利或最坏的情况是中立，因此他们通常会为这种类型的设置收取相当多的费用。

sentences.en: (If they end up having too big of losses then perhaps it could be similar to the the 2007 Banking/Investment companies crisis.)Generally speaking I am not a financial advisor but you can take a look at international index funds/ETF's in general which have less exposure to AI in general.and you can follow the age rule created by Mr Bogle where you have (age)% in bonds and (100-age)% in stocks, so at 70 you have 70% bonds, 30% stocks.So again taking the example of dot com bubble, International Index funds fell from my understanding 30-40% and suppose that you had 30% stocks and 70% bonds.So that would only have a 30% times 30 % which is 9% which perhaps might be more managable as compared to the previous 25%.
sentences.zh: （如果他们最终损失太大，那么可能会类似于2007年的银行/投资公司危机。）一般来说，我不是财务顾问，但你可以看看国际指数基金/ETF，它们对人工智能的敞口一般较少。你可以遵循博格尔先生创建的年龄规则，其中你有（年龄）%的债券和（100年龄）%的股票，所以在70岁时你有70%的债券，30%的股票股票。再次以互联网泡沫为例，国际指数基金从我的理解中下降了 30-40%，假设你有 30% 的股票和 70% 的债券。所以只有 30% 乘以 30%，即 9%，与之前的 25% 相比，这可能更容易管理。

sentences.en: There might be some other strategies as well which can help in diversificationHope this helps!
sentences.zh: 可能还有一些其他策略可以帮助多元化希望这会有所帮助！

Investment companies have to remain profitable or at-worst neutral as such they would generally charge a decent bit of money for this type of setup. (If they end up having too big of losses then perhaps it could be similar to the the 2007 Banking/Investment companies crisis.)

and you can follow the age rule created by Mr Bogle where you have (age)% in bonds and (100-age)% in stocks, so at 70 you have 70% bonds, 30% stocks.

So again taking the example of dot com bubble, International Index funds fell from my understanding 30-40% and suppose that you had 30% stocks and 70% bonds.

So that would only have a 30% times 30 % which is 9% which perhaps might be more managable as compared to the previous 25%. There might be some other strategies as well which can help in diversification

Hope this helps!

sentences.en: > What do you think the cost would be for protective puts per $1M of equity exposure of a portfolio on a monthly basis?
sentences.zh: > 您认为每月投资组合中每 100 万美元的股票风险敞口的保护性看跌期权的成本是多少？

sentences.en: "Bubble Insurance" if you will.
sentences.zh: 如果你愿意的话，可以选择“泡沫保险”。

sentences.en: Going 100% bonds is simply intolerable for the time window for most retirees, but an equity wipeout of this magnitude is equally intolerable.Investment companies have to remain profitable or at-worst neutral as such they would generally charge a decent bit of money for this type of setup.
sentences.zh: 对于大多数退休人员来说，100% 的债券在这个时间窗口内简直是无法忍受的，但如此大规模的股票损失同样令人无法忍受。投资公司必须保持盈利或最坏的情况是中立，因此他们通常会为这种类型的设置收取相当多的费用。

sentences.en: (If they end up having too big of losses then perhaps it could be similar to the the 2007 Banking/Investment companies crisis.)Generally speaking I am not a financial advisor but you can take a look at international index funds/ETF's in general which have less exposure to AI in general.and you can follow the age rule created by Mr Bogle where you have (age)% in bonds and (100-age)% in stocks, so at 70 you have 70% bonds, 30% stocks.So again taking the example of dot com bubble, International Index funds fell from my understanding 30-40% and suppose that you had 30% stocks and 70% bonds.So that would only have a 30% times 30 % which is 9% which perhaps might be more managable as compared to the previous 25%.
sentences.zh: （如果他们最终损失太大，那么可能会类似于2007年的银行/投资公司危机。）一般来说，我不是财务顾问，但你可以看看国际指数基金/ETF，它们对人工智能的敞口一般较少。你可以遵循博格尔先生创建的年龄规则，其中你有（年龄）%的债券和（100年龄）%的股票，所以在70岁时你有70%的债券，30%的股票股票。再次以互联网泡沫为例，国际指数基金从我的理解中下降了 30-40%，假设你有 30% 的股票和 70% 的债券。所以只有 30% 乘以 30%，即 9%，与之前的 25% 相比，这可能更容易管理。

sentences.en: There might be some other strategies as well which can help in diversificationHope this helps!
sentences.zh: 可能还有一些其他策略可以帮助多元化希望这会有所帮助！

blocks.html_en:
Take SPY at a strike of $738, per lot of 100 that's $73 800. Take 14 lots, give or take, to make a cool million.

SPY260821P00738000 (OCC symbol: PUT on SPY expiring the 21th of August 2026 at a strike of $738) is $12.20 as I type this, so $1220 per lot. So $16 800 to protect for a month. So $200 K per year.

A solid 20% yearly, unless my math is way off.

Now of course you can buy, instead of a PUT, a PUT debit spread, or you can buy further from the strike, or you can finance or partially finance your PUT or PUT debit spread with a CALL you'd sell (turning it into a covered strangle) etc. That's not the point of this exercise though. And anyway I doubt many retirees have the know-how to do that.

In any case it's well known that the costs to hedge are extremely high.

In 1929 those who had 10% gold for example "only" lost 25% overall: gold has value since thousands of years. My dumb thinking is that if gold has value since thousands of years, there's an extremely high probability that it'll keep value for the few decades I've got left at most.

sentences.en: Take SPY at a strike of $738, per lot of 100 that's $73 800.
sentences.zh: 以 SPY 为例，行权价为 738 美元，每手 100 手即为 73 800 美元。

sentences.en: Take 14 lots, give or take, to make a cool million.SPY260821P00738000 (OCC symbol: PUT on SPY expiring the 21th of August 2026 at a strike of $738) is $12.20 as I type this, so $1220 per lot.
sentences.zh: 拿走 14 手，无论给予还是拿取，都能赚到一百万美元。SPY260821P00738000（OCC 符号：SPY 的 PUT，于 2026 年 8 月 21 日到期，行使价为 738 美元）在我输入此内容时为 12.20 美元，因此每手 1220 美元。

sentences.en: So $16 800 to protect for a month.
sentences.zh: 因此，一个月的保障金额为 16 800 美元。

sentences.en: So $200 K per year.A solid 20% yearly, unless my math is way off.Now of course you can buy, instead of a PUT, a PUT debit spread, or you can buy further from the strike, or you can finance or partially finance your PUT or PUT debit spread with a CALL you'd sell (turning it into a covered strangle) etc. That's not the point of this exercise though.
sentences.zh: 所以每年 20 万美元。每年稳定的 20%，除非我的数学太差了。现在当然你可以购买看跌期权借方价差，或者你可以从罢工进一步购买，或者你可以通过卖出看涨期权（将其变成有保护的宽跨式期权）来为你的看涨期权或看跌期权借方价差融资或部分融资等。但这不是本次练习的重点。

sentences.en: And anyway I doubt many retirees have the know-how to do that.In any case it's well known that the costs to hedge are extremely high.In 1929 those who had 10% gold for example "only" lost 25% overall: gold has value since thousands of years.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，我怀疑许多退休人员是否具备这样做的专业知识。无论如何，众所周知，对冲成本非常高。例如，在 1929 年，那些“仅”拥有 10% 黄金的人总体损失了 25%：黄金自数千年来就具有价值。

sentences.en: My dumb thinking is that if gold has value since thousands of years, there's an extremely high probability that it'll keep value for the few decades I've got left at most.
sentences.zh: 我愚蠢的想法是，如果黄金几千年来一直有价值，那么它很有可能在我最多还剩下几十年的时间里保持价值。

blocks.html_en:
A more sensible strategy is probably to protect only against large down moves, so get OTM puts, and then longer maturities (like 1/2 year or so), then rotate them every quarter.

ATM options cost approximately 0.4 S sigma T^0.5 (do a Taylor expansion of the "N"s in the Black Scholes formula), so indeed, for current index vols of about 16% we are talking 0.4 * 16% * (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85% for a 1 month option, and 12 of them indeed cost 22% of your portfolio. Not a good idea.

However, if you hold the options only half the way to expiry, you lose only 1/4 of the time value. And if you buy OTM, you have convexity coming your way on the way down.

Lastly, index vols were very low (until yesterday, ha), as so many firms entered the dispersion trade: they wanted to go long dispersion (some firms do well with AI, some lose out), so short correlation, therefore long single stock vol and short index vol. Which means you could buy index vol (ie protection) quite cheap.

sentences.en: A more sensible strategy is probably to protect only against large down moves, so get OTM puts, and then longer maturities (like 1/2 year or so), then rotate them every quarter.ATM options cost approximately 0.4 S sigma T^0.5 (do a Taylor expansion of the "N"s in the Black Scholes formula), so indeed, for current index vols of about 16% we are talking 0.4 * 16% * (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85% for a 1 month option, and 12 of them indeed cost 22% of your portfolio.
sentences.zh: 更明智的策略可能是只防范大幅下跌，因此先购买 OTM 看跌期权，然后选择更长的期限（例如 1/2 年左右），然后每季度轮换一次。ATM 期权的成本约为 0.4 S sigma T^0.5（对 Black Scholes 公式中的“N”进行泰勒展开），所以实际上，对于约 16% 的当前指数波动率，我们谈论的是 0.4 * 16% * 1 个月期权的 (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85%，其中 12 个期权确实花费了您投资组合的 22%。

sentences.en: Not a good idea.However, if you hold the options only half the way to expiry, you lose only 1/4 of the time value.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个好主意。但是，如果您仅持有期权到到期日的一半，您仅损失 1/4 的时间价值。

sentences.en: And if you buy OTM, you have convexity coming your way on the way down.Lastly, index vols were very low (until yesterday, ha), as so many firms entered the dispersion trade: they wanted to go long dispersion (some firms do well with AI, some lose out), so short correlation, therefore long single stock vol and short index vol.
sentences.zh: 如果你买入 OTM，你就会在下跌过程中遇到凸性。最后，指数波动率非常低（直到昨天，哈），因为很多公司进入分散交易：他们想要做多分散（一些公司在人工智能方面表现良好，一些公司失败），因此做空相关性，因此做多单一股票波动率和空头指数波动率。

sentences.en: Which means you could buy index vol (ie protection) quite cheap.
sentences.zh: 这意味着您可以相当便宜地购买指数波动率（即保护）。

sentences.en: A more sensible strategy is probably to protect only against large down moves, so get OTM puts, and then longer maturities (like 1/2 year or so), then rotate them every quarter.
sentences.zh: 更明智的策略可能是只防范大幅下跌，因此购买 OTM 看跌期权，然后选择较长期限（例如 1/2 年左右），然后每季度轮换一次。

blocks.html_en: ATM options cost approximately 0.4 S sigma T^0.5 (do a Taylor expansion of the "N"s in the Black Scholes formula), so indeed, for current index vols of about 16% we are talking 0.4 * 16% * (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85% for a 1 month option, and 12 of them indeed cost 22% of your portfolio. Not a good idea.

sentences.en: ATM options cost approximately 0.4 S sigma T^0.5 (do a Taylor expansion of the "N"s in the Black Scholes formula), so indeed, for current index vols of about 16% we are talking 0.4 * 16% * (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85% for a 1 month option, and 12 of them indeed cost 22% of your portfolio.
sentences.zh: ATM 期权的成本约为 0.4 S sigma T^0.5（对 Black Scholes 公式中的“N”进行泰勒展开），因此，实际上，对于约 16% 的当前指数波动率，我们所说的 1 个月期权为 0.4 * 16% * (1/12)^0.5 = 1.85%，其中 12 个期权确实花费了您投资组合的 22%。

sentences.en: Not a good idea.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个好主意。

blocks.html_en: However, if you hold the options only half the way to expiry, you lose only 1/4 of the time value. And if you buy OTM, you have convexity coming your way on the way down.

sentences.en: However, if you hold the options only half the way to expiry, you lose only 1/4 of the time value.
sentences.zh: 然而，如果您仅持有期权到到期日的一半，您仅损失 1/4 的时间价值。

sentences.en: And if you buy OTM, you have convexity coming your way on the way down.
sentences.zh: 如果你买入 OTM，你就会在下跌过程中遇到凸性。

blocks.html_en: Lastly, index vols were very low (until yesterday, ha), as so many firms entered the dispersion trade: they wanted to go long dispersion (some firms do well with AI, some lose out), so short correlation, therefore long single stock vol and short index vol. Which means you could buy index vol (ie protection) quite cheap.

sentences.en: Lastly, index vols were very low (until yesterday, ha), as so many firms entered the dispersion trade: they wanted to go long dispersion (some firms do well with AI, some lose out), so short correlation, therefore long single stock vol and short index vol.
sentences.zh: 最后，指数波动率非常低（直到昨天，哈），因为有很多公司进入分散交易：他们想做多分散交易（一些公司在人工智能方面做得很好，一些公司输了），所以做空相关性，因此做多个股波动率和做空指数波动率。

sentences.en: Which means you could buy index vol (ie protection) quite cheap.
sentences.zh: 这意味着您可以相当便宜地购买指数波动率（即保护）。

blocks.html_en:
Inherited IRA's, if you aren't the spouse, have some pretty strict draw down rules.

As the boomers die off - if they have these accounts - their kids are quickly going to be forced to liquidate them over the course of 10 years. With some of them having to sell a chunk annually.

sentences.en: Inherited IRA's, if you aren't the spouse, have some pretty strict draw down rules.As the boomers die off - if they have these accounts - their kids are quickly going to be forced to liquidate them over the course of 10 years.
sentences.zh: 继承的 IRA，如果你不是配偶，有一些相当严格的提款规则。随着婴儿潮一代的去世——如果他们拥有这些账户——他们的孩子很快将被迫在 10 年内清算这些账户。

sentences.en: With some of them having to sell a chunk annually.
sentences.zh: 其中一些人每年必须出售一大块。

blocks.html_en:
NVidia makes up 7.5% of the SP500. If it lost 50%, it would be a 3% loss for the index. The concentration is bad, but it would not cause a drop of 50% retirement funds by itself. If you take an all world index, it's even less.

Still, if NVidia lost 50% of their market share, we would probably see a big collapse of the stock market.

EDIT: to note, the top ten companies in SP500 make up an unprecedented concentration but they're not "mostly AI".

sentences.en: NVidia makes up 7.5% of the SP500.
sentences.zh: NVidia 占 SP500 的 7.5%。

sentences.en: If it lost 50%, it would be a 3% loss for the index.
sentences.zh: 如果下跌 50%，则该指数将下跌 3%。

sentences.en: The concentration is bad, but it would not cause a drop of 50% retirement funds by itself.
sentences.zh: 集中度不好，但本身不会导致退休金下降50%。

sentences.en: If you take an all world index, it's even less.Still, if NVidia lost 50% of their market share, we would probably see a big collapse of the stock market.EDIT: to note, the top ten companies in SP500 make up an unprecedented concentration but they're not "mostly AI".
sentences.zh: 如果你拿一个全球指数来看，这个数字就更少了。不过，如果 NVidia 失去了 50% 的市场份额，我们可能会看到股市大崩盘。编辑：需要注意的是，SP500 中排名前十的公司构成了前所未有的集中度，但它们并不是“主要是人工智能”。

blocks.html_en:
Unless they too were somehow tied into Nvidia.

This is what caused the 08' crash. Everything was all tied together so as one massive bank failed it sent a cascading ripple effect through the entire industry which became a sort of black hole that took down many seemingly stable, profitable banks with it.

I can easily see the same happening with AI.

sentences.en: Unless they too were somehow tied into Nvidia.This is what caused the 08' crash.
sentences.zh: 除非他们也以某种方式与 Nvidia 联系在一起。这就是导致 08' 崩溃的原因。

sentences.en: Everything was all tied together so as one massive bank failed it sent a cascading ripple effect through the entire industry which became a sort of black hole that took down many seemingly stable, profitable banks with it.I can easily see the same happening with AI.
sentences.zh: 一切都联系在一起，因此当一家大型银行倒闭时，它会对整个行业产生连锁反应，成为一种黑洞，摧毁了许多看似稳定、盈利的银行。我可以很容易地看到人工智能也会发生同样的情况。

blocks.html_en: A risk in this situation is that in names that are dominated by passive flows there is a pro-cyclical effect where because the name is valued in terms of the overall index but is also part of said index that you end up with a positive feedback loop - which will eventually be arrested by speculators.

sentences.en: A risk in this situation is that in names that are dominated by passive flows there is a pro-cyclical effect where because the name is valued in terms of the overall index but is also part of said index that you end up with a positive feedback loop - which will eventually be arrested by speculators.
sentences.zh: 这种情况下的一个风险是，在以被动流量为主的名称中，存在顺周期效应，因为该名称是根据整体指数进行估值的，但也是所述指数的一部分，最终会形成正反馈循环 - 最终将被投机者逮捕。

blocks.html_en: An AI collapse would represent a generational buying opportunity for companies like Meta, Google, and MS. It would be bumpy for a bit while things unwind, but eventually all this FCF they have been dumping into AI would start dropping to the bottom line instead. It's like when Meta stopped dumping money in Reality Labs, but on a much larger scale.

sentences.en: An AI collapse would represent a generational buying opportunity for companies like Meta, Google, and MS.
sentences.zh: 对于 Meta、Google 和 MS 等公司来说，人工智能的崩溃将代表着一代人的购买机会。

sentences.en: It would be bumpy for a bit while things unwind, but eventually all this FCF they have been dumping into AI would start dropping to the bottom line instead.
sentences.zh: 事情会在一段时间内变得坎坷，但最终他们投入人工智能的所有自由现金流将开始下降到底线。

sentences.en: It's like when Meta stopped dumping money in Reality Labs, but on a much larger scale.
sentences.zh: 就像 Meta 停止向现实实验室投入资金一样，但规模要大得多。

blocks.html_en:
And a lot of their apparent growth post 2022 is AI, but most likely at subsidized, unsustainable prices. So demand that isn't real.

Plus apparently at least for Google, but from other news sources I've seen, at least Amazon and Oracle have basically mortgaged their future in other business units to fund AI, so it's likely many of these other business units will underperform (or already are).

So lots of new debt, unverifiable growth that could be shady, coupled with a slow down of their other businesses could be a really bad combo.

sentences.en: And a lot of their apparent growth post 2022 is AI, but most likely at subsidized, unsustainable prices.
sentences.zh: 2022 年之后，它们的许多明显增长都是人工智能，但很可能是以补贴的、不可持续的价格。

sentences.en: So demand that isn't real.Plus apparently at least for Google, but from other news sources I've seen, at least Amazon and Oracle have basically mortgaged their future in other business units to fund AI, so it's likely many of these other business units will underperform (or already are).So lots of new debt, unverifiable growth that could be shady, coupled with a slow down of their other businesses could be a really bad combo.
sentences.zh: 所以这种需求显然是不真实的。另外，至少对于谷歌来说，但从我所看到的其他新闻来源来看，至少亚马逊和甲骨文基本上已经将自己的未来抵押在其他业务部门来为人工智能提供资金，因此其他许多业务部门很可能表现不佳（或已经表现不佳）。因此，大量的新债务、可能不可靠的无法验证的增长，再加上其他业务的放缓可能是一个非常糟糕的组合。

blocks.html_en: I have this cheap B movie in my head with a primitive people living on an island. They compete in hunting, fishing, building boats, houses, cutting trees, growing crops etc they use sea shells as currency. Someone finds a spot with countless sea shells, 95% of the population spends their days digging up more and more. Almost everyone is insanely rich, everyone except from the dumb people still hunting, fishing, building boats, houses, cutting trees, growing crops etc

sentences.en: I have this cheap B movie in my head with a primitive people living on an island.
sentences.zh: 我脑子里浮现出一部廉价的 B 级电影，讲述的是住在岛上的原始人的故事。

sentences.en: They compete in hunting, fishing, building boats, houses, cutting trees, growing crops etc they use sea shells as currency.
sentences.zh: 他们在狩猎、捕鱼、建造船只、房屋、砍伐树木、种植农作物等方面进行竞争，他们使用贝壳作为货币。

sentences.en: Someone finds a spot with countless sea shells, 95% of the population spends their days digging up more and more.
sentences.zh: 有人发现一个地方有无数的贝壳，95%的人每天都在挖掘越来越多的贝壳。

sentences.en: Almost everyone is insanely rich, everyone except from the dumb people still hunting, fishing, building boats, houses, cutting trees, growing crops etc
sentences.zh: 几乎每个人都非常富有，除了那些愚蠢的人之外，每个人仍然在打猎、捕鱼、造船、建造房屋、砍伐树木、种植庄稼等

blocks.html_en:
Even if theres a massive drawdown it will recover in the medium term (and in the short term is a great buying opportunity).

For the people who are close/early to retirement and can't do that, well, they need to manage sequence of returns risk.

Edit: I think some ppl might interpret this as me being bullish on the SP500. I'm not, I'm bullish on everything evens out and returns to the mean.

sentences.en: Even if theres a massive drawdown it will recover in the medium term (and in the short term is a great buying opportunity).For the people who are close/early to retirement and can't do that, well, they need to manage sequence of returns risk.Edit: I think some ppl might interpret this as me being bullish on the SP500.
sentences.zh: 即使出现大规模回撤，它也会在中期恢复（短期内是一个很好的买入机会）。对于那些接近/提前退休但无法做到这一点的人来说，他们需要管理回报风险的顺序。编辑：我认为有些人可能会将此解释为我看好 SP500。

sentences.en: I'm not, I'm bullish on everything evens out and returns to the mean.
sentences.zh: 我不是，我看好一切都会趋于平衡并回归均值。
