blocks.html_en: I don’t think concentration risk is itself overly concerning. The nature of a market cap weighted index means it will always be heavy on whatever is currently trending. You’ll certainly be hurting if your plan is to retire at the top of the market with just enough, as the inevitable downturn will hammer your portfolio down into not enough. So invest until you have enough to handle volatility or a lost decade with a dip and slow recovery.

sentences.en: I don’t think concentration risk is itself overly concerning.
sentences.zh: 我认为集中风险本身并不过分令人担忧。

sentences.en: The nature of a market cap weighted index means it will always be heavy on whatever is currently trending.
sentences.zh: 市值加权指数的性质意味着它始终会关注当前的趋势。

sentences.en: You’ll certainly be hurting if your plan is to retire at the top of the market with just enough, as the inevitable downturn will hammer your portfolio down into not enough.
sentences.zh: 如果你的计划是在市场顶端退休时只拥有足够的资金，那么你肯定会受到伤害，因为不可避免的经济低迷将使你的投资组合陷入不足。

sentences.en: So invest until you have enough to handle volatility or a lost decade with a dip and slow recovery.
sentences.zh: 因此，请进行投资，直到您有足够的能力来应对波动或经历衰退和缓慢复苏的失去的十年。

blocks.html_en: For those who stick to a meaningful asset allocation (e.g. 60/40, 80/20, etc), this does not pose significant problem -- they would not be buying much stock in the last 3 years. Instead, they would be buying mostly fixed-income. Probably mostly in 401k/IRA accounts.

sentences.en: For those who stick to a meaningful asset allocation (e.g. 60/40, 80/20, etc), this does not pose significant problem -- they would not be buying much stock in the last 3 years.
sentences.zh: 对于那些坚持有意义的资产配置（例如 60/40、80/20 等）的人来说，这不会造成重大问题——他们在过去 3 年不会购买太多股票。

sentences.en: Instead, they would be buying mostly fixed-income.
sentences.zh: 相反，他们将主要购买固定收益产品。

sentences.en: Probably mostly in 401k/IRA accounts.
sentences.zh: 可能大部分在 401k/IRA 账户中。

blocks.html_en:
>How would it affect retirees if they dropped 40-50%, likely taking the market with them?

a drop of 40-50% in the S&P 500!? That didn't even happen in the market crash of 1929. It would lead to unemployment and breadlines for the majority of the population, and retirees would get in line like everybody else. Making income from your savings requires a productive economy; bonds are not the answer because bonds also stop getting paid, and even govt bonds would be erased by inflation.

it's just not a scenario that should be on your radar, the chance is tiny, and the result would be completely non-linear. if you tried to hedge yourself against that, not only would you fail (it's simply out of your control, like an earthquake or tornado), you also wouldn't make any income in good times, and most times are good and it's sensible to plan for that retirement.

sentences.en: >How would it affect retirees if they dropped 40-50%, likely taking the market with them?a drop of 40-50% in the S&P 500!?
sentences.zh: >如果退休人员股价下跌 40-50%，可能会影响整个市场，这会对他们产生什么影响？标准普尔 500 指数下跌 40-50%！？

sentences.en: That didn't even happen in the market crash of 1929.
sentences.zh: 1929 年的市场崩盘中，这种情况甚至都没有发生。

sentences.en: It would lead to unemployment and breadlines for the majority of the population, and retirees would get in line like everybody else.
sentences.zh: 这将导致大多数人失业和排队领取救济金，而退休人员将像其他人一样排队。

sentences.en: Making income from your savings requires a productive economy; bonds are not the answer because bonds also stop getting paid, and even govt bonds would be erased by inflation.it's just not a scenario that should be on your radar, the chance is tiny, and the result would be completely non-linear.
sentences.zh: 从储蓄中赚取收入需要高效的经济；债券不是答案，因为债券也会停止支付，甚至政府债券也会被通货膨胀抹去。这不是你应该注意的情况，机会很小，结果将是完全非线性的。

sentences.en: if you tried to hedge yourself against that, not only would you fail (it's simply out of your control, like an earthquake or tornado), you also wouldn't make any income in good times, and most times are good and it's sensible to plan for that retirement.
sentences.zh: 如果你试图对冲这种风险，你不仅会失败（这完全是你无法控制的，就像地震或龙卷风一样），而且在经济好的时候你也不会获得任何收入，而大多数情况都是好的，为退休做计划是明智的。

The S&P 500 has dropped over 40% multiple times including 1929. It did it in the 70s, 2000 and 2008/9.

The COVID crash nearly hit those levels also

blocks.html_en:
There is no data showing that high concentration is bad in an index.

No correlation with future returns.

On the other hand the world is leveraged to insane levels not seen since world wars or global recessions.

At the same time yields are low while inflation is high.

There is definitely a high level of risk in the financial markets.

A risk nobody, especially politicians, want to look at, because it would unavoidably lead to some major pains, so procrastinating until it's unavoidable seems the way to go.

sentences.zh: 没有数据表明指数的高度集中是不好的。与未来回报没有相关性。另一方面，世界的杠杆率达到了自世界大战或全球衰退以来从未见过的疯狂水平。与此同时，收益率很低，而通货膨胀却很高。金融市场肯定存在很高的风险。没有人，特别是政治家，愿意关注这种风险，因为它不可避免地会导致一些重大痛苦，因此拖延到不可避免似乎是正确的选择。

blocks.html_en:
The problem some have pointed out is that these companies are such a huge portion of the market right now.

The sound advice for the past decades has been, just invest in a low-cost ETF tracking the S&P instead of picking stocks to minimize risk and invest in the market broadly.

So a huge number of people have done that, believing they're diversified, while tech makes up 40% of the index.

Yes you could sell your S&P and find things to invest in least likely to be impacted by a potential bubble, but your average 9-5'er with automated contributions to their 401k is probably not sophisticated enough to do that.

And that's assuming only these companies would be affected if there was a massive draw-down in tech/AI related stocks. We haven't really seen a situation like this before, so it's not easy to predict what effects there might be in the broader economy.

sentences.en: The problem some have pointed out is that these companies are such a huge portion of the market right now.The sound advice for the past decades has been, just invest in a low-cost ETF tracking the S&P instead of picking stocks to minimize risk and invest in the market broadly.So a huge number of people have done that, believing they're diversified, while tech makes up 40% of the index.Yes you could sell your S&P and find things to invest in least likely to be impacted by a potential bubble, but your average 9-5'er with automated contributions to their 401k is probably not sophisticated enough to do that.And that's assuming only these companies would be affected if there was a massive draw-down in tech/AI related stocks.
sentences.zh: 一些人指出的问题是，这些公司目前在市场中占有如此大的份额。过去几十年的合理建议是，只投资追踪标准普尔指数的低成本 ETF，而不是挑选股票以最大程度地降低风险并广泛投资于市场。因此，很多人都这样做了，相信他们是多元化的，而科技股占该指数的 40%。是的，你可以卖掉你的标准普尔指数，然后找到最不可能受到潜在泡沫影响的投资对象，但你的平均水平朝九晚五的人自动向 401k 缴款可能还不够复杂，无法做到这一点。而且这是假设如果科技/人工智能相关股票大幅下跌，只有这些公司会受到影响。

sentences.en: We haven't really seen a situation like this before, so it's not easy to predict what effects there might be in the broader economy.
sentences.zh: 我们以前从未真正见过这样的情况，因此很难预测对更广泛的经济可能产生的影响。

blocks.html_en:
The problem some have pointed out is that these companies are such a huge portion of the market right now.

The sound advice for the past decades has been, just invest in a low-cost ETF tracking the S&P instead of picking stocks to minimize risk and invest in the market broadly.

So a huge number of people have done that, believing they're diversified, while tech makes up 40% of the index.

Yes you could sell your S&P and find things to invest in least likely to be impacted by a potential bubble, but your average 9-5'er with automated contributions to their 401k is probably not sophisticated enough to do that.

And that's assuming only these companies would be affected if there was a massive draw-down in tech/AI related stocks. We haven't really seen a situation like this before, so it's not easy to predict what effects there might be in the broader economy.

sentences.en: The problem some have pointed out is that these companies are such a huge portion of the market right now.The sound advice for the past decades has been, just invest in a low-cost ETF tracking the S&P instead of picking stocks to minimize risk and invest in the market broadly.So a huge number of people have done that, believing they're diversified, while tech makes up 40% of the index.Yes you could sell your S&P and find things to invest in least likely to be impacted by a potential bubble, but your average 9-5'er with automated contributions to their 401k is probably not sophisticated enough to do that.And that's assuming only these companies would be affected if there was a massive draw-down in tech/AI related stocks.

sentences.en: We haven't really seen a situation like this before, so it's not easy to predict what effects there might be in the broader economy.

blocks.html_en:
That is an overly conservative approach that sacrifices a lot of growth for not much more safety. It also exposes you to inflation risk, which is a significant concern these days.

Most people in actual retirement I know do something like keep ~2 years of cash in short-term treasuries and everything else in equities. That gives you a lot of buffer to time-shift equity drawdown, which is the main risk with equities, while retaining almost all of the benefit of equities. Simple and relatively robust.

sentences.en: That is an overly conservative approach that sacrifices a lot of growth for not much more safety.
sentences.zh: 这是一种过于保守的方法，牺牲了大量的增长而没有获得更多的安全性。

sentences.en: It also exposes you to inflation risk, which is a significant concern these days.Most people in actual retirement I know do something like keep ~2 years of cash in short-term treasuries and everything else in equities.
sentences.zh: 它还会让你面临通货膨胀风险，这是当今的一个重大问题。我认识的大多数实际退休的人都会做一些事情，比如将大约 2 年的现金存放在短期国库券中，并将其他所有东西存放在股票中。

sentences.en: That gives you a lot of buffer to time-shift equity drawdown, which is the main risk with equities, while retaining almost all of the benefit of equities.
sentences.zh: 这为您提供了很大的缓冲时间来应对股票的主要风险，同时保留股票的几乎所有收益。

sentences.en: Simple and relatively robust.
sentences.zh: 简单且相对稳健。

blocks.html_en:
It literally doesn't matter if drops 50% in a year. That is a paper loss and you have years worth of cash you can spend while waiting for it to recover. If you panic-sell at the bottom of that market then that's on you. It isn't necessary in order to pay the bills.

What you propose takes on a huge amount of inflation risk. How are you hedging that risk? A guaranteed yield doesn't mean you aren't getting poorer. Obsessing over one type of risk and ignoring another isn't rational.

Reducing variance of net worth has a very high cost. Over-indexing on that singular property, particularly when most people can afford some variability, is a recipe for relative impoverishment.

sentences.en: It literally doesn't matter if drops 50% in a year.
sentences.zh: 如果一年内下跌 50%，实际上并不重要。

sentences.en: That is a paper loss and you have years worth of cash you can spend while waiting for it to recover.
sentences.zh: 这是账面损失，您可以在等待其恢复期间花费数年的现金。

sentences.en: If you panic-sell at the bottom of that market then that's on you.
sentences.zh: 如果你在市场底部恐慌性抛售，那么这就是你的责任。

sentences.en: It isn't necessary in order to pay the bills.What you propose takes on a huge amount of inflation risk.
sentences.zh: 为了支付账单，这并不是必需的。你的建议会带来巨大的通货膨胀风险。

sentences.en: How are you hedging that risk?
sentences.zh: 您如何对冲这种风险？

sentences.en: A guaranteed yield doesn't mean you aren't getting poorer.
sentences.zh: 有保证的收益并不意味着你不会变得更穷。

sentences.en: Obsessing over one type of risk and ignoring another isn't rational.Reducing variance of net worth has a very high cost.
sentences.zh: 沉迷于一种风险而忽视另一种风险是不理性的。减少净资产方差的成本非常高。

sentences.en: Over-indexing on that singular property, particularly when most people can afford some variability, is a recipe for relative impoverishment.
sentences.zh: 对单一财产的过度索引，特别是当大多数人能够承受一定的可变性时，是导致相对贫困的一个因素。

blocks.html_en: It’s probably not even a good idea to try and defend against the bubble by switching up your stock allocation. After all the whole reason passive investing works is that active investment rarely beats the market and if you’ve just been in SPY the whole time it’s unlike you have any edge to gain by switching to an active strategy every time fear creeps up

sentences.en: It’s probably not even a good idea to try and defend against the bubble by switching up your stock allocation.
sentences.zh: 通过改变股票配置来尝试抵御泡沫甚至可能不是一个好主意。

sentences.en: After all the whole reason passive investing works is that active investment rarely beats the market and if you’ve just been in SPY the whole time it’s unlike you have any edge to gain by switching to an active strategy every time fear creeps up
sentences.zh: 毕竟，被动投资有效的全部原因在于，主动投资很少能打败市场，如果你一直处于间谍状态，那么每次恐惧蔓延时，你就不可能通过转向主动策略来获得任何优势。

If you’ve watched The Big Short even those who predicted the housing crisis correctly nearly lost their shirt before and after it happened because timing the crash is the hard part. There is a whole body of research showing that passive strategies outperform active after crashes

blocks.html_en:
The employer selects a financial company to manage the 401k. When you switch jobs, you can roll the 401k from the previous employer into the new one, or into an IRA (Individual Retirement Account).

Usually the financial services company will offer several options: more aggressive/high risk, or less aggressive/lower risk. Most people will just go with whatever is the default option.

So much of the American S&P 500 is dominated by handful of companies that the risk is not that easy to avoid. If or when the AI bubble pops, it's going to take down a lot of the economy with it. You can direct your retirement savings into the lowest yield/lowest risk assets offered by the firm, but you'll forego whatever growth happens in the mean time.

Will the bubble pop next week? Next month? Next year? Who knows. Timing the market is incredibly difficult.

There's a famous quote, attributed (perhaps apocryphally) to John Maynard Keynes: "The market can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent."

sentences.en: The employer selects a financial company to manage the 401k.
sentences.zh: 雇主选择一家金融公司来管理 401k。

sentences.zh: 大多数人都会选择默认选项。美国标准普尔 500 指数的大部分股票都由少数公司主导，因此风险并不容易避免。

sentences.en: If or when the AI bubble pops, it's going to take down a lot of the economy with it.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能泡沫破灭，将会拖垮大量经济。

sentences.en: You can direct your retirement savings into the lowest yield/lowest risk assets offered by the firm, but you'll forego whatever growth happens in the mean time.Will the bubble pop next week?
sentences.zh: 您可以将退休储蓄投入公司提供的最低收益/最低风险资产，但您将放弃同时发生的任何增长。下周泡沫会破裂吗？

sentences.en: Next month?
sentences.zh: 下个月？

sentences.en: Next year?
sentences.zh: 明年？

sentences.en: Who knows.
sentences.zh: 谁知道呢。

sentences.en: Timing the market is incredibly difficult.There's a famous quote, attributed (perhaps apocryphally) to John Maynard Keynes: "The market can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent."
sentences.zh: 把握市场时机非常困难。约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯有一句名言（也许是杜撰的）：“市场保持非理性的时间可能比你保持偿付能力的时间还要长。”

blocks.html_en:
The whole idea of a pension fund is that you don't need to time the system it is the system.

Like my country pension scheme. It went through ups and downs for a hundred years but has always come on top. All you need is a long horizon and a trillion dollars and you basically can't lose.

sentences.en: The whole idea of a pension fund is that you don't need to time the system it is the system.Like my country pension scheme.
sentences.zh: 养老基金的整体理念是，你不需要为系统计时，它就是系统。就像我国家的养老金计划一样。

sentences.en: It went through ups and downs for a hundred years but has always come on top.
sentences.zh: 一百年来，它经历了风风雨雨，但始终屹立不倒。

sentences.en: All you need is a long horizon and a trillion dollars and you basically can't lose.
sentences.zh: 你所需要的只是长远的眼光和一万亿美元，你基本上不会输。

blocks.html_en: Yes of course life insurance and pension funds are going to buy this debt. This is the highest quality debt that's out there. blocks.html_en: OK, wait a minute. Elsewhere in this discussion, people are saying that only a few of these AI companies are going to survive. For stocks, that can still be a reasonable investment - low odds, but still a positive expectation value - but for bonds, it's terrible. You're paying me single-digit interest when there's only a 20% chance that you live long enough to give me my principle back? Get outta here. Literally nobody should be investing in such bonds.

blocks.html_en: OK, wait a minute. Elsewhere in this discussion, people are saying that only a few of these AI companies are going to survive. For stocks, that can still be a reasonable investment - low odds, but still a positive expectation value - but for bonds, it's terrible. You're paying me single-digit interest when there's only a 20% chance that you live long enough to give me my principle back? Get outta here. Literally nobody should be investing in such bonds.

blocks.html_en:
FWIW, a firm can go bankrupt and the stocks be worth zero with the bond holders being paid 100%. In fact, that's sort of the goal and "ideal" scenario (ideal given bankruptcy, of course, which in turn is not ideal).

In the real world, recovery rates for corporate bonds are between 30% to 70% or so, depending on the seniority of the debt and the collateral.

sentences.en: FWIW, a firm can go bankrupt and the stocks be worth zero with the bond holders being paid 100%.
sentences.zh: FWIW，一家公司可能会破产，股票价值为零，而债券持有人将获得 100% 的支付。

sentences.en: In fact, that's sort of the goal and "ideal" scenario (ideal given bankruptcy, of course, which in turn is not ideal).In the real world, recovery rates for corporate bonds are between 30% to 70% or so, depending on the seniority of the debt and the collateral.
sentences.zh: 事实上，这就是目标和“理想”场景（当然，考虑到破产是理想的情况，但破产的情况并不理想）。在现实世界中，公司债券的回收率在 30% 到 70% 左右，具体取决于债务和抵押品的优先级。

> buy debt from big tech companies because you believe it's too risky, then you believe that bonds are just too risky in general

Uh, no? Ignoring the rating, I think there are plenty of other bonds to be found that are less risky.

Dutch government bonds just to name one? The yield wont be the same but that's probably a good indicator?

blocks.html_en:
> When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.

Debt is senior to equity. For private credit to start taking haircuts, the equity has to have already gone to zero. At that point, this will already have been everyone's problem for some time.

sentences.en: > When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.Debt is senior to equity.
sentences.zh: > 当这些失败时，它将成为每个人的问题。债务优先于股权。

sentences.en: For private credit to start taking haircuts, the equity has to have already gone to zero.
sentences.zh: 为了让私人信贷开始削减，资产净值必须已经为零。

sentences.en: At that point, this will already have been everyone's problem for some time.
sentences.zh: 到那时，这已经成为每个人的问题一段时间了。

blocks.html_en:
Are you suggesting that holding private credit assets is relatively risk free?

Equity is a risky asset, so equity being wiped out should not be a surprise to anyone, but life insurance and pension funds failures is indeed a public problem.

blocks.html_en: There's a wide spectrum of 'private credit', and it varies from low-risk to quite high risk, depending on the debtor. In the case of "AI Companies", their debt is low-risk, but many are creating special-purpose-entities which will build and own some or all of their newer datacenters. Those datacenter companies are issuing a great deal of somewhat risky debt; with the exact level of risk depending on the off-take agreement.

sentences.zh: “私人信贷”的范围很广，从低风险到相当高风险不等，具体取决于债务人。

sentences.zh: 这些数据中心公司正在发行大量具有一定风险的债务；确切的风险级别取决于承购协议。

blocks.html_en: You probably meant to say that practically, high leverage tends to leak into companies of public interest. For example, when high net worth individuals start trimming their private credit holdings, which eventually end up with insurers. That is why the regulators have to watch carefully that it does not happen.

blocks.html_en:
No, I meant that having a lot of debt puts you in a position to be more vulnerable to any kind of negative outcome. Intuitively, this seems to hold true across the spectrum from the personal level to the government level.

If a person has a lot of debt and no savings, and they lose their job, then they will find themselves in trouble a lot faster than someone who owns their home and car and has 6 months in a savings account.

If a company has a lot of debt, they might find themselves in trouble with credit rating agencies as soon as they have a bad quarter. Or they might have cashflow issues if rates increase. Both of these can lead to an accelerating negative feedback loop.

Governments (at least those with fiscal independence) have a unique set of tools to work around this situation, but they too can struggle with high debt loads acting as a drag on future prosperity.

Debt plays a critical societal role in allowing new production in advance of revenues, but it can also be a dangerous trap.

These AI and tech companies are priced as if they are still running a capital-light, 0-marginal-cost SaaS business. That's no longer what's happening. This economic engine makes up a substantial amount of both the value and the growth in the American stock market.

If there's a loss in confidence, high leverage will make things fall faster. This could be infectious. Not just the AI and tech companies, but the whole market might suffer.

sentences.en: No, I meant that having a lot of debt puts you in a position to be more vulnerable to any kind of negative outcome.
sentences.zh: 不，我的意思是背负大量债务会让你更容易受到任何负面结果的影响。

sentences.en: Intuitively, this seems to hold true across the spectrum from the personal level to the government level.If a person has a lot of debt and no savings, and they lose their job, then they will find themselves in trouble a lot faster than someone who owns their home and car and has 6 months in a savings account.If a company has a lot of debt, they might find themselves in trouble with credit rating agencies as soon as they have a bad quarter.

sentences.en: Or they might have cashflow issues if rates increase.
sentences.zh: 或者，如果利率上涨，他们可能会遇到现金流问题。

sentences.en: Both of these can lead to an accelerating negative feedback loop.Governments (at least those with fiscal independence) have a unique set of tools to work around this situation, but they too can struggle with high debt loads acting as a drag on future prosperity.Debt plays a critical societal role in allowing new production in advance of revenues, but it can also be a dangerous trap.These AI and tech companies are priced as if they are still running a capital-light, 0-marginal-cost SaaS business.
sentences.zh: 这两者都可能导致加速的负反馈循环。政府（至少是那些财政独立的政府）拥有一套独特的工具来解决这种情况，但它们也可能会面临高债务负担，从而拖累未来的繁荣。债务在允许新产品先于收入产生方面发挥着关键的社会作用，但它也可能是一个危险的陷阱。这些人工智能和科技公司的定价就好像它们仍在经营轻资本、零边际成本的 SaaS 业务一样。

sentences.en: That's no longer what's happening.
sentences.zh: 现在已经不再是这样了。

sentences.en: This economic engine makes up a substantial amount of both the value and the growth in the American stock market.If there's a loss in confidence, high leverage will make things fall faster.
sentences.zh: 这个经济引擎在美国股市的价值和增长中占据了很大一部分。如果信心丧失，高杠杆会让股市下跌得更快。

sentences.en: This could be infectious.
sentences.zh: 这可能具有传染性。

sentences.en: Not just the AI and tech companies, but the whole market might suffer.
sentences.zh: 不仅仅是人工智能和科技公司，整个市场都可能受到影响。

blocks.html_en:
> As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine. The trouble is that private credit is taking control of some life insurance companies and off-loads this debt to these. When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.

Three things:

1) at least SpaceX is already in pension funds "thanks" to NASDAQ and MSCI relaxing their rules. Everyone who invests in NASDAQ or in MSCI World has SpaceX exposure, and assuming the bonanza lasts for 11 more months, so will everyone who invests into S&P 500. In addition NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft, Oracle and Amazon all have been in pretty much every investor's / pension fund depots. No matter what, everyone is going to get fucked when the party crashes, and it will make 2007 look harmless by comparison.

2) The debt of the AI companies is bad enough, but there's all the downstream credit as well, chiefly construction companies and public utilities that are undertaking absurd amounts of buildout. When the party crashes and the demand stops, there will be a lot of construction companies and possibly even a few large utility companies that will be unable to service their debt (because no datacenter means no income) or have to hike rates even more than they already are.

3) All this debt and speculation unwinding will cause an economic downturn. Most of Europe already is in or near recession territory, and the US would be in a recession if it weren't for the wash trading and circular investments artificially propping up the GDP. But unfortunately, with the exception of infamously austere Germany, everyone else has already fired all the guns during 2007ff and Covid, and all the ZIRP money never got slowly deflated out of the market, which means this time there will be no government help possible, it will be a hard crash. No way out of that one.

sentences.en: > As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine.
sentences.zh: > 只要这笔债务不进入人寿保险和养老基金，我们就没事。

sentences.en: The trouble is that private credit is taking control of some life insurance companies and off-loads this debt to these.
sentences.zh: 问题在于，私人信贷正在控制一些人寿保险公司，并将债务转移给这些公司。

sentences.en: When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.Three things:1) at least SpaceX is already in pension funds "thanks" to NASDAQ and MSCI relaxing their rules.
sentences.zh: 当这些失败时，它将成为每个人的问题。三件事：1）至少 SpaceX 已经进入养老基金“感谢”纳斯达克和摩根士丹利资本国际放宽了他们的规则。

sentences.en: Everyone who invests in NASDAQ or in MSCI World has SpaceX exposure, and assuming the bonanza lasts for 11 more months, so will everyone who invests into S&P 500.
sentences.zh: 每个投资纳斯达克或 MSCI World 的人都有 SpaceX 的风险敞口，假设这种繁荣还能持续 11 个月，那么每个投资标准普尔 500 指数的人也会如此。

sentences.en: In addition NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft, Oracle and Amazon all have been in pretty much every investor's / pension fund depots.
sentences.zh: 此外，英伟达、谷歌、微软、甲骨文和亚马逊几乎都在每个投资者/养老基金的仓库中。

sentences.en: No matter what, everyone is going to get fucked when the party crashes, and it will make 2007 look harmless by comparison.2) The debt of the AI companies is bad enough, but there's all the downstream credit as well, chiefly construction companies and public utilities that are undertaking absurd amounts of buildout.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，当派对崩溃时，每个人都会被操蛋，相比之下，这会让 2007 年看起来无害。2）人工智能公司的债务已经够糟糕了，但还有所有下游信贷，主要是建筑公司和公用事业公司，它们正在进行大量的扩建。

sentences.en: When the party crashes and the demand stops, there will be a lot of construction companies and possibly even a few large utility companies that will be unable to service their debt (because no datacenter means no income) or have to hike rates even more than they already are.3) All this debt and speculation unwinding will cause an economic downturn.
sentences.zh: 当聚会崩溃并且需求停止时，将会有很多建筑公司，甚至可能是一些大型公用事业公司将无法偿还债务（因为没有数据中心意味着没有收入），或者不得不加息，甚至比现在更高。3）所有这些债务和投机的平仓将导致经济衰退。

sentences.en: Most of Europe already is in or near recession territory, and the US would be in a recession if it weren't for the wash trading and circular investments artificially propping up the GDP.
sentences.zh: 欧洲大部分地区已经处于或接近衰退区域，如果不是清洗交易和循环投资人为支撑GDP，美国也将陷入衰退。

sentences.en: But unfortunately, with the exception of infamously austere Germany, everyone else has already fired all the guns during 2007ff and Covid, and all the ZIRP money never got slowly deflated out of the market, which means this time there will be no government help possible, it will be a hard crash.
sentences.zh: 但不幸的是，除了臭名昭著的紧缩德国之外，其他国家都已经在2007年和Covid期间开枪了，所有ZIRP资金从未慢慢退出市场，这意味着这一次将不再有政府的帮助，这将是一次严重的崩溃。

sentences.en: No way out of that one.
sentences.zh: 没有办法摆脱那个。

sentences.zh: 当这些失败时，它将成为每个人的问题。三件事：1）至少 SpaceX 已经进入养老基金“感谢”纳斯达克和摩根士丹利资本国际放宽了他们的规则。

sentences.en: Everyone who invests in NASDAQ or in MSCI World has SpaceX exposure, and assuming the bonanza lasts for 11 more months, so will everyone who invests into S&P 500.
sentences.zh: 每个投资纳斯达克或 MSCI World 的人都有 SpaceX 的风险敞口，假设这种繁荣还能持续 11 个月，那么每个投资标准普尔 500 指数的人也会如此。

sentences.en: In addition NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft, Oracle and Amazon all have been in pretty much every investor's / pension fund depots.
sentences.zh: 此外，英伟达、谷歌、微软、甲骨文和亚马逊几乎都在每个投资者/养老基金的仓库中。

sentences.en: No matter what, everyone is going to get fucked when the party crashes, and it will make 2007 look harmless by comparison.2) The debt of the AI companies is bad enough, but there's all the downstream credit as well, chiefly construction companies and public utilities that are undertaking absurd amounts of buildout.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，当派对崩溃时，每个人都会被操蛋，相比之下，这会让 2007 年看起来无害。2）人工智能公司的债务已经够糟糕了，但还有所有下游信贷，主要是建筑公司和公用事业公司，它们正在进行大量的扩建。

sentences.en: When the party crashes and the demand stops, there will be a lot of construction companies and possibly even a few large utility companies that will be unable to service their debt (because no datacenter means no income) or have to hike rates even more than they already are.3) All this debt and speculation unwinding will cause an economic downturn.
sentences.zh: 当聚会崩溃并且需求停止时，将会有很多建筑公司，甚至可能是一些大型公用事业公司将无法偿还债务（因为没有数据中心意味着没有收入），或者不得不加息，甚至比现在更高。3）所有这些债务和投机的平仓将导致经济衰退。

sentences.en: Most of Europe already is in or near recession territory, and the US would be in a recession if it weren't for the wash trading and circular investments artificially propping up the GDP.
sentences.zh: 欧洲大部分地区已经处于或接近衰退区域，如果不是清洗交易和循环投资人为支撑GDP，美国也将陷入衰退。

sentences.en: But unfortunately, with the exception of infamously austere Germany, everyone else has already fired all the guns during 2007ff and Covid, and all the ZIRP money never got slowly deflated out of the market, which means this time there will be no government help possible, it will be a hard crash.
sentences.zh: 但不幸的是，除了臭名昭著的紧缩德国之外，其他国家都已经在2007年和Covid期间开枪了，所有ZIRP资金从未慢慢退出市场，这意味着这一次将不再有政府的帮助，这将是一次严重的崩溃。

sentences.en: No way out of that one.
sentences.zh: 没有办法摆脱那个。

blocks.html_en: > As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine. The trouble is that private credit is taking control of some life insurance companies and off-loads this debt to these. When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.

sentences.en: > As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine.
sentences.zh: > 只要这笔债务不进入人寿保险和养老基金，我们就没事。

sentences.en: The trouble is that private credit is taking control of some life insurance companies and off-loads this debt to these.
sentences.zh: 问题在于，私人信贷正在控制一些人寿保险公司，并将债务转移给这些公司。

sentences.en: When these fail, it will become everyone's problem.
sentences.zh: 当这些失败时，这将成为每个人的问题。

blocks.html_en: Three things:

sentences.en: Three things:
sentences.zh: 三件事：

blocks.html_en: 1) at least SpaceX is already in pension funds "thanks" to NASDAQ and MSCI relaxing their rules. Everyone who invests in NASDAQ or in MSCI World has SpaceX exposure, and assuming the bonanza lasts for 11 more months, so will everyone who invests into S&P 500. In addition NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft, Oracle and Amazon all have been in pretty much every investor's / pension fund depots. No matter what, everyone is going to get fucked when the party crashes, and it will make 2007 look harmless by comparison.

sentences.en: 1) at least SpaceX is already in pension funds "thanks" to NASDAQ and MSCI relaxing their rules.
sentences.zh: 1）至少“感谢”纳斯达克和摩根士丹利资本国际放宽了规则，SpaceX 已经加入了养老基金。

sentences.en: Everyone who invests in NASDAQ or in MSCI World has SpaceX exposure, and assuming the bonanza lasts for 11 more months, so will everyone who invests into S&P 500.
sentences.zh: 每个投资纳斯达克或 MSCI World 的人都有 SpaceX 的风险敞口，假设这种繁荣还能持续 11 个月，那么每个投资标准普尔 500 指数的人也会如此。

sentences.en: In addition NVIDIA, Google, Microsoft, Oracle and Amazon all have been in pretty much every investor's / pension fund depots.
sentences.zh: 此外，英伟达、谷歌、微软、甲骨文和亚马逊几乎都在每个投资者/养老基金的仓库中。

sentences.en: No matter what, everyone is going to get fucked when the party crashes, and it will make 2007 look harmless by comparison.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，当聚会失败时，每个人都会被操蛋，相比之下，这会让 2007 年看起来无害。

blocks.html_en: 2) The debt of the AI companies is bad enough, but there's all the downstream credit as well, chiefly construction companies and public utilities that are undertaking absurd amounts of buildout. When the party crashes and the demand stops, there will be a lot of construction companies and possibly even a few large utility companies that will be unable to service their debt (because no datacenter means no income) or have to hike rates even more than they already are.

sentences.en: 2) The debt of the AI companies is bad enough, but there's all the downstream credit as well, chiefly construction companies and public utilities that are undertaking absurd amounts of buildout.
sentences.zh: 2）人工智能公司的债务已经够糟糕了，但还有所有下游信贷，主要是建筑公司和公用事业公司，它们正在进行大量的扩建。

sentences.en: When the party crashes and the demand stops, there will be a lot of construction companies and possibly even a few large utility companies that will be unable to service their debt (because no datacenter means no income) or have to hike rates even more than they already are.
sentences.zh: 当派对崩溃并且需求停止时，将会有很多建筑公司，甚至可能是一些大型公用事业公司将无法偿还债务（因为没有数据中心意味着没有收入），或者不得不将利率提高到比现在更高的水平。

blocks.html_en: 3) All this debt and speculation unwinding will cause an economic downturn. Most of Europe already is in or near recession territory, and the US would be in a recession if it weren't for the wash trading and circular investments artificially propping up the GDP. But unfortunately, with the exception of infamously austere Germany, everyone else has already fired all the guns during 2007ff and Covid, and all the ZIRP money never got slowly deflated out of the market, which means this time there will be no government help possible, it will be a hard crash. No way out of that one.

sentences.en: 3) All this debt and speculation unwinding will cause an economic downturn.
sentences.zh: 3）所有这些债务和投机的平仓将导致经济衰退。

sentences.en: But unfortunately, with the exception of infamously austere Germany, everyone else has already fired all the guns during 2007ff and Covid, and all the ZIRP money never got slowly deflated out of the market, which means this time there will be no government help possible, it will be a hard crash.
sentences.zh: 但不幸的是，除了臭名昭著的紧缩德国之外，其他国家都已经在2007年和Covid期间开枪了，所有ZIRP资金从未慢慢退出市场，这意味着这一次将不再有政府的帮助，这将是一次严重的崩溃。

sentences.en: No way out of that one.
sentences.zh: 没有办法摆脱那个。

blocks.html_en:
> As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine.

I think for small to even large numbers you are correct, but given how yuge this debt amount is a broad-based default will probably cause a contagion. I will not predict how far and wide.

sentences.en: > As long as this debt does not make it into life insurance and pension funds, we are fine.I think for small to even large numbers you are correct, but given how yuge this debt amount is a broad-based default will probably cause a contagion.
sentences.zh: > 只要这笔债务不进入人寿保险和养老基金，我们就没事。我认为对于从小到大的数字来说，你是正确的，但考虑到这笔债务金额有多大，广泛的违约可能会导致蔓延。

sentences.en: I will not predict how far and wide.
sentences.zh: 我不会预测有多远和多宽。

blocks.html_en: You say this as though every company doesn't take on debt, and all debt isn't a risk. I'm sure you have some much riskier debt than ChatGPT already in your portfolio, and interest rates are adjusted by relative as judged by the market. I'm sure some debt will fail, but certainly all of it won't, and while anything could cause a market crash saying "when these fail" holds a lot of incorrect assumptions.

sentences.en: You say this as though every company doesn't take on debt, and all debt isn't a risk.
sentences.zh: 你这么说就好像并不是每家公司都承担债务，而且所有债务都不是风险。

sentences.en: I'm sure you have some much riskier debt than ChatGPT already in your portfolio, and interest rates are adjusted by relative as judged by the market.
sentences.zh: 我确信您的投资组合中已有一些比 ChatGPT 风险更高的债务，并且利率根据市场判断进行相对调整。

blocks.html_en:
bingo - if the firms holding the debt keep holding the debt & the debt doesn't get passed to other entities - the system will be fine.

if say meta owes 720Bn, they wouldn't have trouble paying that back in 10 years.

this doesn't take away the fact that 'a.i' right now is a bubble.

sentences.en: bingo - if the firms holding the debt keep holding the debt & the debt doesn't get passed to other entities - the system will be fine.if say meta owes 720Bn, they wouldn't have trouble paying that back in 10 years.this doesn't take away the fact that 'a.i' right now is a bubble.
sentences.zh: 宾果游戏 - 如果持有债务的公司继续持有债务并且债务不会传递给其他实体 - 系统会很好。如果说元欠 7200 亿美元，他们在 10 年内偿还这笔钱不会有困难。这并不能消除“人工智能”现在是泡沫的事实。

blocks.html_en:
Do they? Is a company with $200 billion annual revenue and earnings (EBITDA) of $100 billion having $420 billion of off-balance-sheet debt really staggering?

In many other industries that would be a perfectly normal amount of debt to have. It's only unusual because we are used to tech companies having so much cash on hand they don't know where to put it

sentences.en: Do they?
sentences.zh: 他们吗？

sentences.en: Is a company with $200 billion annual revenue and earnings (EBITDA) of $100 billion having $420 billion of off-balance-sheet debt really staggering?In many other industries that would be a perfectly normal amount of debt to have.
sentences.zh: 一家年收入 2000 亿美元、利润 (EBITDA) 为 1000 亿美元的公司拥有 4200 亿美元的表外债务真的令人震惊吗？在许多其他行业，这是完全正常的债务数额。

sentences.en: It's only unusual because we are used to tech companies having so much cash on hand they don't know where to put it
sentences.zh: 这很不寻常，因为我们习惯了科技公司手头有大量现金，他们不知道该把它放在哪里

blocks.html_en:
I think that a lot of the accounting, used mostly in factories and such, is having trouble with the new financial reality in the tech space. Running a factory, having a debt to asset ratio over 1.0 would be disastrous because the assets are unable to produce revenue fast enough to cover the interest.

With tech it's weird because something that is worth nothing on an accounting sheet could be worth a hundred billion dollars during a sale to Alphabet or Meta.

sentences.en: I think that a lot of the accounting, used mostly in factories and such, is having trouble with the new financial reality in the tech space.
sentences.zh: 我认为，大部分在工厂等中使用的会计在科技领域的新财务现实中遇到了麻烦。

sentences.en: Running a factory, having a debt to asset ratio over 1.0 would be disastrous because the assets are unable to produce revenue fast enough to cover the interest.With tech it's weird because something that is worth nothing on an accounting sheet could be worth a hundred billion dollars during a sale to Alphabet or Meta.
sentences.zh: 经营一家工厂，资产负债率超过 1.0 将是灾难性的，因为资产无法足够快地产生收入来支付利息。对于科技来说，这很奇怪，因为在会计报表上一文不值的东西在出售给 Alphabet 或 Meta 时可能价值 1000 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: With tech it's weird because something that is worth nothing on an accounting sheet could be worth a hundred billion dollars during a sale to Alphabet or Meta.

sentences.en: With tech it's weird because something that is worth nothing on an accounting sheet could be worth a hundred billion dollars during a sale to Alphabet or Meta.
sentences.zh: 对于科技行业来说，这很奇怪，因为在会计报表上一文不值的东西在出售给 Alphabet 或 Meta 时可能价值 1000 亿美元。

blocks.html_en:
These companies have valuations reflecting a debt light business. At a minimum, 420 billion in debt is enough to change the stock price by 10-20%. If the company plans to add another 400 billion in debt you need to give it the side eye.

If 50 billion in revenue is from other companies debt spending… then You have a problem.

sentences.en: These companies have valuations reflecting a debt light business.
sentences.zh: 这些公司的估值反映了其轻负债业务。

sentences.zh: 至少，4200亿的债务足以让股价改变10-20%。

blocks.html_en:
To be precise, market perception of future growth.

All other things being equal, debt is a downward force on that perception.

That is a general point, although I'll stipulate it isn't a factor in AI picks because they are approximately all doing the same and because the market is giddy with FOMO. More strongly, i believe that some Schmanthropic with equivalent offering and unit cost and userbase but with sustainable finance would signal, through lack of recklessness, that it is not "going to the moon". I'm willing to call this "irrationality". But hey, I'm not exposed, other than being a taxpayer with savings who will inevitably foot the bill for the bailouts.

sentences.en: To be precise, market perception of future growth.All other things being equal, debt is a downward force on that perception.That is a general point, although I'll stipulate it isn't a factor in AI picks because they are approximately all doing the same and because the market is giddy with FOMO.
sentences.zh: 准确地说，是市场对未来增长的看法。在所有其他条件相同的情况下，债务是这种看法的下行力量。这是一个普遍观点，尽管我会规定它不是人工智能选择的一个因素，因为它们几乎都在做同样的事情，而且因为市场对 FOMO 感到头晕目眩。

sentences.en: More strongly, i believe that some Schmanthropic with equivalent offering and unit cost and userbase but with sustainable finance would signal, through lack of recklessness, that it is not "going to the moon".
sentences.zh: 更强烈的是，我相信一些具有同等产品、单位成本和用户基础但具有可持续财务的 Schmanthropic 会通过不鲁莽发出信号，表明它不是“登月”。

sentences.en: I'm willing to call this "irrationality".
sentences.zh: 我愿意称之为“非理性”。

sentences.en: But hey, I'm not exposed, other than being a taxpayer with savings who will inevitably foot the bill for the bailouts.
sentences.zh: 但是，嘿，除了作为一个有积蓄的纳税人不可避免地要为救助买单之外，我并没有暴露在外。

blocks.html_en:
> These companies have valuations reflecting a debt light business.

Sorry, but this doesn’t make sense. The valuations of these companies reflect their growth.

In finance there’s nothing inherently virtuous about a “debt-light business”. It’s all an allocation decision based on how you expect to grow relative the cost of that growth.

Try and reframe it: are cash-heavy businesses given a premium?

sentences.en: > These companies have valuations reflecting a debt light business.Sorry, but this doesn’t make sense.
sentences.zh: > 这些公司的估值反映了轻负债业务。抱歉，但这没有意义。

sentences.en: The valuations of these companies reflect their growth.In finance there’s nothing inherently virtuous about a “debt-light business”.
sentences.zh: 这些公司的估值反映了它们的增长。 在金融领域，“轻负债企业”本身并没有什么优点。

sentences.en: It’s all an allocation decision based on how you expect to grow relative the cost of that growth.Try and reframe it: are cash-heavy businesses given a premium?
sentences.zh: 这都是基于您期望相对于增长成本如何增长的分配决策。尝试重新构建它：现金充裕的企业是否会获得溢价？

blocks.html_en:
Growth of what exactly? AI doesn't have the normal leverage factor that software usually does where a simple codebase can drive a billion dollars of subscription revenue with 90%+ gross margin. There's no eventual state where the capex is in place and the margins flip. They're in the datacenter business, which is real estate, with tenant improvements consisting of rapidly depreciating/obsoleting equipment. These margins have no path to flip around and allow for a huge amount of revenues to flow through. If they start testing price sensitivity in the way that would justify the valuations, it will just accelerate the transition of AI from datacenter to local.

>Experts continue to warn of an AI bubble, noting the enormous and widening gulf between company valuations and their comparatively measly profits

sentences.en: Growth of what exactly?
sentences.zh: 增长究竟是什么？

sentences.en: AI doesn't have the normal leverage factor that software usually does where a simple codebase can drive a billion dollars of subscription revenue with 90%+ gross margin.
sentences.zh: 人工智能不具有软件通常具有的正常杠杆因素，而软件通常可以通过简单的代码库带来 10 亿美元的订阅收入和 90% 以上的毛利率。

sentences.en: There's no eventual state where the capex is in place and the margins flip.
sentences.zh: 不存在资本支出到位且利润率发生翻转的最终状态。

sentences.en: They're in the datacenter business, which is real estate, with tenant improvements consisting of rapidly depreciating/obsoleting equipment.
sentences.zh: 他们从事数据中心业务，即房地产，租户的改进包括快速折旧/陈旧的设备。

sentences.en: These margins have no path to flip around and allow for a huge amount of revenues to flow through.
sentences.zh: 这些利润没有办法扭转，也没有办法让大量的收入流过。

sentences.en: If they start testing price sensitivity in the way that would justify the valuations, it will just accelerate the transition of AI from datacenter to local.>Experts continue to warn of an AI bubble, noting the enormous and widening gulf between company valuations and their comparatively measly profits
sentences.zh: 如果他们开始以证明估值合理的方式测试价格敏感性，那只会加速人工智能从数据中心向本地的转变。>专家们继续警告人工智能泡沫，指出公司估值与其相对微薄的利润之间存在巨大且不断扩大的鸿沟

blocks.html_en: I think your analysis assumes current closed SOTA models have no moat, which isn't a given considered the protectionism the Trump administration is already considering for US AI firms.

blocks.html_en:
It is a given. Unless the Trump admin figures out how to ban 1s and 0s, you can't stop Chinese labs from open sourcing their models. They can certainly ban new providers from using the Chinese models, but they can't ban Canadian ones, European ones, other Asian countries etc. from setting up provider proxies and bypassing those rules. Then what, great firewall of USA? Block off all internet traffic to other countries? Bye bye economy.

I think the burden of proof is on the closed SOTA model providers to prove they have a moat, because common sense indicates they don't.

blocks.html_en: While there may not be anything inherently bad about debt levels, the level of debt fundamentally affects stock price by the equation of total enterprise value = stock + debt - cash (i.e. total enterprise value is the amount someone would need to pay to buy all the outstanding equity AND take on or retire the outstanding debt). So if there is hidden debt that isn't being factored in by investors in means the stock price should go down because "how much a company is worth" (i.e total enterprise value) would remain the same, it's just now that more is debt and less is shareholder equity.

sentences.en: While there may not be anything inherently bad about debt levels, the level of debt fundamentally affects stock price by the equation of total enterprise value = stock + debt - cash (i.e. total enterprise value is the amount someone would need to pay to buy all the outstanding equity AND take on or retire the outstanding debt).
sentences.zh: 虽然债务水平本身可能没有什么坏处，但债务水平从根本上影响股票价格，其方程式为企业总价值=股票+债务-现金（即企业总价值是某人购买所有已发行股本并承担或偿还未偿债务所需支付的金额）。

sentences.en: So if there is hidden debt that isn't being factored in by investors in means the stock price should go down because "how much a company is worth" (i.e total enterprise value) would remain the same, it's just now that more is debt and less is shareholder equity.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果投资者没有考虑到隐性债务，这意味着股价应该下跌，因为“公司价值多少”（即企业总价值）将保持不变，那么现在债务更多，股东权益更少。

blocks.html_en: Debt always raises risks, it can be rational but an asset rich and debt free business is vastly less likely to suddenly fail which has a real impact on rational evaluation of the value of their stock.

> The valuations of these companies reflect their growth.

If you look at was happening around 2022 - 2023, their growth was slowing down. Lots of the new growth is based on AI but since almost everything AI based is subsidized to hell and back, we have no way of knowing yet if that AI demand is real at unsubsidized AI prices.

blocks.html_en:
This is a common objection, "It's already priced in". I've believed it for a long time, but also been really interested in the claim, since it feels recursive in the kind of suspicious way. Recently I read / watched a lot of material about it, just out of personal interest. In short, this claim (the efficient market hypothesis, "It's already priced in") is very optimistic. It's not possible to prove definitively if it is or isn't true, and people will argue both ways. However, it seems very unlikely that _all_ effects are _always_ already priced in. Some evidence by way of contradiction:

1. It's widely known that Spacex is overvalued. How is it possible for it to be widely known, and yet still overvalued? Either it is fairly valued, or these issues are _not_ already priced in.

2. If people read Enron's filings, they could have been aware of the shady reporting, and should have seen the reduction in price coming. But clearly most did not! Enron persisted for a long time despite shady tactics, that essentially were happening "in the open" if you dug into the paperwork.

3. The same argument can be made about housing loans during 2008, the dot com boom in 2000, and the response to covid in 2020

If you're interested I have much more to say on the topic! It's fascinating and I've only recently been convinced that the efficient market hypothesis is untrue (or at least it is suspect). I also highly recommend any of patrick boyle's videos, he makes great content often touching on this topic.

disclaimer: I'm not an expert in this industry, or really in this industry at all. I just like learning about it.

sentences.zh: 我已经相信它很长时间了，但也对这个说法非常感兴趣，因为它感觉以一种可疑的方式递归。

sentences.en: Recently I read / watched a lot of material about it, just out of personal interest.
sentences.zh: 最近，出于个人兴趣，我阅读/观看了很多有关它的材料。

sentences.en: In short, this claim (the efficient market hypothesis, "It's already priced in") is very optimistic.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，这种说法（有效市场假说，“它已经被定价”）是非常乐观的。

sentences.en: It's not possible to prove definitively if it is or isn't true, and people will argue both ways.
sentences.zh: 不可能明确证明它是否正确，人们会以两种方式争论。

sentences.en: However, it seems very unlikely that _all_ effects are _always_ already priced in.
sentences.zh: 然而，似乎不太可能所有影响都已被定价。

sentences.en: Some evidence by way of contradiction:1.
sentences.zh: 一些反证证据： 1．

sentences.en: It's widely known that Spacex is overvalued.
sentences.zh: 众所周知，SpaceX 的估值被高估了。

sentences.en: How is it possible for it to be widely known, and yet still overvalued?
sentences.zh: 它怎么可能广为人知，但仍然被高估呢？

sentences.en: Either it is fairly valued, or these issues are _not_ already priced in.2.
sentences.zh: 要么它的估值是合理的，要么这些问题尚未定价。2。

sentences.en: If people read Enron's filings, they could have been aware of the shady reporting, and should have seen the reduction in price coming.
sentences.zh: 如果人们阅读安然公司的文件，他们可能已经意识到这些可疑的报告，并且应该看到价格的下降。

sentences.en: But clearly most did not!
sentences.zh: 但显然大多数人没有！

sentences.en: Enron persisted for a long time despite shady tactics, that essentially were happening "in the open" if you dug into the paperwork.3.
sentences.zh: 尽管采取了不正当的策略，安然公司仍然坚持了很长一段时间，如果你深入研究文件，这基本上是在“公开”发生的。3。

sentences.en: The same argument can be made about housing loans during 2008, the dot com boom in 2000, and the response to covid in 2020If you're interested I have much more to say on the topic!
sentences.zh: 同样的论点也适用于 2008 年的住房贷款、2000 年的互联网繁荣以及 2020 年对新冠疫情的反应。如果你感兴趣，我对这个话题还有很多话要说！

sentences.en: It's fascinating and I've only recently been convinced that the efficient market hypothesis is untrue (or at least it is suspect).
sentences.zh: 这很有趣，我直到最近才确信有效市场假说是不真实的（或者至少是值得怀疑的）。

sentences.en: I also highly recommend any of patrick boyle's videos, he makes great content often touching on this topic.disclaimer: I'm not an expert in this industry, or really in this industry at all.
sentences.zh: 我还强烈推荐帕特里克·博伊尔的任何视频，他制作的精彩内容经常涉及这个主题。免责声明：我不是这个行业的专家，或者根本不是这个行业的专家。

sentences.en: I just like learning about it.
sentences.zh: 我只是喜欢了解它。

blocks.html_en: This is a common objection, "It's already priced in". I've believed it for a long time, but also been really interested in the claim, since it feels recursive in the kind of suspicious way. Recently I read / watched a lot of material about it, just out of personal interest. In short, this claim (the efficient market hypothesis, "It's already priced in") is very optimistic. It's not possible to prove definitively if it is or isn't true, and people will argue both ways. However, it seems very unlikely that _all_ effects are _always_ already priced in. Some evidence by way of contradiction:

blocks.html_en:
Which routed those funds through the economy's financial circuit and caused ZIRP, inflation in asset prices, and the evaporation of risk premiums.

But the pendulum swinging rapidly to the other side has routed all those funds through the real economy, caused goods inflation, and looks like it will crash the economy.

sentences.en: Which routed those funds through the economy's financial circuit and caused ZIRP, inflation in asset prices, and the evaporation of risk premiums.But the pendulum swinging rapidly to the other side has routed all those funds through the real economy, caused goods inflation, and looks like it will crash the economy.
sentences.zh: 这些资金流经经济的金融循环，导致零利率（ZIRP）、资产价格通胀和风险溢价蒸发。但钟摆迅速摆向另一侧，所有这些资金流经实体经济，导致商品通胀，看起来会导致经济崩溃。

blocks.html_en:
Bob spends $5 on Alice’s lemonade. Alice spends $1 on lemons from Jack. $15 on wood for the stand from Jill. Jack spends $5 on pesticide from Bob. Jill leases the plot for the tree from Jack for $10. Jack spends $5 on lemonade from Alice. Jill spends $10 on lemonade from Alice. Alice spends $3 on more lemons from Jack. Jack builds a stand to sell lemons with $20 in wood from Jill. Jill leases more land from Jack.

Take the circular money and add even more spend, even less profit, and somewhere in the background is a money tree that is about to die.

sentences.en: Bob spends $5 on Alice’s lemonade.
sentences.zh: 鲍勃花了 5 美元买了爱丽丝的柠檬水。

sentences.en: Alice spends $1 on lemons from Jack. $15 on wood for the stand from Jill.
sentences.zh: 爱丽丝花了 1 美元从杰克那里买了柠檬。吉尔提供的支架木料售价 15 美元。

sentences.en: Jack spends $5 on pesticide from Bob.
sentences.zh: 杰克花了 5 美元从鲍勃那里购买了农药。

sentences.en: Jill leases the plot for the tree from Jack for $10.
sentences.zh: 吉尔以 10 美元的价格从杰克那里租了这棵树的地块。

sentences.en: Jack spends $5 on lemonade from Alice.
sentences.zh: 杰克花了 5 美元从爱丽丝那里买了柠檬水。

sentences.en: Jill spends $10 on lemonade from Alice.
sentences.zh: 吉尔花了 10 美元从爱丽丝那里买了柠檬水。

sentences.en: Alice spends $3 on more lemons from Jack.
sentences.zh: 爱丽丝花了 3 美元从杰克那里购买了更多柠檬。

sentences.en: Jack builds a stand to sell lemons with $20 in wood from Jill.
sentences.zh: 杰克用吉尔提供的价值 20 美元的木材搭建了一个摊位来出售柠檬。

sentences.en: Jill leases more land from Jack.Take the circular money and add even more spend, even less profit, and somewhere in the background is a money tree that is about to die.
sentences.zh: 吉尔从杰克那里租了更多的土地。拿着循环钱，增加更多的支出，甚至更少的利润，背景中的某个地方有一棵即将枯死的摇钱树。

blocks.html_en:
There are many reasons to use subsidiaries for things like this, like to invite outside investment, ringfence risk, cede operational risk, and many more.

This is all like CFO 101 type stuff, and not nefarious. I find it amusing that people assume the worst for things they understand little about, rather than trying to learn.

Maybe the best way I can explain it to the programming crowd is this: imagine how ridiculous it would sound if outsiders were saying that Google was on the verge of collapse because its codebase has billions of lines of code.

sentences.en: There are many reasons to use subsidiaries for things like this, like to invite outside investment, ringfence risk, cede operational risk, and many more.This is all like CFO 101 type stuff, and not nefarious.
sentences.zh: 使用子公司来做这样的事情有很多原因，比如邀请外部投资、隔离风险、放弃运营风险等等。这一切都像 CFO 101 类型的东西，而不是邪恶的。

sentences.en: I find it amusing that people assume the worst for things they understand little about, rather than trying to learn.Maybe the best way I can explain it to the programming crowd is this: imagine how ridiculous it would sound if outsiders were saying that Google was on the verge of collapse because its codebase has billions of lines of code.
sentences.zh: 我觉得很有趣的是，人们对自己知之甚少的事情做出最坏的打算，而不是努力学习。也许我能向编程人群解释这一点的最好方式是这样的：想象一下，如果局外人说谷歌正处于崩溃的边缘，因为它的代码库有数十亿行代码，那听起来是多么荒谬。

blocks.html_en:
> I find it amusing that people assume the worst for things they understand little about, rather than trying to learn.

I don't know if you intended this to be exclusive of the opposite, but I do often find actually the opposite thing is true. That is people tend to be wildly, inappropriately optimistic for things they don't understand well and more likely to be skeptical in the details of things they do.

sentences.en: > I find it amusing that people assume the worst for things they understand little about, rather than trying to learn.I don't know if you intended this to be exclusive of the opposite, but I do often find actually the opposite thing is true.
sentences.zh: > 我觉得很有趣的是，人们对他们知之甚少的事情做出最坏的打算，而不是努力学习。我不知道你是否想排除相反的情况，但我确实经常发现实际上相反的事情是正确的。

sentences.en: That is people tend to be wildly, inappropriately optimistic for things they don't understand well and more likely to be skeptical in the details of things they do.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，人们往往对他们不太理解的事情抱有过度的、不恰当的乐观态度，并且更有可能对他们所做的事情的细节持怀疑态度。

blocks.html_en:
> There are many reasons to use subsidiaries for things like this, like to invite outside investment, ringfence risk, cede operational risk, and many more.

Agreed, there are many valid reasons to have subsidiaries of course.

The issue is rather with the fact that we are having the assumption that the threat is outside rather than inside and so systems with mechanisms to be less transparent are far more prone to this risk.

It has been academically shown that most corporate/ white paper scams aren't done from outside but rather from inside the company itself through genuine structures and incentives which go wild. (Something shockingly visible in AI space), Enron's example also comes to my mind.

The best way I can explain it to the programming crowd is this: Imagine how ridiculous it would sound if you are ranked with how many lines of code you ship and how much token you would spend and so we end up with tokenmaxxing and hearing stories about people literally burning tokens in innovative ways because they want to get on top of a leaderboard. Oh wait, it is already happening or has happened.

Generally speaking, It is preferable to be transparent with debt and other things rather than not especially so for long term because sooner rather than later you might get caught. Obviously if there is some stuff which prefers from ringfencing risk then sure.

Also as I spoke of Enron, but the exact structure was used by Enron as well as @fzeroracer discusses in their comment[0] so it might be a genuine question.

[0]: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49023596

sentences.en: > There are many reasons to use subsidiaries for things like this, like to invite outside investment, ringfence risk, cede operational risk, and many more.Agreed, there are many valid reasons to have subsidiaries of course.The issue is rather with the fact that we are having the assumption that the threat is outside rather than inside and so systems with mechanisms to be less transparent are far more prone to this risk.It has been academically shown that most corporate/ white paper scams aren't done from outside but rather from inside the company itself through genuine structures and incentives which go wild.
sentences.zh: > 使用子公司来做这样的事情有很多理由，比如邀请外部投资、隔离风险、放弃运营风险等等。同意，当然有很多正当的理由。问题在于我们假设威胁来自外部而不是内部，因此机制不太透明的系统更容易出现这种风险。学术上已经表明，大多数公司/白皮书骗局不是从外部进行的，而是通过真正的结构从公司内部进行的以及疯狂的激励措施。

sentences.en: (Something shockingly visible in AI space), Enron's example also comes to my mind.The best way I can explain it to the programming crowd is this: Imagine how ridiculous it would sound if you are ranked with how many lines of code you ship and how much token you would spend and so we end up with tokenmaxxing and hearing stories about people literally burning tokens in innovative ways because they want to get on top of a leaderboard.
sentences.zh: （人工智能领域中令人震惊的事情），安然的例子也浮现在我的脑海中。我可以向编程人群解释它的最好方式是：想象一下，如果你按照你发送的代码行数和你会花费多少代币来排名，那么这听起来是多么荒谬，所以我们最终会以 tokenmaxxing 的方式结束，并听到人们以创新的方式燃烧代币的故事，因为他们想登上排行榜的榜首。

sentences.en: Oh wait, it is already happening or has happened.Generally speaking, It is preferable to be transparent with debt and other things rather than not especially so for long term because sooner rather than later you might get caught.
sentences.zh: 哦等等，它已经发生或已经发生了。一般来说，最好对债务和其他事情保持透明，而不是长期不这样做，因为迟早你可能会被抓住。

sentences.en: Obviously if there is some stuff which prefers from ringfencing risk then sure.Also as I spoke of Enron, but the exact structure was used by Enron as well as @fzeroracer discusses in their comment[0] so it might be a genuine question.[0]: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49023596
sentences.zh: 显然，如果有一些东西更喜欢规避风险，那么当然。正如我谈到安然一样，但安然使用了确切的结构以及@fzeroracer在他们的评论中讨论[0]，所以这可能是一个真正的问题。[0]：https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49023596

blocks.html_en:
"There are many reasons to use subsidiaries for things like this, like to invite outside investment, ringfence risk, cede operational risk,..."

In other words, they are intentionally deceiving investors and hiding the risk from them. That does not sound like CFO 101, it sounds like fraud. But if grift is your business, I guess those are as valid reasons as any.

blocks.html_en:
>but may make it difficult for retail investors to recognize risks

Ok, so just to be clear: institutional investors are (a) the ones investing the large proportion of capital in these companies and (b) are well equipped to decipher financial statements. The idea that any significant amount of retail investors have even seen a financial statement, let alone is making decisions based on their analysis of a financial statement, is laughable. And, even then, if someone is putting in that effort, then presumably they're not going to get tripped up by a legitimate practice that they ought to specifically be looking for given the context.

And all of that doesn't even take into account that every article discussing the financials of AI firms over the last half a decade have been pointing out these dynamics. We're literally in a thread discussing this exact dynamic. Retail investors are certainly far more likely to make investing decisions based on these kinds of articles and threads than they are based solely on independent financial statement analysis that they're conducting. At a minimum, I think anybody taking any of this seriously has gotten the hint by now.

If it comes out that these firms are committing straight-up fraud, then there will be a lot more to discuss. But, as of now, the sentiment is that these firms are behaving perfectly legitimately, just abnormally and maybe irresponsibly compared to their historical context. If an investor isn't equipped to handle this kind of analysis under these circumstances, then I'm not going to feel too bad if they lose their money "investing" when they're really just gambling.

blocks.html_en: Whether the datacenters will end up profitable is a different question than whether they are a real risk for the business. This whole thing could be a massive mistake and all the big tech companies will come out the other end just fine. I think the executives at these companies realize that, this whole build out is a massive case of FOMO. No one wants to be microsoft missing the boat on mobile and theyre willing to flush money away to ensure that never happens.

sentences.en: Whether the datacenters will end up profitable is a different question than whether they are a real risk for the business.
sentences.zh: 数据中心最终是否会盈利是一个不同的问题，而不是它们是否会给企业带来真正的风险。

sentences.en: This whole thing could be a massive mistake and all the big tech companies will come out the other end just fine.
sentences.zh: 整件事可能是一个巨大的错误，而所有大型科技公司都会顺利地走出困境。

sentences.en: I think the executives at these companies realize that, this whole build out is a massive case of FOMO.
sentences.zh: 我认为这些公司的高管意识到，整个建设是一个巨大的 FOMO 案例。

sentences.en: No one wants to be microsoft missing the boat on mobile and theyre willing to flush money away to ensure that never happens.
sentences.zh: 没有人希望微软在移动领域错失良机，他们愿意砸钱以确保这种情况永远不会发生。

blocks.html_en: Are they really "trying to hide" this debt? I think it's pretty common knowledge that a lot of these companies are using debt/bonds for funding. The debt not showing up where the author wants is a reporting formality not an attempt to hide it.

sentences.en: Are they really "trying to hide" this debt?
sentences.zh: 他们真的是在“试图隐藏”这笔债务吗？

sentences.en: I think it's pretty common knowledge that a lot of these companies are using debt/bonds for funding.
sentences.zh: 我认为，众所周知，许多公司都在使用债务/债券进行融资。

sentences.en: The debt not showing up where the author wants is a reporting formality not an attempt to hide it.
sentences.zh: 债务没有出现在作者想要的地方是一种报告形式，而不是试图隐藏它。

blocks.html_en: I think the point is that it’s not showing up on the standard financial filings. If you were to pull the annual reports for these companies, you wouldn’t see it. That doesn’t mean it’s impossible to find it. Obviously, it is otherwise the article wouldn’t have been written. But you’re going to have to go the extra mile. To be clear, none of this is illegal. It’s just covered in the advanced CFO accounting class.

sentences.en: If you were to pull the annual reports for these companies, you wouldn’t see it.
sentences.zh: 如果你提取这些公司的年度报告，你将看不到它。

sentences.en: That doesn’t mean it’s impossible to find it.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着不可能找到它。

sentences.en: Obviously, it is otherwise the article wouldn’t have been written.
sentences.zh: 显然，否则这篇文章就不会写出来。

sentences.en: But you’re going to have to go the extra mile.
sentences.zh: 但你必须加倍努力。

sentences.en: To be clear, none of this is illegal.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，这些都不违法。

sentences.en: It’s just covered in the advanced CFO accounting class.
sentences.zh: 高级 CFO 会计课程刚刚涵盖了这一点。

blocks.html_en: It's not hidden at all. Financial blogs very accessible to laymen like Matt Levine's Money Stuff have talked about this structure months ago. If you are an investor and surprised by this news you weren't sufficiently prepared and shouldn't have been investing in the first place.

sentences.en: It's not hidden at all.
sentences.zh: 它根本没有隐藏。

sentences.en: Financial blogs very accessible to laymen like Matt Levine's Money Stuff have talked about this structure months ago.
sentences.zh: 像 Matt Levine 的 Money Stuff 这样的外行人很容易接触到的金融博客几个月前就已经讨论过这种结构。

sentences.en: If you are an investor and surprised by this news you weren't sufficiently prepared and shouldn't have been investing in the first place.
sentences.zh: 如果您是一名投资者并对这一消息感到惊讶，那么您就没有做好充分的准备，因此一开始就不应该投资。

blocks.html_en:
I'm with you inasmuch as the frothiness is, while massaged a bit, sufficiently perceptible for emptors to caveat.

My concern is that the subprime mortgages were not perceptible to even savvy fund managers, and the same thing could happen again; and also, even if that insidious risk-poisoning of institutional funds does not occur, them a collapsing bubble still fucks us all into recession.

sentences.en: I'm with you inasmuch as the frothiness is, while massaged a bit, sufficiently perceptible for emptors to caveat.My concern is that the subprime mortgages were not perceptible to even savvy fund managers, and the same thing could happen again; and also, even if that insidious risk-poisoning of institutional funds does not occur, them a collapsing bubble still fucks us all into recession.
sentences.zh: 我同意你的观点，因为泡沫虽然经过了一些调整，但足够容易被买家察觉，从而让买家发出警告。我担心的是，即使是精明的基金经理也无法察觉次级抵押贷款，而且同样的事情可能会再次发生；而且，即使机构资金的潜在风险中毒没有发生，泡沫破灭仍然会让我们所有人陷入衰退。

sentences.zh: 我同意你的观点，因为泡沫虽然经过了一些调整，但足够容易被买家察觉，从而让买家发出警告。我担心的是，即使是精明的基金经理也无法察觉次级抵押贷款，而且同样的事情可能会再次发生；而且，即使机构资金的潜在风险中毒没有发生，泡沫破灭仍然会让我们所有人陷入衰退。

blocks.html_en:
A legitimate reason to do this is so that you can sell it all off or restructure it later without it being intertwined with your main books. So, for instance, it you’re an AI company, your main business is developing models and selling inference, but you need data centers to do that. You could buy those data centers yourself, or you could create a data center subsidiary that would take on debt and build your data centers and then rent them back to you. In the future, if you decide you don’t need that capability any longer, you just sell the subsidiary and you don’t have to tease apart the P&L and personnel to do it. It’s clean and separate because it was structured that way up front.

Now, that’s not to say that there aren’t other benefits of having a separate balance sheet related to moving numbers around. But it doesn’t have to be nefarious.

sentences.en: A legitimate reason to do this is so that you can sell it all off or restructure it later without it being intertwined with your main books.
sentences.zh: 这样做的一个合理原因是，您可以将其全部出售或稍后重组，而不会与您的主要书籍交织在一起。

sentences.en: You could buy those data centers yourself, or you could create a data center subsidiary that would take on debt and build your data centers and then rent them back to you.
sentences.zh: 您可以自己购买这些数据中心，也可以创建一个数据中心子公司，该子公司将承担债务并建造您的数据中心，然后将其租回给您。

sentences.en: In the future, if you decide you don’t need that capability any longer, you just sell the subsidiary and you don’t have to tease apart the P&L and personnel to do it.
sentences.zh: 将来，如果您决定不再需要这种能力，您只需出售子公司即可，而无需梳理损益表和人员来做到这一点。

sentences.en: It’s clean and separate because it was structured that way up front.Now, that’s not to say that there aren’t other benefits of having a separate balance sheet related to moving numbers around.
sentences.zh: 它是干净且独立的，因为它是预先构建的。现在，这并不是说拥有与移动数字相关的单独资产负债表没有其他好处。

sentences.en: But it doesn’t have to be nefarious.
sentences.zh: 但它不一定是邪恶的。

blocks.html_en:
If you open the annual report and look at the numbers they’re citing, they’re adding lease payments from uncommenced leases and future purchase commitments. Accounting rules prohibit either of these as being listed as debt. It’s also not possible to agree to a future lease without reporting it like this.

Generally speaking after commencement the former will become both liabilities (for a smaller amount than the number they listed due to discounting) and an asset (representing the asset of the right to occupy the lease)

The latter will generally become some sort of asset

I have no idea what the people going on about subsidiaries could even possibly be referring to as it relates to this article, this is just lease accounting

sentences.en: If you open the annual report and look at the numbers they’re citing, they’re adding lease payments from uncommenced leases and future purchase commitments.
sentences.zh: 如果你打开年度报告并查看他们引用的数字，他们会添加未开始的租赁和未来购买承诺的租赁付款。

sentences.en: Accounting rules prohibit either of these as being listed as debt.
sentences.zh: 会计规则禁止将其中任何一项列为债务。

sentences.en: It’s also not possible to agree to a future lease without reporting it like this.Generally speaking after commencement the former will become both liabilities (for a smaller amount than the number they listed due to discounting) and an asset (representing the asset of the right to occupy the lease)The latter will generally become some sort of assetI have no idea what the people going on about subsidiaries could even possibly be referring to as it relates to this article, this is just lease accounting
sentences.zh: 也不可能在不这样报告的情况下同意未来的租赁。一般来说，在开始之后，前者将成为负债（由于贴现，金额比他们列出的数字要小）和资产（代表占用租赁权的资产）后者通常会成为某种资产我不知道人们对子公司的看法与本文有关，这只是租赁会计

blocks.html_en: The structures theyre using reduce risk. The 2 big moves here are 1. agreeing to lease a datacenter from developer instead of building it yourself and 2. using subsidiaries to finance and own the datacenters. Both these moves exist so that the company isnt left holding the bag if something goes wrong with the construction. They dont need to worry about zoning and construction because their partners are doing that. This also means the companies dont need to take out debt themselves, since these agreements dont count.

sentences.en: The structures theyre using reduce risk.
sentences.zh: 他们使用的结构可以降低风险。

sentences.en: The 2 big moves here are 1.
sentences.zh: 这里的 2 个大动作是 1。

sentences.en: agreeing to lease a datacenter from developer instead of building it yourself and 2.
sentences.zh: 同意从开发商那里租用数据中心而不是自己建造 2.

sentences.en: using subsidiaries to finance and own the datacenters.
sentences.zh: 利用子公司资助并拥有数据中心。

sentences.en: Both these moves exist so that the company isnt left holding the bag if something goes wrong with the construction.
sentences.zh: 这两项举措的存在是为了确保如果施工出现问题，公司就不会承担责任。

sentences.en: They dont need to worry about zoning and construction because their partners are doing that.
sentences.zh: 他们不需要担心分区和建设，因为他们的合作伙伴正在这样做。

sentences.en: This also means the companies dont need to take out debt themselves, since these agreements dont count.
sentences.zh: 这也意味着公司不需要自己偿还债务，因为这些协议不算数。

blocks.html_en: Take-or-pay contracts appear as "contractual commitments" in 10-K. They are not hidden. That's the way they are reported in all industries where take-or-pay contracts exist. There's nothing nefarious about it.

blocks.html_en: Yes, the contractual obligation shows up, but not the debt the subsidiaries took on. You have to follow the entity structure and look at their books for that piece. And it’ll show up as a different accounting category, “services rendered,” for instance, not as debt repayments.

sentences.en: Yes, the contractual obligation shows up, but not the debt the subsidiaries took on.
sentences.zh: 是的，合同义务出现了，但子公司承担的债务却没有出现。

sentences.en: You have to follow the entity structure and look at their books for that piece.
sentences.zh: 您必须遵循实体结构并查看他们的书籍以了解该内容。

sentences.en: And it’ll show up as a different accounting category, “services rendered,” for instance, not as debt repayments.
sentences.zh: 它将显示为不同的会计类别，例如“提供的服务”，而不是债务偿还。

sentences.en: And it’ll show up as a different accounting category, “services rendered,” for instance, not as debt repayments.
sentences.zh: 它将显示为不同的会计类别，例如“提供的服务”，而不是债务偿还。

blocks.html_en: I can't assess if it's illegal, and it sounds like typical structuring for high-capital assets. But it also somewhat subvert the spirit of requiring financial filings for public companies in the first place. Investment markets are built on the principle that every investor should have the same public understanding on material matters to the business - and large expenditures or lease committments should really make the disclosure. If nothing else there is a self-dealing/self-enrichment of principals risk and watering down of investment if something underhanded is going on with these deals - as has happened in the past (e.g. WeWork, reportedly some cases in Amzon too)

sentences.en: I can't assess if it's illegal, and it sounds like typical structuring for high-capital assets.
sentences.zh: 我无法评估这是否违法，而且这听起来像是典型的高资本资产结构。

sentences.en: But it also somewhat subvert the spirit of requiring financial filings for public companies in the first place.
sentences.zh: 但这也在一定程度上颠覆了要求上市公司进行财务申报的初衷。

sentences.en: Investment markets are built on the principle that every investor should have the same public understanding on material matters to the business - and large expenditures or lease committments should really make the disclosure.
sentences.zh: 投资市场建立的原则是，每个投资者都应该对企业的重大事项有相同的公众理解，并且大额支出或租赁承诺应该真正进行披露。

sentences.en: If nothing else there is a self-dealing/self-enrichment of principals risk and watering down of investment if something underhanded is going on with these deals - as has happened in the past (e.g. WeWork, reportedly some cases in Amzon too)
sentences.zh: 如果不出意外的话，如果这些交易中存在不正当行为，就会出现自我交易/自我致富的本金风险，并削弱投资——就像过去发生的那样（例如 WeWork，据报道亚马逊也有一些案例）

blocks.html_en:
If it didn't matter, why would they bother jumping through hoops to keep the debt off their balance sheet?

In the run-up to 2008 a big factor in the bubble forming was that poor quality loans were packaged in a way to hide the risk in those investments. I'm not expert enough in finance to know if it's the case now, but we do know that clever accounting to hide debt can lead to the incorrect valuation of assets, potentially leading to financial ruin.

sentences.en: If it didn't matter, why would they bother jumping through hoops to keep the debt off their balance sheet?In the run-up to 2008 a big factor in the bubble forming was that poor quality loans were packaged in a way to hide the risk in those investments.
sentences.zh: 如果这不重要，他们为什么要费尽心思把债务从资产负债表上剔除呢？在2008年之前，泡沫形成的一个重要因素是低质量贷款的包装方式是为了隐藏这些投资的风险。

sentences.en: I'm not expert enough in finance to know if it's the case now, but we do know that clever accounting to hide debt can lead to the incorrect valuation of assets, potentially leading to financial ruin.
sentences.zh: 我在金融方面不够专业，不知道现在情况是否如此，但我们确实知道，隐藏债务的巧妙会计可能会导致资产估值不正确，从而可能导致财务破产。

blocks.html_en:
They're not jumping through any hoops, I think they're simply complying with reporting requirements. It's not on their balance sheet because being recorded as strait debt would itself be misleading. My understanding is that these sort of off-balance sheet "debt" is mostly in the form of deal terms that may or may not be expressed at some point in the future.

An analogy that comes to mind is when companies used to book future sales in the present. They got in trouble for this and is now forbidden. I recall reading that one deal had terms that transferred assets if certain conditions were not met. If terms-based debt should be booked now, then terms-based assets should as well. This stuff makes my head hurt.

Either way, as long as it's not hidden (and it's not for the public companies), then it's fine.

sentences.en: They're not jumping through any hoops, I think they're simply complying with reporting requirements.
sentences.zh: 他们没有跳过任何障碍，我认为他们只是遵守报告要求。

sentences.en: It's not on their balance sheet because being recorded as strait debt would itself be misleading.
sentences.zh: 它不在他们的资产负债表上，因为被记录为海峡债务本身就会产生误导。

sentences.en: My understanding is that these sort of off-balance sheet "debt" is mostly in the form of deal terms that may or may not be expressed at some point in the future.An analogy that comes to mind is when companies used to book future sales in the present.
sentences.zh: 我的理解是，这类表外“债务”主要以交易条款的形式出现，这些条款可能会也可能不会在未来的某个时候表达。我想到的一个类比是，公司过去常常在当前登记未来销售。

sentences.en: They got in trouble for this and is now forbidden.
sentences.zh: 他们因此惹上了麻烦，现在被禁止了。

sentences.en: I recall reading that one deal had terms that transferred assets if certain conditions were not met.
sentences.zh: 我记得读到过一项交易有条款，如果某些条件不满足，则转移资产。

sentences.en: If terms-based debt should be booked now, then terms-based assets should as well.
sentences.zh: 如果现在应该登记基于期限的债务，那么也应该登记基于期限的资产。

sentences.en: This stuff makes my head hurt.Either way, as long as it's not hidden (and it's not for the public companies), then it's fine.
sentences.zh: 这东西让我头疼。不管怎样，只要不是隐藏的（而且不是针对上市公司的），那就没问题。

blocks.html_en: Because this isnt actaully debt. Almost all of it is agreements to pay for completed datacenters from developers. Its just a way to offload operational risk when constructing datacenters. If the construction somehow fails theyre not stuck with the bill.

sentences.en: Because this isnt actaully debt.
sentences.zh: 因为这实际上并不是债务。

sentences.en: Almost all of it is agreements to pay for completed datacenters from developers.
sentences.zh: 几乎所有这些都是开发商为已建成的数据中心支付费用的协议。

sentences.en: Its just a way to offload operational risk when constructing datacenters.
sentences.zh: 这只是构建数据中心时减轻运营风险的一种方法。

sentences.en: If the construction somehow fails theyre not stuck with the bill.
sentences.zh: 如果施工因某种原因失败，他们就不会被账单困扰。

> If the construction somehow fails theyre not stuck with the bill.

Who is stuck with that bill and what is their ultimately their primary source of financing?
