blocks.html_en:
> The debt not showing up where the author wants is a reporting formality not an attempt to hide it.

Couldn't you characterize Enron that way? The liabilities are there, you "just" have to look at Raptor II or whatever!

blocks.html_en: You couldn't just characterize them that way, they are using literally the exact same setup that Enron did in order to hide the massive amount of debt and liabilities they had that eventually sunk the company. People jumping in to defend this as being totally legit or not-fraud seem quite insane to me: companies should not be trying to hide these things to pump up their stock prices or to entice investors that otherwise might not see it.

sentences.en: You couldn't just characterize them that way, they are using literally the exact same setup that Enron did in order to hide the massive amount of debt and liabilities they had that eventually sunk the company.
sentences.zh: 你不能仅仅这样描述他们，他们实际上使用了与安然公司完全相同的设置，以隐藏他们所拥有的巨额债务和负债，最终使公司陷入困境。

sentences.en: People jumping in to defend this as being totally legit or not-fraud seem quite insane to me: companies should not be trying to hide these things to pump up their stock prices or to entice investors that otherwise might not see it.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，人们站出来捍卫这一点是完全合法或非欺诈的，这似乎很疯狂：公司不应该试图隐藏这些事情来抬高股价或吸引那些可能看不到的投资者。

blocks.html_en:
It's an interesting counter to the efficient market hypothesis. "Everybody" knows about this debt. It's in the most public news outlets there are, and the word has been getting around. It's about as secret as Taylor Swift's concert schedules. Any serious investor knows about this debt.

And yet... the companies do this because it works. If they held this debt on balance sheet, the sensible assumption is that their stock values would take a rather substantial hit, and they could face other sorts of scrutiny. It works like pull-in sales works. It works like channel stuffing works. It works even when everybody knows that's what's happening. It works even when everybody knows that everybody knows that's what's happening.

There's something broken here. In an era where AIs move millions upon millions of dollars around because of some blip of a headline somewhere and every AI improvement of any kind is immediately scrutinized for its ability to be used by the financial system, it is completely incredible that the system doesn't know and react to these things. I'm not sure what's broken. My first best guess would be the increasingly mindless investment via index funds in pensions and the slow-but-ever-increasing ability of financial engineering to abuse that mindless investment, but I call that a "guess" for a reason. Possibly there's still a lot of really stupid AIs hooked up to the stock market that just look at the most basic of numbers and are easily fooled by this? But who is running such a precise combination of "huge" and "stupid" on the market? I dunno. Something's weird here.

sentences.zh: 这是对有效市场假说的有趣反驳。

sentences.en: It works like pull-in sales works.
sentences.zh: 它的工作原理就像拉动销售一样。

sentences.en: In an era where AIs move millions upon millions of dollars around because of some blip of a headline somewhere and every AI improvement of any kind is immediately scrutinized for its ability to be used by the financial system, it is completely incredible that the system doesn't know and react to these things.
sentences.zh: 在这个时代，人工智能因为某处的某个头条新闻而转移了数百万美元，并且任何类型的人工智能改进都会立即受到金融系统使用的能力的审查，系统不知道这些事情并对这些事情做出反应是完全令人难以置信的。

sentences.en: I'm not sure what's broken.
sentences.zh: 我不确定什么坏了。

sentences.en: My first best guess would be the increasingly mindless investment via index funds in pensions and the slow-but-ever-increasing ability of financial engineering to abuse that mindless investment, but I call that a "guess" for a reason.

sentences.en: Possibly there's still a lot of really stupid AIs hooked up to the stock market that just look at the most basic of numbers and are easily fooled by this?
sentences.zh: 也许仍然有很多非常愚蠢的人工智能与股票市场挂钩，他们只看最基本的数字，很容易被这个愚弄？

sentences.en: But who is running such a precise combination of "huge" and "stupid" on the market?
sentences.zh: 但谁在市场上运营着如此精准的“庞大”与“愚蠢”组合呢？

sentences.en: I dunno.
sentences.zh: 我不知道。

sentences.en: Something's weird here.
sentences.zh: 这里有些奇怪。

blocks.html_en:
> In an era where AIs move millions upon millions of dollars around because of some blip of a headline somewhere [...] I'm not sure what's broken.

I think there is some sort of collective collusion. Like a school of fish moving. If there is an external stimuli the price can rise or fall eventhough it doesn't make sense from a 10y dividend perspective.

blocks.html_en:
I am repeating myself: at least a partial solution is enforcing anti-dumping laws, that is, make money losing subscriptions illegal. If businesses and individuals pay actual token costs, then they will naturally only use tokens in ways that make business sense.

Usually I applaud optimism, but the current approach in the USA will probably adversely affect most non-rich people, and I would like to see more public well grounded in reality pessimism.

sentences.en: I am repeating myself: at least a partial solution is enforcing anti-dumping laws, that is, make money losing subscriptions illegal.
sentences.zh: 我重复一遍：至少部分解决方案是执行反倾销法，即通过亏损订阅来赚钱。

sentences.en: If businesses and individuals pay actual token costs, then they will naturally only use tokens in ways that make business sense.Usually I applaud optimism, but the current approach in the USA will probably adversely affect most non-rich people, and I would like to see more public well grounded in reality pessimism.
sentences.zh: 如果企业和个人支付实际的代币成本，那么他们自然只会以具有商业意义的方式使用代币。通常我会赞扬乐观情绪，但美国目前的做法可能会对大多数非富人产生不利影响，我希望看到更多公众扎根于现实的悲观情绪。

blocks.html_en:
yeah my read is that this industry was for a long time underlevered; these companies were generating so much cash that the only meaningful candidates for investment were in effect moonshots with highly unpredictable returns, and you can't fund those bets with debt.

but now the story is different. while the effects of ai on other industries and classes may be unpredictable, (a) the tendency toward ai itself (the market demand for economically useful intelligence) seems plausibly more inevitable than any tendency in the history of capitalism (certainly more inevitable than any in the history of these big tech companies) and (b) the technical scaling laws have been eerily steady (intelligence as log of compute). taken together, (a) and (b) make it much easier to finance than anything meta or google or microsoft have ever worked on. there are risks, but there is at least also a model, a projection; that model did not formerly exist, for these companies. anything outside their core business was literally a guess.

the upshot of these stabilizing patterns is that the industry is in a certain sense just maturing. that is, its financial profile is starting to look more like other mature industries that are mostly juggling around known quantities to try to get a small edge that they can, with financial leverage, magnify enough to M&A the competition away and thereby secure the only relief possible in a well-delineated, well-populated niche: monopoly by scale/consolidation rather than by differentiation. (which is not to say that these mature industries are less competitive! they are actually more competitive; the intensity of the competition is what drives the "anti-competitive" behavior.)

sentences.en: yeah my read is that this industry was for a long time underlevered; these companies were generating so much cash that the only meaningful candidates for investment were in effect moonshots with highly unpredictable returns, and you can't fund those bets with debt.but now the story is different.
sentences.zh: 是的，我的理解是这个行业长期以来杠杆率不足；这些公司产生了如此多的现金，以至于唯一有意义的投资候选者实际上是回报高度不可预测的登月计划，而且你不能用债务为这些赌注提供资金。但现在情况不同了。

sentences.en: while the effects of ai on other industries and classes may be unpredictable, (a) the tendency toward ai itself (the market demand for economically useful intelligence) seems plausibly more inevitable than any tendency in the history of capitalism (certainly more inevitable than any in the history of these big tech companies) and (b) the technical scaling laws have been eerily steady (intelligence as log of compute).
sentences.zh: 虽然人工智能对其他行业和阶级的影响可能是不可预测的，但（a）人工智能本身的趋势（对经济上有用的智能的市场需求）似乎比资本主义历史上的任何趋势都更不可避免（当然比这些大型科技公司历史上的任何趋势都更不可避免），以及（b）技术规模法则一直出奇地稳定（智能作为计算的日志）。

sentences.en: taken together, (a) and (b) make it much easier to finance than anything meta or google or microsoft have ever worked on.
sentences.zh: 总而言之，（a）和（b）使得融资比元、谷歌或微软曾经做过的任何事情都容易得多。

sentences.en: there are risks, but there is at least also a model, a projection; that model did not formerly exist, for these companies.
sentences.zh: 有风险，但至少还有一个模型、一个预测；对于这些公司来说，这种模式以前并不存在。

sentences.en: anything outside their core business was literally a guess.the upshot of these stabilizing patterns is that the industry is in a certain sense just maturing.
sentences.zh: 核心业务之外的任何事情实际上都是猜测。这些稳定模式的结果是，该行业在某种意义上刚刚走向成熟。

sentences.en: that is, its financial profile is starting to look more like other mature industries that are mostly juggling around known quantities to try to get a small edge that they can, with financial leverage, magnify enough to M&A the competition away and thereby secure the only relief possible in a well-delineated, well-populated niche: monopoly by scale/consolidation rather than by differentiation.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，它的财务状况开始变得更像其他成熟的行业，这些行业大多在已知的数量上进行权衡，试图获得一点优势，利用财务杠杆，放大到足以并购竞争对手，从而确保在一个明确界定的、人口众多的利基市场中唯一可能的救济：通过规模/整合而不是通过差异化实现垄断。

sentences.en: (which is not to say that these mature industries are less competitive!
sentences.zh: （这并不是说这些成熟的行业竞争力较差！

sentences.en: they are actually more competitive; the intensity of the competition is what drives the "anti-competitive" behavior.)
sentences.zh: 他们实际上更有竞争力；竞争的强度是“反竞争”行为的驱动力。）

blocks.html_en: the upshot of these stabilizing patterns is that the industry is in a certain sense just maturing. that is, its financial profile is starting to look more like other mature industries that are mostly juggling around known quantities to try to get a small edge that they can, with financial leverage, magnify enough to M&A the competition away and thereby secure the only relief possible in a well-delineated, well-populated niche: monopoly by scale/consolidation rather than by differentiation. (which is not to say that these mature industries are less competitive! they are actually more competitive; the intensity of the competition is what drives the "anti-competitive" behavior.)

sentences.en: the upshot of these stabilizing patterns is that the industry is in a certain sense just maturing.
sentences.zh: 这些稳定模式的结果是，该行业在某种意义上刚刚走向成熟。

sentences.en: that is, its financial profile is starting to look more like other mature industries that are mostly juggling around known quantities to try to get a small edge that they can, with financial leverage, magnify enough to M&A the competition away and thereby secure the only relief possible in a well-delineated, well-populated niche: monopoly by scale/consolidation rather than by differentiation.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，它的财务状况开始变得更像其他成熟的行业，这些行业大多在已知的数量上进行权衡，试图获得一点优势，利用财务杠杆，放大到足以并购竞争对手，从而确保在一个明确界定的、人口众多的利基市场中唯一可能的救济：通过规模/整合而不是通过差异化实现垄断。

sentences.en: (which is not to say that these mature industries are less competitive!
sentences.zh: （这并不是说这些成熟的行业竞争力较差！

sentences.en: they are actually more competitive; the intensity of the competition is what drives the "anti-competitive" behavior.)
sentences.zh: 他们实际上更有竞争力；竞争的强度是“反竞争”行为的驱动力。）

blocks.html_en:
Meta making 60B a year doesn't make this any less scary.

If Meta put every cent towards their debt, it would take at least 6 years, assuming 0 interest and 0 new debt. The interest alone in the next 5-10 years likely would push it close to 1T debt.

How on earth are they meant to pay for this?

sentences.en: Meta making 60B a year doesn't make this any less scary.If Meta put every cent towards their debt, it would take at least 6 years, assuming 0 interest and 0 new debt.

sentences.en: The interest alone in the next 5-10 years likely would push it close to 1T debt.How on earth are they meant to pay for this?
sentences.zh: 仅未来 5-10 年的利息就可能使其债务接近 1T。他们到底要如何支付这笔费用？

blocks.html_en:
If I were nearing retirement and had a decent pension pot where I could control it in fine detail...I would be diversifying away from tech stocks and holding some cash for immediate needs. There probably won't be much time when it unravels...I wouldn't be over exposed to the Nasdaq 100, for instance. Although you could probably pick some AI safe companies out of it.

The real problem will be figuring out where all this debt is

blocks.html_en:
The problem is 1) the Nasdaq 100 is where the majority of gains are coming from, and 2) it very well might be another 3+ years before anything unravels, if it unravels at all.

If you're truly at retirement, absolutely cycle out. But if you're still young and trying to maximize portfolio growth, it's not obvious that a non-tech strategy would yield better returns.

sentences.en: The problem is 1) the Nasdaq 100 is where the majority of gains are coming from, and 2) it very well might be another 3+ years before anything unravels, if it unravels at all.If you're truly at retirement, absolutely cycle out.
sentences.zh: 问题是 1) 纳斯达克 100 指数是大部分收益的来源，2) 很可能还需要 3 年多的时间才会出现任何问题（如果真的出现问题的话）。如果你真的要退休了，绝对要退出。

sentences.en: But if you're still young and trying to maximize portfolio growth, it's not obvious that a non-tech strategy would yield better returns.
sentences.zh: 但如果你还年轻并试图最大化投资组合增长，那么非技术策略能否产生更好的回报并不明显。

blocks.html_en:
Article appears to be conflating big tech companies that print money with AI startups like OpenAI and Anthropic.

After the opening paragraphs about the accounting practices of meta, Microsoft, alphabet, etc - which, it should be noted are not “houses of cards” and earn plenty of money - the article quietly transitions to

> Experts continue to warn of an AI bubble, noting the enormous and widening gulf between company valuations and their comparatively measly profits.

I think hoping people will apply the “house of cards” logic by that analyst they quoted to the startups, when instead the analyst was talking about the megacorps’ accounting.

blocks.html_en:
Really feels like the govt + industry, through protectionism and fear-mongering, are propping up a "Too big to fail" situation.

Long term, I think the best thing the economy could do is to make training on model outputs fair-use, as suggested by Ben Thompson[1]. Short of that, the companies should enter into distillation agreements with other US labs to let them make near-Fable models.

As it stands now, the companies want to hold all the upside. While also being culturally so safety focused - "only we have the right to regulate this" that its IMO counterproductive to US leadership in AI.

A different universe where X.ai, Meta, and everyone were also building Fable competitive open weights models - because they can distill - would probably be better for the US long term. But there's too much capital on the line right now behind OpenAI / Anthropic for them to do this.

They're really in a bind IMO.

1 - http://stratechery.com/2026/whos-afraid-of-chinese-models/

sentences.en: Really feels like the govt + industry, through protectionism and fear-mongering, are propping up a "Too big to fail" situation.Long term, I think the best thing the economy could do is to make training on model outputs fair-use, as suggested by Ben Thompson[1].
sentences.zh: 确实感觉政府+行业通过保护主义和散布恐惧，正在支撑“太大而不能倒”的局面。从长远来看，我认为经济可以做的最好的事情就是使模型输出的培训合理使用，正如本·汤普森[1]所建议的那样。

sentences.en: Short of that, the companies should enter into distillation agreements with other US labs to let them make near-Fable models.As it stands now, the companies want to hold all the upside.
sentences.zh: 除此之外，这些公司应该与其他美国实验室签订蒸馏协议，让他们制造出近乎神话般的模型。就目前情况而言，这些公司希望掌握所有优势。

sentences.en: While also being culturally so safety focused - "only we have the right to regulate this" that its IMO counterproductive to US leadership in AI.A different universe where X.ai, Meta, and everyone were also building Fable competitive open weights models - because they can distill - would probably be better for the US long term.
sentences.zh: 同时在文化上也非常注重安全——“只有我们有权对此进行监管”，以至于在我看来，它对美国在人工智能领域的领导地位产生了反作用。在一个不同的宇宙中，X.ai、Meta 和每个人都在构建《神鬼寓言》竞争性开放权重模型——因为它们可以提炼——从长远来看可能对美国更好。

sentences.en: But there's too much capital on the line right now behind OpenAI / Anthropic for them to do this.They're really in a bind IMO.1 - http://stratechery.com/2026/whos-afraid-of-chinese-models/
sentences.zh: 但目前 OpenAI / Anthropic 背后的资金太多，他们无法做到这一点。IMO.1 - http://stratechery.com/2026/whos-afraid-of-chinese-models/

blocks.html_en: Well that's a big of a separate issue. You aren't violating copyright law to use the output of one LLM to train your own, but that doesn't mean they need to let you do it. You likewise aren't violating Apple's copyright if you use iTunes to make a missile, but Apple doesn't have to let you do it. (Example chosen because that carveout is/was in their EULA)

sentences.en: Well that's a big of a separate issue.
sentences.zh: 嗯，这是一个很大的单独问题。

sentences.en: You aren't violating copyright law to use the output of one LLM to train your own, but that doesn't mean they need to let you do it.
sentences.zh: 使用一位法学硕士的成果来培训自己的作品并不违反版权法，但这并不意味着他们需要让你这样做。

sentences.en: You likewise aren't violating Apple's copyright if you use iTunes to make a missile, but Apple doesn't have to let you do it.
sentences.zh: 同样，如果您使用 iTunes 制造导弹，您也不会侵犯 Apple 的版权，但 Apple 并不一定要让您这样做。

sentences.en: (Example chosen because that carveout is/was in their EULA)
sentences.zh: （选择示例是因为该剥离是/曾经在其 EULA 中）

blocks.html_en: The lease agreement does become a financial liability and corresponding asset when it commences. In this case the article is adding leases that haven’t started yet, so GAAP accounting rules prohibit it from being listed as a liability. As a result it’s disclosed in the notes. nb the typical US investor way to analyze it is to still not treat operating leases as debt, since the rent is already reducing the EBITDA (a proxy for cash flow before debt items are paid). The other way to do it is you add back the rent to your cash flow proxy and count the leases as debt. You end up in a similar place.

sentences.en: The lease agreement does become a financial liability and corresponding asset when it commences.
sentences.zh: 租赁协议在开始时即成为一项金融负债和相应的资产。

sentences.en: In this case the article is adding leases that haven’t started yet, so GAAP accounting rules prohibit it from being listed as a liability.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，本文添加了尚未开始的租赁，因此 GAAP 会计规则禁止将其列为负债。

sentences.en: As a result it’s disclosed in the notes.
sentences.zh: 因此，它在注释中披露。

sentences.en: nb the typical US investor way to analyze it is to still not treat operating leases as debt, since the rent is already reducing the EBITDA (a proxy for cash flow before debt items are paid).
sentences.zh: 注意，美国投资者的典型分析方法是仍然不将经营租赁视为债务，因为租金已经减少了 EBITDA（支付债务项目之前的现金流代理）。

sentences.en: The other way to do it is you add back the rent to your cash flow proxy and count the leases as debt.
sentences.zh: 另一种方法是将租金加回到现金流量代理中，并将租赁计为债务。

sentences.en: You end up in a similar place.
sentences.zh: 你最终会遇到类似的情况。

sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，本文添加了尚未开始的租赁，因此 GAAP 会计规则禁止将其列为负债。

sentences.en: As a result it’s disclosed in the notes.
sentences.zh: 因此，它在注释中披露。

sentences.en: nb the typical US investor way to analyze it is to still not treat operating leases as debt, since the rent is already reducing the EBITDA (a proxy for cash flow before debt items are paid).
sentences.zh: 注意，美国投资者的典型分析方法是仍然不将经营租赁视为债务，因为租金已经减少了 EBITDA（支付债务项目之前的现金流代理）。

sentences.en: The other way to do it is you add back the rent to your cash flow proxy and count the leases as debt.
sentences.zh: 另一种方法是将租金加回到现金流量代理中，并将租赁计为债务。

sentences.en: You end up in a similar place.
sentences.zh: 你最终会遇到类似的情况。

blocks.html_en:
Wouldn't improving LLM efficiency make them even more useful across the board, then they can enjoy the nice economies of scale?

The plan is to have LLM working completely autonomously, in that case, the more resources you have, the better. Perhaps people will use local LLM to ask questions, or coders use them for their personal projects, but that's not where the real money is.

blocks.html_en:
The problem is that if the AI companies pass through the actual costs they're incurring, then charges to those companies will >10x.

If the companies don't see that kind of value (so LLMs don't become dramatically better in some kind of quantum leap from where they are now), they won't want to pay those costs. Already, most AI projects in corporations tend to fail.

If the efficiency of LLMs gets 10x better, then either corporations will "private cloud" their own AI or start using competitors that aren't carrying those kinds of debt loads from the "gold rush" phase.

sentences.en: The problem is that if the AI companies pass through the actual costs they're incurring, then charges to those companies will >10x.If the companies don't see that kind of value (so LLMs don't become dramatically better in some kind of quantum leap from where they are now), they won't want to pay those costs.
sentences.zh: 问题是，如果人工智能公司转嫁了他们所产生的实际成本，那么向这些公司收取的费用将超过 10 倍。如果这些公司没有看到这种价值（因此法学硕士不会在某种飞跃上从现在的水平上取得显着的进步），他们就不会愿意支付这些成本。

sentences.en: Already, most AI projects in corporations tend to fail.If the efficiency of LLMs gets 10x better, then either corporations will "private cloud" their own AI or start using competitors that aren't carrying those kinds of debt loads from the "gold rush" phase.
sentences.zh: 目前，企业中的大多数人工智能项目都倾向于失败。如果法学硕士的效率提高 10 倍，那么企业要么将自己的人工智能“私有云”，要么开始使用不承担“淘金热”阶段此类债务负担的竞争对手。

blocks.html_en:
Truly, people have been saying this since 2017 and the Apple Neural Engine has proven them wrong time immemorial.

Apple's own desktops, with the fastest Apple Silicon GPUs and TDPs 20x higher than an iPhone still can't compete for real-world datacenter use even with RDNA clustering. Apple's GPGPU architecture is behind AMD at this point, there's a reason why Apple Intelligence is critically reliant on Nvidia and Google to provide inference backends.

sentences.en: Truly, people have been saying this since 2017 and the Apple Neural Engine has proven them wrong time immemorial.Apple's own desktops, with the fastest Apple Silicon GPUs and TDPs 20x higher than an iPhone still can't compete for real-world datacenter use even with RDNA clustering.
sentences.zh: 确实，人们自 2017 年以来就一直在这么说，而 Apple 神经引擎自古以来就证明了他们的错误。Apple 自己的台式机拥有最快的 Apple Silicon GPU 和比 iPhone 高 20 倍的 TDP，即使采用 RDNA 集群，仍然无法在现实世界的数据中心使用中竞争。

sentences.en: Apple's GPGPU architecture is behind AMD at this point, there's a reason why Apple Intelligence is critically reliant on Nvidia and Google to provide inference backends.
sentences.zh: Apple 的 GPGPU 架构目前落后于 AMD，这也是 Apple Intelligence 严重依赖 Nvidia 和 Google 提供推理后端的原因。

blocks.html_en: If the inference gets an order of magnitude cheaper, labs can afford to subsidise an order of magnitude more usage for the same marketing cost. So that part of usage will, if not accelerate with efficiency, at least still grow linearly with it. And there is a substantial amount of usage at or above true costs - everyone using a 3P harness, everyone on enterprise contracts, and everyone self-hosting an open weights model in a 3P cloud.

sentences.en: So that part of usage will, if not accelerate with efficiency, at least still grow linearly with it.

sentences.zh: 而且大量的使用量等于或高于真实成本 - 每个人都使用 3P 工具，每个人都签订企业合同，每个人都在 3P 云中自行托管开放权重模型。

blocks.html_en: If the inference gets an order of magnitude cheaper, labs can afford to subsidise an order of magnitude more usage for the same marketing cost. So that part of usage will, if not accelerate with efficiency, at least still grow linearly with it. And there is a substantial amount of usage at or above true costs - everyone using a 3P harness, everyone on enterprise contracts, and everyone self-hosting an open weights model in a 3P cloud.

Massively. Extracting more useful energy from coal has always been a goal.

Very much unlike with software. Where the goal for long while is to burn as many resources as possible on end user devices.

blocks.html_en: I assume the reference was to the Jevons paradox, as described in Jevons' 1865 book, "The Coal Question". Watt's steam engine massively increased the efficiency of coal in steam engines, which increased the use of coal fired steam engines, which increased coal consumption.

sentences.en: I assume the reference was to the Jevons paradox, as described in Jevons' 1865 book, "The Coal Question".
sentences.zh: 我认为这里指的是杰文斯悖论，如杰文斯 1865 年出版的著作《煤炭问题》中所述。

sentences.en: Watt's steam engine massively increased the efficiency of coal in steam engines, which increased the use of coal fired steam engines, which increased coal consumption.
sentences.zh: 瓦特的蒸汽机极大地提高了蒸汽机中煤炭的效率，从而增加了燃煤蒸汽机的使用，从而增加了煤炭消耗。

blocks.html_en: The (any!) comparrison to photovoltaics is not acurate.Photovoltaics (PV) are primary energy producing infrastructure that produces its own fuel and is now verticly integrated into it's own supply chain, nothing other than life itself posseses this atribute. AI, is exceptionaly likely to work in exactly the opposite fashion and take its host out as it goes down.

blocks.html_en: PV had to prove that it was truely indispensable and also prove to have realistic prospects for improvement and volume production before investments were made, and then prices came down. AI has proven that it burns more money faster than anything else, ever. I will admit that I am an early adopter of solar, but an AI refusenic, but still there is no reasonable comparison of AI and anything outside of religion.

Agents to agents

Agentic teams.

Finetuned models for everything like Java/spanish coding model.

Very long term research like multiply hours or days or weeks and plenty of these in parallel.

blocks.html_en:
I disagree in a way, part of the reason they can't really succeed at the moment is because it's way too expensive to really deploy at scale for most companies, but even for those AI companies themselves. If they can make business access subsidized/cheap the same way pro/plus/max/whatever plan are for regular users while still being profitable, this can work out. The other solution is if they do reach that "it's so super smart it's reinventing the world every day", but that one is much more of a maybe possibly one day.

What they can't do is the rug pull of pricing like Fable did, hoping for profitability while playing the "it's so super smart" card. It's very profitable, but customer will be very happy to leave for cheaper pasture and that's why the recent news about this or that cheaper chinese models make headlines.

Essentially, the rush now is "if I make it a boring profitable company I'm not worth a trillion AND i'm overshadowed that plays the singularity card even if they're bullshitting"

sentences.en: I disagree in a way, part of the reason they can't really succeed at the moment is because it's way too expensive to really deploy at scale for most companies, but even for those AI companies themselves.
sentences.zh: 我在某种程度上不同意，他们目前无法真正成功的部分原因是，对于大多数公司来说，真正大规模部署的成本太高，甚至对于那些人工智能公司本身来说也是如此。

sentences.en: If they can make business access subsidized/cheap the same way pro/plus/max/whatever plan are for regular users while still being profitable, this can work out.
sentences.zh: 如果他们能够以与普通用户的专业/增强/最大/任何计划相同的方式提供业务访问补贴/便宜，同时仍然有利可图，那么这可以解决。

sentences.en: The other solution is if they do reach that "it's so super smart it's reinventing the world every day", but that one is much more of a maybe possibly one day.What they can't do is the rug pull of pricing like Fable did, hoping for profitability while playing the "it's so super smart" card.
sentences.zh: 另一种解决方案是，如果他们确实达到了“它是如此超级智能，每天都在重塑世界”，但那只是可能有一天。他们不能做的是像神鬼寓言那样通过定价拉动，希望在打“它是如此超级智能”这张牌的同时获得盈利。

sentences.en: It's very profitable, but customer will be very happy to leave for cheaper pasture and that's why the recent news about this or that cheaper chinese models make headlines.Essentially, the rush now is "if I make it a boring profitable company I'm not worth a trillion AND i'm overshadowed that plays the singularity card even if they're bullshitting"
sentences.zh: 它非常有利可图，但客户会很乐意离开，去寻找更便宜的牧场，这就是为什么最近有关这个或那个更便宜的中国模型的新闻成为头条新闻。从本质上讲，现在的热潮是“如果我让它成为一家无聊的盈利公司，我就不值一万亿美元，而且我会被掩盖，即使他们在胡说八道，也打奇点牌”

sentences.zh: 它非常有利可图，但客户会很乐意离开，去寻找更便宜的牧场，这就是为什么最近有关这个或那个更便宜的中国模型的新闻成为头条新闻。从本质上讲，现在的热潮是“如果我让它成为一家无聊的盈利公司，我就不值一万亿美元，而且我会被掩盖，即使他们在胡说八道，也打奇点牌”

blocks.html_en: I disagree in a way, part of the reason they can't really succeed at the moment is because it's way too expensive to really deploy at scale for most companies, but even for those AI companies themselves. If they can make business access subsidized/cheap the same way pro/plus/max/whatever plan are for regular users while still being profitable, this can work out. The other solution is if they do reach that "it's so super smart it's reinventing the world every day", but that one is much more of a maybe possibly one day.

sentences.en: I disagree in a way, part of the reason they can't really succeed at the moment is because it's way too expensive to really deploy at scale for most companies, but even for those AI companies themselves.
sentences.zh: 我在某种程度上不同意，他们目前无法真正成功的部分原因是，对于大多数公司来说，真正大规模部署的成本太高，甚至对于那些人工智能公司本身来说也是如此。

sentences.en: If they can make business access subsidized/cheap the same way pro/plus/max/whatever plan are for regular users while still being profitable, this can work out.
sentences.zh: 如果他们能够以与普通用户的专业/增强/最大/任何计划相同的方式提供业务访问补贴/便宜，同时仍然有利可图，那么这可以解决。

sentences.en: The other solution is if they do reach that "it's so super smart it's reinventing the world every day", but that one is much more of a maybe possibly one day.
sentences.zh: 另一种解决方案是，如果他们确实达到了“它是如此超级智能，它每天都在重塑世界”，但那更可能是有一天。

blocks.html_en: What they can't do is the rug pull of pricing like Fable did, hoping for profitability while playing the "it's so super smart" card. It's very profitable, but customer will be very happy to leave for cheaper pasture and that's why the recent news about this or that cheaper chinese models make headlines.

sentences.en: What they can't do is the rug pull of pricing like Fable did, hoping for profitability while playing the "it's so super smart" card.
sentences.zh: 他们不能做的是像《神鬼寓言》那样通过定价拉动，希望在打出“超级聪明”牌的同时获得盈利。

sentences.en: It's very profitable, but customer will be very happy to leave for cheaper pasture and that's why the recent news about this or that cheaper chinese models make headlines.
sentences.zh: 这是非常有利可图的，但客户会很乐意离开到更便宜的牧场，这就是为什么最近有关这个或那个更便宜的中国模型的新闻成为头条新闻。

blocks.html_en: Essentially, the rush now is "if I make it a boring profitable company I'm not worth a trillion AND i'm overshadowed that plays the singularity card even if they're bullshitting"

sentences.zh: 从本质上讲，现在的热潮是“如果我让它成为一家无聊的盈利公司，我的身价就不会达到一万亿美元，而且即使他们在胡说八道，我也会打奇点牌而黯然失色”

blocks.html_en: You do realize "subsidizing" means charging less for something than it costs to provide, right? So they'll lose a dollar on every sale, but they'll make up for it in volume? E2E is usually where the profit comes from. If they're subsidizing getting regular users on board (pro/plus/max), and they're subsidizing to get businesses on board (massive deploys), where can the profit possibly come from without a pricing rug pull?

sentences.en: You do realize "subsidizing" means charging less for something than it costs to provide, right?
sentences.zh: 您确实意识到“补贴”意味着对某种东西的收费低于其提供成本，对吧？

sentences.en: So they'll lose a dollar on every sale, but they'll make up for it in volume?
sentences.zh: 那么他们每次销售都会损失一美元，但他们会通过销量来弥补吗？

sentences.en: E2E is usually where the profit comes from.
sentences.zh: E2E通常是利润的来源。

sentences.en: If they're subsidizing getting regular users on board (pro/plus/max), and they're subsidizing to get businesses on board (massive deploys), where can the profit possibly come from without a pricing rug pull?
sentences.zh: 如果他们补贴让普通用户加入（pro/plus/max），并且他们补贴让企业加入（大规模部署），那么在没有定价拉动的情况下，利润可能来自哪里？

I do, my point was answering to the "if it comes so cheap that" they would stop losing money of that, they would still need to subsidize for acquisition or some big clients or for rush times. It's the all-you-can-eat-buffet strategy.

I'm not saying I see them going that way or that I would, but at least THAT would possibly work.

blocks.html_en: Thank you for the clarification. I figured I was missing something in your meaning. Although "if it comes so cheap that..." means it will probably come cheap for other providers, and the margins wouldn't be there in the end because a price increase to take advantage of those big clients / rush times might lose the clients. I think we agree that their chances of becoming profitable don't look great.

sentences.zh: 谢谢您的澄清。

sentences.en: I figured I was missing something in your meaning.
sentences.zh: 我想我漏掉了你的意思。

sentences.en: Although "if it comes so cheap that..."
sentences.zh: 虽然“如果它这么便宜……”

sentences.en: means it will probably come cheap for other providers, and the margins wouldn't be there in the end because a price increase to take advantage of those big clients / rush times might lose the clients.
sentences.zh: 这意味着对于其他提供商来说，它可能会很便宜，而且最终不会有利润，因为为了利用这些大客户/高峰时间而提高价格可能会失去客户。

sentences.en: I think we agree that their chances of becoming profitable don't look great.
sentences.zh: 我想我们都同意他们盈利的机会看起来并不大。

blocks.html_en:
How about we regulate private corporations so they can't take a "gamble" that's equivalent to a private company giving everyone ferraris on the idea that they will eventually all become formula 1 drivers and give back 10x the ferrari's cost?

Even better, that "gamble" will have to be rescued by taxpayer money.

sentences.en: How about we regulate private corporations so they can't take a "gamble" that's equivalent to a private company giving everyone ferraris on the idea that they will eventually all become formula 1 drivers and give back 10x the ferrari's cost?Even better, that "gamble" will have to be rescued by taxpayer money.
sentences.zh: 我们如何监管私营公司，让他们不能进行一场“赌博”，相当于一家私营公司向每个人提供法拉利，让他们最终都成为一级方程式赛车手，并回馈 10 倍的法拉利成本？更好的是，这场“赌博”必须由纳税人的钱来拯救。

blocks.html_en: Jevon's Paradox ("As efficiency of resource use increases, usage of the resource increases") says otherwise. One things become more efficient, we can use them in lots of ways that would not have been viable before, driving up usage.

sentences.zh: 杰文悖论（“随着资源使用效率的提高，资源的使用量也会增加”）却另有说法。

sentences.en: One things become more efficient, we can use them in lots of ways that would not have been viable before, driving up usage.
sentences.zh: 一件事变得更加高效，我们可以以多种以前不可行的方式使用它们，从而提高使用率。

blocks.html_en:
Something doesn't quite smell right about this story. Here's a key paragraph from the Nikkei story that this Futurism story re-tells:

> Companies disclose such future debt not in their balance sheets, but in annotations to their quarterly financial statements. This is a legitimate practice under accounting rules, but may make it difficult for retail investors to recognize risks.

Does that justify a "tries to hide" headline?

This is also one of those cases where the headline is free but the details are behind a paywall.

I do think the story itself is notable, but I expect the discussion is going to lack some nuance.

sentences.en: Something doesn't quite smell right about this story.
sentences.zh: 这个故事有些不太对劲。

sentences.en: Here's a key paragraph from the Nikkei story that this Futurism story re-tells:> Companies disclose such future debt not in their balance sheets, but in annotations to their quarterly financial statements.
sentences.zh: 以下是日经新闻故事中的一个关键段落，这个未来主义故事重新讲述了：> 公司不是在资产负债表中披露此类未来债务，而是在季度财务报表的注释中披露。

sentences.en: This is a legitimate practice under accounting rules, but may make it difficult for retail investors to recognize risks.Does that justify a "tries to hide" headline?This is also one of those cases where the headline is free but the details are behind a paywall.I do think the story itself is notable, but I expect the discussion is going to lack some nuance.
sentences.zh: 根据会计规则，这是合法的做法，但可能会使散户投资者难以识别风险。这是否证明“试图隐藏”标题是合理的？这也是标题免费但细节隐藏在付费墙后面的情况之一。我确实认为这个故事本身值得注意，但我预计讨论将缺乏一些细微差别。

Futurism has a pretty strong anti-AI bias. Both article titles and the articles themselves tend to be editorialized. On a scale of zero to Ed Zitron, they're somewhere around the middle.

I don't treat it as a reliable publication when it comes to anything AI related.

blocks.html_en: The crux of the problem is models are not evolving anymore, they're iterating. Cheaper, faster, better. We're only seeing better, and that's because there is fierce competition with massive debt behind it. The "cheaper and faster" part is all taking place with local models. All of this adds up to a big red flag for a bubble.

sentences.en: The crux of the problem is models are not evolving anymore, they're iterating.
sentences.zh: 问题的关键是模型不再进化，而是迭代。

sentences.en: Cheaper, faster, better.
sentences.zh: 更便宜、更快、更好。

sentences.en: We're only seeing better, and that's because there is fierce competition with massive debt behind it.
sentences.zh: 我们只是看到了更好的结果，那是因为激烈的竞争和背后的巨额债务。

sentences.en: The "cheaper and faster" part is all taking place with local models.
sentences.zh: “更便宜、更快”的部分都发生在本地模型上。

sentences.en: All of this adds up to a big red flag for a bubble.
sentences.zh: 所有这些加起来对泡沫来说是一个巨大的危险信号。

blocks.html_en: Oh, that's crazy when you buy every single piece of computing hardware that's ever been made in the last five years, for inflated prices to preempt competition, then sit on it because there isn't enough electric in the entire world to power it, do you think that would be pure profit?

sentences.en: Oh, that's crazy when you buy every single piece of computing hardware that's ever been made in the last five years, for inflated prices to preempt competition, then sit on it because there isn't enough electric in the entire world to power it, do you think that would be pure profit?
sentences.zh: 哦，当你购买过去五年中生产的每一件计算硬件，以高昂的价格来抢占竞争优势，然后因为全世界没有足够的电力来为其提供动力而坐拥它，你认为这会是纯利润吗？

blocks.html_en:
For the last 20+ years i was really relaxed about tech industry - the fat cash positions maintained by the companies as a lesson from the early 2000-s crash (when companies were running out of cash and failing or severely downsizing, etc.) almost guaranteed that the tech will easily weather any trouble times - and that was clearly demonstrated in 2008 and in 2020.

Now i'm starting to get scared - these cash positions are basically gone if matched against the new debt and various creative financing taken on for the AI buildouts. That looks a lot like 2000 - very promising tech everybody is piling money in. And i'm sure that several years later it will provide several decades of tremendous success like Internet did in the last 20 years. It is just those few initial choppy years of the hockey stick trough that we may be coming upon and that many may not survive not having that fat cash position anymore, and thus those years may happen to be very painful for the tech and for the whole economy.

25 years ago there were a lot (estimates put even as high as 95%) of dark fiber left as a result of the dotcom buildout and crash. It was successfully put back into action several years later. I wonder whether we will have similar dark datacenters in a few years.

sentences.en: For the last 20+ years i was really relaxed about tech industry - the fat cash positions maintained by the companies as a lesson from the early 2000-s crash (when companies were running out of cash and failing or severely downsizing, etc.) almost guaranteed that the tech will easily weather any trouble times - and that was clearly demonstrated in 2008 and in 2020.Now i'm starting to get scared - these cash positions are basically gone if matched against the new debt and various creative financing taken on for the AI buildouts.
sentences.zh: 在过去的 20 多年里，我对科技行业感到非常放松 - 作为 2000 年代初期崩溃（当时公司现金耗尽、倒闭或严重裁员等）的教训，公司保持的大量现金头寸几乎可以保证科技将轻松度过任何困难时期 - 这在 2008 年和 2020 年得到了清楚的证明。现在我开始感到害怕 - 如果与新债务和各种创造性融资相匹配，这些现金头寸基本上就会消失。人工智能增强。

sentences.en: That looks a lot like 2000 - very promising tech everybody is piling money in.
sentences.zh: 这看起来很像 2000 年——每个人都在投入大量资金的非常有前途的技术。

sentences.en: And i'm sure that several years later it will provide several decades of tremendous success like Internet did in the last 20 years.
sentences.zh: 我相信几年后它将像过去 20 年的互联网一样带来几十年的巨大成功。

sentences.en: It is just those few initial choppy years of the hockey stick trough that we may be coming upon and that many may not survive not having that fat cash position anymore, and thus those years may happen to be very painful for the tech and for the whole economy.25 years ago there were a lot (estimates put even as high as 95%) of dark fiber left as a result of the dotcom buildout and crash.
sentences.zh: 我们可能会遇到曲棍球棒低谷最初的那几年，许多人可能无法再拥有丰厚的现金头寸，因此这些年对科技和整个经济来说可能会非常痛苦。25 年前，由于互联网泡沫的扩张和崩溃，留下了很多（估计甚至高达 95%）暗光纤。

sentences.en: It was successfully put back into action several years later.
sentences.zh: 几年后，它成功地重新投入使用。

sentences.en: I wonder whether we will have similar dark datacenters in a few years.
sentences.zh: 我想知道几年后我们是否还会有类似的黑暗数据中心。

blocks.html_en:
It is.

A predictable reset schedule with refinancing ("valuations"). Loose money leading to debt obligations to be paid in the future. Circular financing.

And what's funny, is it's precisely the off-book debt that will make the compression happen slowly. There's no bailouts this time.

sentences.en: It is.A predictable reset schedule with refinancing ("valuations").
sentences.zh: 这是一个可预测的再融资重置时间表（“估值”）。

sentences.en: Loose money leading to debt obligations to be paid in the future.
sentences.zh: 宽松的资金导致未来需要偿还债务。

sentences.en: Circular financing.And what's funny, is it's precisely the off-book debt that will make the compression happen slowly.
sentences.zh: 循环融资。有趣的是，正是账外债务使得压缩缓慢发生。

sentences.en: There's no bailouts this time.
sentences.zh: 这次没有救助。

blocks.html_en: Given that AI exec's have been careful to lavish praise (and cash) on Trump for the last few years, I suspect bailouts will not be off the table.

blocks.html_en:
I suspect bailouts will be off the table. Simply because the people who support OpenAI and the tech industry are on the same side of the political aisle as the people protesting data centers.

"Big Tech" as a pejorative started on the Right. That never changed.

Remember the hubbub about companies having "free speech" while they deplatformed and censored critics? That never sat well with a certain group of people. And they're the kind of people with long memories, that might have guests entertained as a visible sign of submission, and not as friends.

The more interesting revelation will be if certain persons can psychologically come to admit the people they are against are politically aligned with them, and their opponents are more intelligent than they are willing to let on.

sentences.en: I suspect bailouts will be off the table.
sentences.zh: 我怀疑救助计划不会被考虑。

sentences.en: Simply because the people who support OpenAI and the tech industry are on the same side of the political aisle as the people protesting data centers."Big Tech" as a pejorative started on the Right.
sentences.zh: 仅仅是因为支持 OpenAI 和科技行业的人与抗议数据中心的人站在政治通道的同一边。“Big Tech”作为一个贬义词从右翼开始。

sentences.en: That never changed.Remember the hubbub about companies having "free speech" while they deplatformed and censored critics?
sentences.zh: 这一点从未改变。还记得有关公司在取消平台和审查批评者的同时拥有“言论自由”的喧嚣吗？

sentences.en: That never sat well with a certain group of people.
sentences.zh: 这对某些人来说从来都不是一件好事。

sentences.en: And they're the kind of people with long memories, that might have guests entertained as a visible sign of submission, and not as friends.The more interesting revelation will be if certain persons can psychologically come to admit the people they are against are politically aligned with them, and their opponents are more intelligent than they are willing to let on.
sentences.zh: 他们是那种记忆力很长的人，可能会招待客人作为屈服的明显标志，而不是作为朋友。更有趣的启示将是，如果某些人能够在心理上承认他们反对的人在政治上与他们结盟，并且他们的对手比他们愿意表现出来的更聪明。

blocks.html_en:
I suspect bailouts will be off the table. Simply because the people who support OpenAI and the tech industry are on the same side of the political aisle as the people protesting data centers.

"Big Tech" as a pejorative started on the Right. That never changed.

Remember the hubbub about companies having "free speech" while they deplatformed and censored critics? That never sat well with a certain group of people. And they're the kind of people with long memories, that might have guests entertained as a visible sign of submission, and not as friends.

The more interesting revelation will be if certain persons can psychologically come to admit the people they are against are politically aligned with them, and their opponents are more intelligent than they are willing to let on.

blocks.html_en:
They're obviously not taking on enough debt because I'm paying $200 per month for one AI, $100 per month for a second, a $20 "donation" to Gemini[1] paying for a service I never use just to fund its development, and yet here I am doing my own laundry, making my own damn breakfast, lunch, and dinner and manually tracking my Calories and macros, I'm putting my own damn dishes away, racking and unracking my own damn weights at home, and taking minutes to set up and record my exercise form and then take screenshots of it of key frames that I manually ask the AI's to form check (they don't consume video natively as an input) rather than have a robot do any of the above (including act as a fitness coach) because where's my household robot I can rent on a monthly payment? Can't be that expensive, servos and pressure sensors and cameras are cheap, what's missing here is that here we are and AI can't do shit for me day to day other than knowledge work and software engineering. I'd like these companies to take on as much debt as possible and rent me a robot that can do stuff for me. I have a petition for this that you can sign here if you want:

https://www.change.org/p/create-a-physical-embodiment-for-cl...

[1] I don't use Gemini for anything ever, I pay just to put my vote to them making a useful model (I know my $20 isn't much but I apply Kant'e categorical imperative - if everyone did it they'd take their AI seriously and not be in last place behind OpenAI, Anthropic, and even open-weight models).

sentences.en: They're obviously not taking on enough debt because I'm paying $200 per month for one AI, $100 per month for a second, a $20 "donation" to Gemini[1] paying for a service I never use just to fund its development, and yet here I am doing my own laundry, making my own damn breakfast, lunch, and dinner and manually tracking my Calories and macros, I'm putting my own damn dishes away, racking and unracking my own damn weights at home, and taking minutes to set up and record my exercise form and then take screenshots of it of key frames that I manually ask the AI's to form check (they don't consume video natively as an input) rather than have a robot do any of the above (including act as a fitness coach) because where's my household robot I can rent on a monthly payment?
sentences.zh: 他们显然没有承担足够的债务，因为我每月为一个人工智能支付 200 美元，每月为一秒钟支付 100 美元，向 Gemini[1] 支付 20 美元的“捐款”，用于支付一项我从未使用过的服务，只是为了资助其开发，但我在这里自己洗衣服，自己做该死的早餐、午餐和晚餐，并手动跟踪我的卡路里和宏指令，我正在把我自己的该死的盘子收起来，在家里摆放和打开我自己该死的体重，并带着分钟来设置和记录我的锻炼形式，然后截取关键帧的屏幕截图，我手动要求人工智能进行检查（它们本身不使用视频作为输入），而不是让机器人执行上述任何操作（包括充当健身教练），因为我可以按月付款租用的家用机器人在哪里？

sentences.en: Can't be that expensive, servos and pressure sensors and cameras are cheap, what's missing here is that here we are and AI can't do shit for me day to day other than knowledge work and software engineering.
sentences.zh: 不可能那么贵，伺服系统、压力传感器和摄像头都很便宜，这里缺少的是我们在这里，除了知识工作和软件工程之外，人工智能不能为我做日常的事情。

sentences.en: I'd like these companies to take on as much debt as possible and rent me a robot that can do stuff for me.
sentences.zh: 我希望这些公司承担尽可能多的债务，并租给我一个可以为我做事的机器人。

sentences.en: I have a petition for this that you can sign here if you want:https://www.change.org/p/create-a-physical-embodiment-for-cl...[1] I don't use Gemini for anything ever, I pay just to put my vote to them making a useful model (I know my $20 isn't much but I apply Kant'e categorical imperative - if everyone did it they'd take their AI seriously and not be in last place behind OpenAI, Anthropic, and even open-weight models).
sentences.zh: 我为此准备了一份请愿书，如果您愿意，可以在这里签名：https://www.change.org/p/create-a-physical-embodiment-for-cl...[1]我从来没有使用 Gemini 做任何事情，我付钱只是为了把我的选票投给他们，让他们制作一个有用的模型（我知道我的 20 美元并不算多，但我应用了 Kant'e 绝对命令 - 如果每个人都这样做，他们就会认真对待他们的人工智能，而不是落后于 OpenAI、Anthropic 甚至开放权重模型）。

blocks.html_en:
You are donating money to Google, one of the richest companies in the world?

It really doesn't need your help, and it's already way too powerful. If you have money to spare, can't you give to good causes instead?

sentences.en: You are donating money to Google, one of the richest companies in the world?It really doesn't need your help, and it's already way too powerful.
sentences.zh: 你正在向世界上最富有的公司之一谷歌捐款？它真的不需要你的帮助，而且它已经太强大了。

sentences.en: If you have money to spare, can't you give to good causes instead?
sentences.zh: 如果你有闲钱，你就不能捐献给公益事业吗？

blocks.html_en:
Market can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent...

Simply choosing not to get involved might be most reasonable action.

blocks.html_en:
We also may be at the wealth inequality level where prices become… weird.

If there is really only a few dozen people doing the buying and the selling at the top on a weighted basis, then the prices are whatever they convince themselves of.

sentences.en: We also may be at the wealth inequality level where prices become…
sentences.zh: 我们也可能处于财富不平等的水平，价格变得……

sentences.en: weird.If there is really only a few dozen people doing the buying and the selling at the top on a weighted basis, then the prices are whatever they convince themselves of.
sentences.zh: 很奇怪。如果真的只有几十个人在加权的基础上在顶部进行买卖，那么价格就是他们说服自己的任何价格。

blocks.html_en:
And? Its not my debt.

If they continue investing in compute, memory, memory bandwidth, network infrastructure, etc. it makes a relevant contribution of progress in all of these fields which I will leverage.

A small form factor PC with 100gb fast memory and being able to run something like sonnet or opus level LLM would be massive.

I have so many things i want to do and still sitting it out due to cost.

sentences.en: And?
sentences.zh: 和？

sentences.en: Its not my debt.If they continue investing in compute, memory, memory bandwidth, network infrastructure, etc. it makes a relevant contribution of progress in all of these fields which I will leverage.A small form factor PC with 100gb fast memory and being able to run something like sonnet or opus level LLM would be massive.I have so many things i want to do and still sitting it out due to cost.
sentences.zh: 这不是我的债务。如果他们继续投资于计算、内存、内存带宽、网络基础设施等，它将为我将利用的所有这些领域的进步做出相关贡献。一台具有 100GB 快速内存并能够运行十四行诗或作品级别 LLM 之类的小型 PC 将是巨大的。我有很多想做的事情，但由于成本而仍然搁置。

blocks.html_en: Lots of people didn't invest in mortgage backed securities but still got screwed in 2008. When something is systemic, you don't have to be directly exposed to be effected when it goes sideways.

sentences.en: Lots of people didn't invest in mortgage backed securities but still got screwed in 2008.
sentences.zh: 很多人没有投资抵押贷款支持证券，但在 2008 年仍然惨遭失败。

sentences.en: When something is systemic, you don't have to be directly exposed to be effected when it goes sideways.
sentences.zh: 当某件事是系统性的时，当它出现横向波动时，你不必直接暴露就会受到影响。

blocks.html_en:
Is this in practice what's going to happen, or are (1) the prices going to hike (and never go down) for the consumer and (2) the memory companies will just continue doing what they already do because they're still selling their old shovels to the gold diggers?

I don't see how this will benefit the consumer, but I might be missing some second order effect?

sentences.en: Is this in practice what's going to happen, or are (1) the prices going to hike (and never go down) for the consumer and (2) the memory companies will just continue doing what they already do because they're still selling their old shovels to the gold diggers?I don't see how this will benefit the consumer, but I might be missing some second order effect?
sentences.zh: 这在实践中会发生什么吗？或者（1）消费者的价格将会上涨（并且永远不会下降），（2）内存公司将继续做他们已经做的事情，因为他们仍然把旧铲子卖给淘金者？我不知道这会给消费者带来什么好处，但我可能会错过一些二阶效应？

blocks.html_en:
I read somewhere that the memory companies were massivly pushed for lowest prices especially by companies like apple.

I want to hope that this money will lead to more capacity, more R&D and lower prices in the long term again.

Nvidia would have changed its GPU strategy a long time ago if the demand wouldn't be real. They still can afford the GPU prices. But memory is not a monopoly.

For memory though i do assume a lot more people and companies want a massive amount more memory than ever before. I have 64gb in my pc for a few years now, i was quite happy with that. It became a no brainer. But today? Hey give me 100, 300 and even more. I really want to run bigger LLM models locally.

sentences.en: I read somewhere that the memory companies were massivly pushed for lowest prices especially by companies like apple.I want to hope that this money will lead to more capacity, more R&D and lower prices in the long term again.Nvidia would have changed its GPU strategy a long time ago if the demand wouldn't be real.
sentences.zh: 我在某处读到，内存公司被大规模推向最低价格，尤其是像苹果这样的公司。我希望这笔钱能够再次带来更多的产能、更多的研发和长期更低的价格。如果需求不真实，Nvidia 早就改变了 GPU 策略。

sentences.en: They still can afford the GPU prices.
sentences.zh: 他们仍然负担得起 GPU 的价格。

sentences.en: But memory is not a monopoly.For memory though i do assume a lot more people and companies want a massive amount more memory than ever before.
sentences.zh: 但内存并不是垄断。对于内存，我确实假设有更多的人和公司比以往任何时候都需要更多的内存。

sentences.en: I have 64gb in my pc for a few years now, i was quite happy with that.
sentences.zh: 我的电脑里有 64GB 内存已经好几年了，我对此非常满意。

sentences.en: It became a no brainer.
sentences.zh: 这变得理所当然。

sentences.en: But today?
sentences.zh: 但今天呢？

sentences.en: Hey give me 100, 300 and even more.
sentences.zh: 嘿，给我 100、300 甚至更多。

sentences.en: I really want to run bigger LLM models locally.
sentences.zh: 我真的很想在本地运行更大的法学硕士模型。

blocks.html_en:
> And? Its not my debt.

For the moment.

There are several ways that ordinary investors and even simple pension holders could end up stuck with the downside of this.

The debt risk hidden in CDOs wasn't your debt either but if you had a pension plan, the crisis absolutely cost you money you would have earned, and in many cases pension fund values dropped by five to ten per cent within a year.

The SPV/CDO comparison being made is by no means exact, but hidden debt at this scale surprising analysts tends to cause problems. If more institutions are severely exposed than anyone thought, it is bad.

Especially since any success strategy is predicated on literally unbelievably rosy predictions.

sentences.en: > And?
sentences.zh: > 然后呢？

sentences.en: Its not my debt.For the moment.There are several ways that ordinary investors and even simple pension holders could end up stuck with the downside of this.The debt risk hidden in CDOs wasn't your debt either but if you had a pension plan, the crisis absolutely cost you money you would have earned, and in many cases pension fund values dropped by five to ten per cent within a year.The SPV/CDO comparison being made is by no means exact, but hidden debt at this scale surprising analysts tends to cause problems.
sentences.zh: 这不是我的债务。就目前而言。普通投资者甚至简单的养老金持有者可能会通过多种方式陷入这种不利的境地。CDO 中隐藏的债务风险也不是你的债务，但如果你有养老金计划，这场危机绝对会损失你本应赚到的钱，而且在许多情况下，养老基金价值在一年内下降了 5% 到 10%。所做的 SPV/CDO 比较绝不准确，但如此规模的隐性债务往往会导致问题，令分析师感到惊讶。

sentences.en: If more institutions are severely exposed than anyone thought, it is bad.Especially since any success strategy is predicated on literally unbelievably rosy predictions.
sentences.zh: 如果受到严重影响的机构比任何人想象的都要多，那就糟糕了。特别是因为任何成功的策略都是建立在令人难以置信的乐观预测的基础上的。

blocks.html_en: There are several ways that ordinary investors and even simple pension holders could end up stuck with the downside of this.

sentences.en: There are several ways that ordinary investors and even simple pension holders could end up stuck with the downside of this.
sentences.zh: 普通投资者甚至简单的养老金持有者可能会通过多种方式最终陷入这种负面影响。

blocks.html_en: The debt risk hidden in CDOs wasn't your debt either but if you had a pension plan, the crisis absolutely cost you money you would have earned, and in many cases pension fund values dropped by five to ten per cent within a year.

sentences.en: The debt risk hidden in CDOs wasn't your debt either but if you had a pension plan, the crisis absolutely cost you money you would have earned, and in many cases pension fund values dropped by five to ten per cent within a year.
sentences.zh: CDO 中隐藏的债务风险也不是你的债务，但如果你有养老金计划，这场危机绝对会损失你本应赚到的钱，而且在许多情况下，养老金价值在一年内下降了 5% 到 10%。

blocks.html_en: The SPV/CDO comparison being made is by no means exact, but hidden debt at this scale surprising analysts tends to cause problems. If more institutions are severely exposed than anyone thought, it is bad.

sentences.en: The SPV/CDO comparison being made is by no means exact, but hidden debt at this scale surprising analysts tends to cause problems.
sentences.zh: SPV/CDO 的比较并不准确，但如此规模的隐性债务往往会引发问题，令分析师感到惊讶。

sentences.en: If more institutions are severely exposed than anyone thought, it is bad.
sentences.zh: 如果受到严重影响的机构比任何人想象的都要多，那就糟糕了。

blocks.html_en: Especially since any success strategy is predicated on literally unbelievably rosy predictions.

sentences.en: Especially since any success strategy is predicated on literally unbelievably rosy predictions.
sentences.zh: 特别是因为任何成功策略都基于令人难以置信的乐观预测。

blocks.html_en: The thing is they actually pushed the price tags of memory and disks high so we wouldn’t be able to afford it. Unless ofc you rent from them.

sentences.zh: 问题是他们实际上把内存和磁盘的价格推高了，所以我们买不起。

sentences.en: Unless ofc you rent from them.
sentences.zh: 除非你从他们那里租。

blocks.html_en: If hyperscalers flop, and there’s a good chance they will, memory and disk prices will crater. They are historically the most volatile asset in tech. If Samsung, Micron et al can’t sell to hyperscalers they will switch back to consumer, because they can’t just turn off a memory fab without losing billions.

sentences.en: If hyperscalers flop, and there’s a good chance they will, memory and disk prices will crater.
sentences.zh: 如果超大规模企业失败（而且很有可能失败），那么内存和磁盘价格将会大幅下跌。

sentences.en: They are historically the most volatile asset in tech.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，它们是科技领域最不稳定的资产。

sentences.en: If Samsung, Micron et al can’t sell to hyperscalers they will switch back to consumer, because they can’t just turn off a memory fab without losing billions.
sentences.zh: 如果三星、美光等公司无法向超大规模厂商出售产品，他们将转回消费者市场，因为他们无法在不损失数十亿美元的情况下关闭内存工厂。

blocks.html_en:
only that is not so. disk prices maybe, the memory will not be available to consumers because it's a tech that makes sense only for datacenters full of GPUs and massive power/cooling. by now all fabs have converted to it, there might be a lot of HBM capacity freed but no consumer devices that can use it. retooling all fabs to produce consumer level memory will take a lot of time if they even do it at all instead of just pushing for consumers to rent datacenter capacity directly.

all three major remaining players in the mem sector have already tried in the past to collude for memory price fixing.

this is the first time I'm rooting for chinese chip tech to reach more or less parity.

sentences.en: only that is not so.
sentences.zh: 但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: disk prices maybe, the memory will not be available to consumers because it's a tech that makes sense only for datacenters full of GPUs and massive power/cooling.
sentences.zh: 磁盘价格可能会上涨，消费者将无法使用内存，因为这项技术仅对充满 GPU 和大量电源/冷却的数据中心有意义。

sentences.en: by now all fabs have converted to it, there might be a lot of HBM capacity freed but no consumer devices that can use it.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，所有晶圆厂都已转向它，可能会释放大量 HBM 产能，但没有消费设备可以使用它。

sentences.en: retooling all fabs to produce consumer level memory will take a lot of time if they even do it at all instead of just pushing for consumers to rent datacenter capacity directly.all three major remaining players in the mem sector have already tried in the past to collude for memory price fixing.this is the first time I'm rooting for chinese chip tech to reach more or less parity.
sentences.zh: 重组所有晶圆厂以生产消费级内存将需要花费大量时间（如果他们真的这样做的话），而不是仅仅推动消费者直接租用数据中心容量。内存行业中剩下的三个主要参与者过去已经试图串通内存价格操纵。这是我第一次支持中国芯片技术达到或多或少的平价。

blocks.html_en: That switch will actually take a few months, technically speaking. What may delay it is manufacturers strategizing to avoid oversupply and trying to minimize their loses from all the investment in tooling they cannot longer repurpose. It will be a logistical and financial nightmare for them too.

sentences.en: That switch will actually take a few months, technically speaking.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，这种转变实际上需要几个月的时间。

sentences.en: What may delay it is manufacturers strategizing to avoid oversupply and trying to minimize their loses from all the investment in tooling they cannot longer repurpose.
sentences.zh: 可能会推迟这一进程的是制造商制定战略以避免供应过剩，并试图将因无法再重新利用的工具投资而造成的损失降至最低。

sentences.en: It will be a logistical and financial nightmare for them too.
sentences.zh: 对于他们来说，这也将是一场后勤和财务噩梦。

blocks.html_en:
Yes that is unfortunate for sure don't get me wrong this affects me but the overall benefit will still be bigger i assume.

10 years ago i watched a talk about the problem of compute vs. memory. Compute increased significantly while memory speed did not.

This gigantic investment will solve this problem.

So either this blows and we will have way too much capacity which will lead to cheap and mass amount of memory for everyone + cheap GPUs again OR AGI. So win - win.

sentences.en: Yes that is unfortunate for sure don't get me wrong this affects me but the overall benefit will still be bigger i assume.10 years ago i watched a talk about the problem of compute vs. memory.
sentences.zh: 是的，这肯定是不幸的，不要误会我的意思，这会影响我，但我认为总体好处仍然会更大。10 年前，我观看了一个关于计算与内存问题的演讲。

sentences.en: Compute increased significantly while memory speed did not.This gigantic investment will solve this problem.So either this blows and we will have way too much capacity which will lead to cheap and mass amount of memory for everyone + cheap GPUs again OR AGI.
sentences.zh: 计算显着增加，而内存速度却没有。这项巨大的投资将解决这个问题。所以要么这会失败，我们将拥有太多的容量，这将导致每个人都拥有廉价和大量的内存+廉价的 GPU 或 AGI。

sentences.en: So win - win.
sentences.zh: 所以双赢。

blocks.html_en:
> And? Its not my debt.

Your view seems very myopic.

AFAIK they have heavily relaxed the rules for IPO. Pension funds are practically forced to buy from the top-100 companies, and these companies risk crashing much more than the others.

SpaceX value is already lower than at launch. If this costs are externalized to the common public, this will be your debt.

All these companies are too big to fail, in an environment where you can buy pardons and laws.

Hell, a 3T$ crash will have global repercussion and probably partially crash many other countries, too.

sentences.en: > And?
sentences.zh: > 然后呢？

sentences.en: Its not my debt.Your view seems very myopic.AFAIK they have heavily relaxed the rules for IPO.
sentences.zh: 这不是我的债务。你的观点似乎非常短视。据我所知，他们已经大大放宽了 IPO 的规则。

sentences.en: Pension funds are practically forced to buy from the top-100 companies, and these companies risk crashing much more than the others.SpaceX value is already lower than at launch.
sentences.zh: 养老基金实际上被迫从前 100 名公司购买股票，而这些公司崩溃的风险比其他公司大得多。SpaceX 的价值已经低于发射时的水平。

sentences.en: If this costs are externalized to the common public, this will be your debt.All these companies are too big to fail, in an environment where you can buy pardons and laws.Hell, a 3T$ crash will have global repercussion and probably partially crash many other countries, too.
sentences.zh: 如果这些成本被外部化到普通公众身上，这将是你的债务。所有这些公司都太大而不能倒，在一个你可以购买赦免和法律的环境中。见鬼，3T美元的崩溃将产生全球影响，并可能部分地影响许多其他国家。

blocks.html_en: Hell, a 3T$ crash will have global repercussion and probably partially crash many other countries, too.

sentences.en: Hell, a 3T$ crash will have global repercussion and probably partially crash many other countries, too.
sentences.zh: 哎呀，3T$ 的崩溃将会产生全球性的影响，并且可能也会部分地影响许多其他国家。

blocks.html_en:
Luckily so far only one index changed its rules to cover SpaceX and what the AI IPOs would need.

I don't know how specifically significant SpaceX being lower than at launch is, because actually most IPOs underperform the market and their own targets for the first three to five years. What is happening to it is not that unusual; its overvaluation is.

I do think there is a major risk here, and ordinary investors and pension holders will be hurt.

I am not sure any individual AI company is too big to fail, though probably one of the big two will be rescued, most likely Anthropic. I think OpenAI will fail, and it'll be stripped for parts. As will Oracle, who are overexposed to it.

sentences.en: Luckily so far only one index changed its rules to cover SpaceX and what the AI IPOs would need.I don't know how specifically significant SpaceX being lower than at launch is, because actually most IPOs underperform the market and their own targets for the first three to five years.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，到目前为止，只有一个指数改变了规则，以涵盖 SpaceX 以及人工智能 IPO 所需的内容。我不知道 SpaceX 低于发射时的水平具体有多大，因为实际上大多数 IPO 在前三到五年的表现都低于市场和它们自己的目标。

blocks.html_en: Exactly, this is how capitalism works. Let them shoot for the moon and let them fail. Worst case their over-valued assets are liquidated and continued on with at a more reasonable valuation. Just make sure they play by the rules and don't make new rules in the name of national security, ie boxing out open source.

sentences.en: Exactly, this is how capitalism works.
sentences.zh: 确切地说，这就是资本主义的运作方式。

sentences.en: Let them shoot for the moon and let them fail.
sentences.zh: 让他们奔向月球，让他们失败。

sentences.en: Worst case their over-valued assets are liquidated and continued on with at a more reasonable valuation.
sentences.zh: 最坏的情况是，他们被高估的资产被清算，并以更合理的估值继续持有。

sentences.en: Just make sure they play by the rules and don't make new rules in the name of national security, ie boxing out open source.
sentences.zh: 只要确保他们遵守规则，不要以国家安全的名义制定新规则，即排除开源。

blocks.html_en: Except this is not how American capitalism works at this scale, and it’s ridiculous to think their debt isn’t your debt when you have the entire country’s history to look back on and count the numerous government bailouts.

content_html_zh: 只要这笔债务不进入人寿保险和养老基金，我们就没事。 问题在于，私人信贷正在控制一些人寿保险公司，并将债务转移给这些公司。 当这些失败时，这将成为每个人的问题。 > 金融稳定的风险也可能来自于私人信贷市场敞口特别高的实体，例如受私募股权公司和某些养老基金群体影响的保险公司。 近年来，私募股权控制的保险公司的资产大幅增长，这些实体比其他保险公司拥有更多流动性较低的投资https://www.imf.org/-/media/files/publications/gfsr/2024/apr...https://www.imf.org/-/media/files/publications/gfsr/2024/apr...考虑到标准普尔指数集中在这些公司，这难道不是一个问题吗？目前，这些公司在大部分美国人的 401k 计划中占很大一部分。 如果退休人员的工资下降 40-50%，可能会带走市场，这会对他们产生什么影响？我建议研究“EQL”，或者更好的是，通过在 SPDR 的 11 XL* 行业基金中持有头寸来复制其指数，并为每个基金分配相同的权重。 最终，人们的股票将按行业平均加权，并拥有大量大盘股，而不是整个市场等权重策略中明显的中盘股倾斜。就我个人而言，我完全放弃金融行业（托马斯主义禁止高利贷），这使得甚至 10 只基金在心理和实践中都很容易分配。 例如，假设 60/40 分配，其中一个人持有大部分股票，其余部分持有债券（我在这里用黄金、加密货币、现金和瑞士法郎的组合代替），则分配如下：XLC 6% XLY 6% XLP 6% XLE 6% XLV 6% XLI 6% XLB 6% XLK 6% XLU 6% XLRE 6%（请注意，XLF 是有意识地不作为一个职位，请决定它是否适合您。 根据行业选择规则，会使 XLRE 出现问题的 Mortgate REIT 属于 XLF）如果您不介意高利贷或某种与股票负相关或中性相关的资产，那么剩下的 40% 是债券债务。问题是这种策略仅在税收优惠账户中可行。 否则，再平衡过程中税收的拖累会大大降低回报。因为债券涉及利息。 根据《神学大全》：> 以借出的钱收取高利贷本身就是不公正的，因为这是出售不存在的东西，这显然会导致与正义相悖的不平等。https://www.newadvent.org/summa/3078.htm...阿奎那扩展了分析，但它相对简单：所有利息都是高利贷。就我个人而言，我发现想象两个代表整个经济的假设人很有帮助，一个是贷款的债权人，另一个是贷款人接受贷款的借款人。 在这个具有固定货币数量的超简单封闭模型中，前者实际上要求比整个系统中实际存在的货币单位更多的货币单位。 当贷款到期时，借款人欠下一笔无法从流通媒介本身全额支付的款项。 然后，和解需要违约、债权人宽恕超额部分，或者转移实际货物和财产来弥补差额。 扩大规模后，我怀疑同样的压力（超过现有货币存量的货币债权的不断产生）给家庭和社区带来了很多微妙和明显的压力，而在西方和现代增长导向的资本社会中，人们经常抱怨这种压力。这种高利贷的定义不同于现代的漏洞定义：只有当利率超过某个模糊的滥用门槛时，计息贷款才算高利贷。 在上述托马斯主义的解释中，所有的利益都是社会问题和不受欢迎的。 当犹太人之间借贷时，犹太教也有类似的利息禁令。 伊斯兰教同样更广泛地禁止高利贷。 尽管中世纪基督教世界禁止高利贷，其他宗教也长期禁止高利贷，但仍有许多现代天主教徒和新教徒更倾向于现代解释而不是托马斯的理解。我只是不是他们中的一员。>实际上要求的货币单位比整个系统中实际存在的货币单位还要多。取决于你如何定义“整个系统”。 如果我借了 100 美元并赚了 110 美元，后者并不是凭空出现的。 贷方也可以将这 100 美元变成 110 美元。为什么他们不应该获得机会成本的补偿呢？如果我理解正确的话，可以像合伙企业一样进行投资，即您从 100 美元和您的合伙人的努力中共同赚取 110 美元。 但你必须像你的伴侣一样面临失败的负面影响。如果一个人从两个具体个体的角度来思考，那么机会成本点就足够公平了。 我提供的例子是在更抽象的层面上，两个人代表封闭经济体的债权人和债务人。 在这个框架中，问题不在于特定借款人是否可以将资金用于生产性用途（显然他可以），而在于整个系统被要求生成比当前存在的更多的流通媒介单位。 即使创造了实际价值，货币债权仍然超过货币存量。 然后，结算需要不断扩大货币供应量，不断将现有资产转移给债权人，或者定期违约。 这种结构性压力正是我想要表达的。我怀疑更深层次的困难在于债券本身的“债券”，即利息带来的持续强迫。 一旦附加利息，债务人就有持续义务提出额外的债权，只是为了防止账目破裂。 基督教和伊斯兰教方面的传统作家通常更喜欢避免这种持续压力的安排。 纯粹的贴现（与贴现的国库券和类似工具一样）预先对时间要素进行一次定价：债权人预付较小的金额，然后收到较大的票面金额。 该费用是在开始时支付的，而不是作为必须在未来流通中满足的经常性索赔来征收。 从这个意义上说，时间价值是在没有强制系统继续产生比目前存在的更多货币单位的机制的情况下得到承认的。[编辑：]澄清了折扣语言。这对我来说似乎仍然不对。 如果我是这样一个闭环系统中的潜在债权人，并且我被告知由于这些货币供应限制我绝对不能收取利息，我要么完全停止放贷，要么我会要求一些不严格以货币计价的东西来使我所承担的风险等值得我的资本支出。但最终，如果系统足够复杂，我可能会厌倦我们已经进行的任何复杂的易货系统，然后很可能一些第三方会介入提供完全不以货币计价的东西钱，伙计，相信我，这就像一个方便的借条票据交换所，供那些从事这些非货币恩惠交易的人使用。持有或提供此类借条的人可能会采取大胆的步骤，称它们为非排他性的，就像我会为碰巧拥有我的“割过一次草坪”凭证的人修剪草坪一样，我只是碰巧把它给了第一个家伙，我不知道他之后用它做了什么...... 其他人意识到这种“非排他性”交易实际上使优惠券更有价值，你可以用它做比其他方式更多的事情...... 你知道我要说什么了。 它没有通过我的嗅探测试。以折扣价购买债券与按面值购买并连本带利偿还没有什么不同。任何声称两者之间存在重要区别的说法都是诡辩。借款人正在争取时间。 我很高兴我现在可以买一些我看重的东西，而不是等待。 这对我有用，我很乐意为此付费但是，现代金融中没有固定数量的货币。 它是由每个人或某个企业从银行获得贷款以及每次政府花钱时产生的。 当偿还贷款或缴纳税款时，它就会被销毁。无限的金钱毫无价值。 现代金融是废话，无限的货币追逐有限的能源就是全部。听起来像是对所有贷款的宗教厌恶？高利贷通常意味着毁灭性的高利率，而不是所有贷款。一般来说，除非你是穆斯林。在许多历史社会中，宗教禁止高利贷意味着收取任何形式的利息。乘坐时光机到 1515 年问马丁·路德，或者到 1260 年问托马斯·阿奎那，他们会告诉你这是有罪的。在当今时代，相当多的伊斯兰民众认为利息违反了他们的宗教规则。 因此，存在清真金融行业，例如，您可以获得“murabahah 合同”，银行购买房屋，然后以更高的价格将房屋卖给您，同时允许您按月分期付款。比这些中的任何一个都更古老：“你不得以利息借给你的兄弟：金钱的利息，食物的利息，任何借出利息的东西的利息”（Deut 23.20 JPS Tanakh）。我喜欢宗教有这样的规则律师。 它很容易抹黑宗教。 好像他们全能的上帝会被花哨的文书或法律漏洞所愚弄。规则中没有规定的一点是风险是共担的。 为了使交易符合宗教法，贷款人必须接受与借款人相同的风险，平等。所以我猜在这种情况下，如果房子被烧毁，保险只支付约定价值的 50%，那么贷款人只能收到约定还款的 50%。通常，付息贷款有担保作为担保，因此贷款人可以通过对其他资产的债权来要求借款人偿还。他们不是想愚弄上帝，而是想愚弄你，让你同意。 他们不在乎上帝怎么想，甚至可能根本不相信他，但除非他们能让你相信这个漏洞，否则他们就会遵守规则本身。当人们利用宗教来制定这样的规则时，你是否喜欢它，就好像人们可以被愚弄，认为他们/知道/上帝的想法一样？具体来说是不同的。 如果房子变得一文不值，“借款人”可以解除合同，不欠任何东西，而银行则保留房子。 银行在签订这份合同时最好考虑房子的价值，而不仅仅是“借来的”人的支付能力。这是一个有趣的想法。 这种增长是如此极端，如果标准普尔 500 指数今天下跌 50%，它将达到 2022 年最后一次出现的水平。 鉴于时间跨度如此之短，老实说，我不确定 401k 会像人们预期的那样糟糕，除非你所有的投资都集中在过去 4 年。我认为如果你的计算是“当我的 401k 达到 X 美元绝对值时我就会退休”，情况会更糟，但我认为大多数人只是在某个年龄退休，而不是把风险分散到几十年。其中一个大问题是回报风险的顺序。 如果你退休并依赖你的投资组合，但在你离开劳动力市场后市场暴跌一两年，你的投资组合总价值就会因为你在低点出售而受到打击。切线：我认为很多人忘记/低估了他们工作背后的行业需要多元化的程度。换句话说，程序员应该比平均水平多投资一点软件，房地产经纪人应该比平均水平多投资一点房地产，煤矿工人应该多投资能源和采矿业，等等。如果你有工作，你就可以经受住股票低迷的考验，如果投资稳定，你可以通过清算来度过一段失业时期。有一些，但如果两者同时遇到麻烦，那就更糟糕了。这是有道理的，也许应该说得更多。 这可能只是因为我们投资于我们所知道的东西。 如果您是房地产经纪人，您对房地产感兴趣，您会一直考虑这一点，因此您会购买自己的房地产，或者投资建筑商股票。 如果你从事科技行业，那么我不需要这么说，因为我们大多数人都是这样。 如果您在能源行业工作，我想您对投机矿工或钻探等的运输和深奥知识略知一二。> 这可能只是因为我们投资于我们所知道的东西。我主要想到的是那些会被动投资于大型广泛指数基金的人，然后假设他们在平衡行业/部门风险方面已经达到了终点。这也是为什么你不应该持有自己公司的股权超过必要的时间（例如，苹果员工不应该将其大部分净资产投入苹果股票）。我经常举的例子是安然公司，以及对那些退休基金在自己“太棒了而不能倒”的雇主手中的员工的影响。哦，当然，由于欺诈的角度，情况变得更糟，但即使这只是一种更诚实的狂热，这种安排也是鲁莽和糟糕的。这就是为什么你会保留 3-5 年的现金支出（或者如果你想花哨的话可以使用债券阶梯）。大多数人甚至没有3-5年的总储蓄“花钱”（无论是什么）；如果你将其保留为现金，那么当市场翻倍时，你每年可以获得 2-3% 的收益。上周我在家乡的一个乡村小社区的银行。 出纳员接了电话，我无意中听到她说“你的支票账户里有 1.53 美元，储蓄有 150 美元”。大概这就是他们的总净资产。 我认为这种情况比此类论坛上的人们意识到的要普遍得多。 大多数人都会工作，直到他们真的无法工作为止，然后靠社会保障勉强度日，直到去世。 我认为保持这种观点很重要。要么就是这样，要么他们背负着巨额债务。 （有时两者兼而有之！） 在现代美国，普通人都在挣扎。因为普通人也会做出一连串糟糕的财务决策。公立学校艺术学位的学生贷款余额为 10 万美元，放弃健康保险，但希望获得 20 万美元的免费护理，或者以 12.5% 的贷款购买 8 万美元的 F150，并滚动负资产。 美国的家庭可支配收入中位数在世界上排名第二，仅次于卢森堡，比挪威高出 10% 以上。 对于税后和转移支付后的人均每日收入中位数，我们仅落后于挪威、瑞士、卢森堡、卡塔尔和阿联酋。 除了石油国家、微型国家和瑞士之外，没有哪个国家拥有比其他国家更富裕的“普通”人口。美国当然没有其他一些国家的安全网，但这些并不是你被社会扔进的洞：它们是你在 99% 的情况下故意跳进去的坑。你在这里的错误，或者如果我们慷慨的话，缺失的部分就是人们花钱的地方。 这里的医疗保健费用要高得多。 高中毕业后，学校教育并不是免费的。 日托不是免费的。 天哪，在法国或英国，即使是财产税也“不算什么”，那里的税收和市政税分别只占美国人支付的财产税的一小部分——当然，财产税是为“免费公立学校”支付的。现在，让我们谈谈保险，这也高得多。 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。 两者都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。你可能会反驳说，这些欧洲国家（以及类似的国家，如德国、比荷卢经济联盟等）的能源成本要高得多，购买力可能更高，但工资却低得多。也就是说，人们乱花钱的比喻需要考虑世界各地许多人永远不需要考虑的事情的惊人成本。 法国低劣的工资被如此之多的生活必需品所掩盖，而这些生活必需品无需高成本或在某些情况下不需要任何成本。>你在这里的错误，或者如果我们慷慨的话，缺失的部分就是人们花钱的地方。 这里的医疗保健费用要高得多。 高中毕业后，学校教育并不是免费的。 日托不是免费的。 天哪，在法国或英国，甚至连财产税也根本“不算什么”，那里的税收和市政税分别只占美国人缴纳的财产税的一小部分——当然，财产税是为“免费公立学校”支付费用的。美国工人的平均工资大约相当于每美元劳动力成本的 70 美分；即使不考虑增值税，比利时、德国、法国、奥地利或意大利的工人的工资也接近 47 美分。 欧洲没有人“免费”获得你提到的这些服务——你只是让其他人通过对他们的劳动力征税来支付这些服务。 当谈到支付公共服务的财产税时，您几乎将这些点联系起来，但错过了欧洲评估所得税。>现在，让我们谈谈保险，这也高得多。 在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州等州，房屋保险是非常高的，在新泽西州和纽约州等一些州，汽车保险是非常高的。 两者的涨幅都远远高于通货膨胀（就像我第一段中的项目）。这是一系列问题。
